Name: Amunition +1
Text: You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this piece of magic ammunition. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical. This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of ten or twenty pieces. Ten pieces of this ammunition are equivalent in value to a potion of the same rarity (200 GP).

Name: Armor +1
Text:
You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.

Base items.

Name: Rod of the Pact Keeper +1
Text:
While holding this rod, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your Warlock spells.

In addition, you can regain one spell slot as a Magic action while holding the rod. You can't use this property again until you finish a Long Rest.

Name: Shield +1(*)
Text:
* This Wondrous Item has the same name and source as the item +1 Shield.

While holding this Shield, you have a +1 bonus to Armor Class, in addition to the Shield's normal bonus to AC.

Name: Wand of the War Mage +1
Text: While holding this wand, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls. In addition, you ignore Half Cover when making a spell attack roll.

Name: Weapon +1
Text: You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

Name: Wraps of Unarmed Power +1
Text: While wearing these wraps, you have a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with your Unarmed Strikes. Those strikes deal your choice of Force damage or their normal damage type.

Name: Ammunition +2
Text:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this piece of magic ammunition. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical.

This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of ten or twenty pieces. Ten pieces of this ammunition are equivalent in value to a potion of the same rarity (2000 GP).

Name: Armor +2
Text: You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.

Name: Rod of the Pact Keeper +2
Text:
While holding this rod, you gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your Warlock spells.

In addition, you can regain one spell slot as a Magic action while holding the rod. You can't use this property again until you finish a Long Rest.

Name: Shield +2(*)
Text:
* This Wondrous Item has the same name and source as the item +2 Shield.

While holding this Shield, you have a +2 bonus to Armor Class, in addition to the Shield's normal bonus to AC.

Name: Wand of the War Mage +2
Text: While holding this wand, you gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls. In addition, you ignore Half Cover when making a spell attack roll.

Name: Weapon +2
Text: You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

Name: Wraps of Unarmed Power +2
Text: While wearing these wraps, you have a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with your Unarmed Strikes. Those strikes deal your choice of Force damage or their normal damage type.

Text:
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this piece of magic ammunition. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical.

This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of ten or twenty pieces. Ten pieces of this ammunition are equivalent in value to a potion of the same rarity (20000GP).

Name: Armor +3
Text: You have a +3 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.

Name: Rod of the Pact Keeper +3
Text:
While holding this rod, you gain a +3 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your Warlock spells.

In addition, you can regain one spell slot as a Magic action while holding the rod. You can't use this property again until you finish a Long Rest.

Name: Shield +3(*)
Text:
* This Wondrous Item has the same name and source as the item +3 Shield.

While holding this Shield, you have a +3 bonus to Armor Class, in addition to the Shield's normal bonus to AC.

Name: Wand of the War Mage +3
Text: While holding this wand, you gain a +3 bonus to spell attack rolls. In addition, you ignore Half Cover when making a spell attack roll.

Name: Weapon +3
Text: You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

Name: Wraps of Unarmed Power +3
Text: While wearing these wraps, you have a +3 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with your Unarmed Strikes. Those strikes deal your choice of Force damage or their normal damage type.

Name: Acid
Text: When you take the Attack action, you may substitute one of your attacks by hurling a vial of Acid. Choose a creature or object you can see within 20 feet. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, it suffers 2d6 Acid damage.

Name: Adamantine Armor
Text: This armor is strengthened with adamantine, a material renowned for its exceptional hardness. While wearing it, any Critical Hit scored against you is treated as a normal hit instead.

Name: Adamantine Weapon
Text: This weapon or piece of ammunition is forged from adamantine, one of the most resilient substances known. Whenever it strikes an object, that hit is automatically considered a Critical Hit.

Name: Airship
Damage: Speed: 8 mphCarrying Capacity: 1 ton cargo, 20 passengersCrew 10, AC 13, HP 300
Text:
Speed. A vessel traveling against a strong wind moves at half its normal speed. In completely still conditions, waterborne ships relying on sails cannot move and must be rowed instead. Keelboats and Rowboats operate on lakes and rivers. When moving downstream, add the current’s speed (typically 3 miles per hour) to the vehicle’s speed. These craft cannot be rowed effectively against a strong current, though draft animals on the shore can tow them upstream. A Rowboat weighs 100 pounds and can be carried.

Crew. Any ship larger than a Keelboat or Rowboat requires a crew of trained hirelings (see "Services") to operate. The minimum number of skilled crew members varies by ship type.

Passengers. This indicates how many Small or Medium passengers the ship can house using hammocks. A ship fitted with private quarters can carry one-fifth of that number. A hammock berth usually costs 5 SP per day, though prices differ by vessel. A small private cabin typically costs 2 GP per day.

Damage Threshold. Refer to the rules glossary.

Ship Repair. Repairs can be made while the ship is docked. Restoring 1 Hit Point requires 1 day of work and costs 20 GP in materials and labor. In well-equipped locations such as city shipyards, both the time and cost are reduced by half.

Name: Alchemical Fire
Text: When you take the Attack action, you may substitute one of your attacks by hurling a flask of Alchemical Fire. Choose a creature or object you can see within 20 feet. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, it takes 1d4 Fire damage and ignites.

Name: Alchemist's Kit
Text:
Ability: Intelligence

Utilize: Determine the nature of a substance (DC 15), or ignite a flame (DC 15)

Craft: Acid, Alchemical Fire, Component Pouch, Oil, Paper, Perfume

Each set of Artisan's Tools is specialized for creating goods or practicing a trade. Every tool type requires its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to ability checks involving it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Transmutation Jug
Text:
This sturdy ceramic container seems capable of holding a gallon of liquid and weighs 12 pounds regardless of its contents. Even when empty, it sloshes faintly when shaken.

By taking a Magic action, you can speak the name of a liquid listed in the Transmutation Jug Liquids table, causing the jug to generate that substance. You may then uncork it as a Utilize action to pour the liquid at a rate of up to 2 gallons per minute. The maximum quantity produced depends on the chosen liquid.

Once the jug begins producing a liquid, it cannot create a different one—or exceed the maximum amount—until the next dawn.

Transmutation Jug Liquids
Liquid | Max. Amount
Acid | 8 ounces
Basic Poison | 4 ounces
Beer | 4 gallons
Honey | 1 gallon
Mayonnaise | 2 gallons
Oil | 1 quart
Vinegar | 2 gallons
Water, fresh | 8 gallons
Water, salt | 12 gallons
Wine | 1 gallon

Name: Ale (mug)
Text: A simple mug of ale, commonly served in taverns and inns, offering a mild alcoholic drink enjoyed by travelers and locals alike.

Name: Alexandrite
Text:
A deep green stone that shifts subtly in tone depending on the light.

Gemstones are compact, easy to transport, and far simpler to safeguard than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Amber
Text:
A gemstone ranging from pale golden hues to a deep, warm gold.

Gemstones are compact, easy to transport, and far simpler to safeguard than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Amethyst
Text:
A vivid purple crystal known for its rich coloration.

Gemstones are compact, easy to transport, and far simpler to safeguard than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Slaying Ammunition
Text:
This enchanted ammunition is crafted to destroy a specific creature type, chosen by the DM or determined randomly using the table below. When a creature of that type is damaged by the ammunition, it must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw, taking an additional 6d10 Force damage on a failed save or half that amount on a success.

Once it deals its bonus damage, the ammunition loses its magical properties.

1d100 | Creature Type
01–10 Aberrations
11–15 Beasts
16–20 Celestials
21–25 Constructs
26–35 Dragons
36–45 Elementals
46–50 Humanoids
51–60 Fey
61–70 Fiends
71–75 Giants
76–80 Monstrosities
81–85 Oozes
86–90 Plants
91–00 Undead

Base items. This variant can be applied to the following ammunition types:

Arrow (Arrow of Slaying)
Bolt (Bolt of Slaying)
Energy Cell (Energy Cell of Slaying)
Firearm Bullet (Firearm Bullet of Slaying)
Needle (Needle of Slaying)
Sling Bullet (Sling Bullet of Slaying)

Name: Amulet
Text:
To function as a Holy Symbol, an Amulet must be worn or held.

A Holy Symbol has a distinct design, often adorned or decorated, allowing divine power to flow through it. Clerics and Paladins may use a Holy Symbol as their Spellcasting Focus.

Name: Amulet of Health
Text: While wearing this amulet, your Constitution score becomes 19. If your Constitution is already 19 or higher without it, the amulet provides no additional benefit.

Name: Amulet of Veiled Presence
Text: While attuned to and wearing this amulet, Divination spells and magical scrying sensors cannot target or perceive you unless you willingly permit it.

Name: Amulet of the Planes
Text:
While wearing this amulet, you may take a Magic action to name a location you know on another plane. Then make a DC 15 Intelligence (Arcana) check. On a success, you cast Plane Shift. On a failure, you and all creatures and objects within 15 feet are transported to a random destination determined by rolling 1d100 and consulting the table below.

1d100 | Destination
01–60 Random location on the named plane
61–70 Random location on an Inner Plane (1 Air, 2 Earth, 3 Fire, 4 Water, 5 Feywild, 6 Shadowfell)
71–80 Random location on an Outer Plane (1 Arborea, 2 Arcadia, 3 Beastlands, 4 Bytopia, 5 Elysium, 6 Mechanus, 7 Mount Celestia, 8 Ysgard)
81–90 Random location on an Outer Plane (1 Abyss, 2 Acheron, 3 Carceri, 4 Gehenna, 5 Hades, 6 Limbo, 7 Nine Hells, 8 Pandemonium)
91–00 Random location on the Astral Plane

Name: Animated Shield
Text: While holding this shield, you may use a Bonus Action to animate it. The shield rises into the air and hovers in your space, granting its protection as though you were wielding it while leaving your hands free. It remains animated for 1 minute, until you use a Bonus Action to dismiss it, or until you die or become Incapacitated, at which point it drops to the ground or into your free hand.

Name: Antitoxin
Text: As a Bonus Action, you can drink a vial of Antitoxin, granting you Advantage on saving throws made to resist or end the Poisoned condition for 1 hour.

Name: Apparatus of Kwalish
Text:
At first glance, this device resembles a sealed iron barrel weighing 500 pounds. A concealed latch can be discovered with a successful DC 20 Intelligence (Investigation) check. Opening it reveals a hatch at one end, allowing up to two Medium or smaller creatures to climb inside. Ten levers line the far interior wall, each resting in a neutral position and capable of being pushed up or down. Activating certain levers causes the machine to shift into the form of a massive mechanical lobster.

The Apparatus of Kwalish is a Large object with the following statistics: AC 20; HP 200; Speed 30 ft., Swim 30 ft. (both become 0 ft. if the legs are retracted); immunity to Poison and Psychic damage.

A single pilot is required to operate the vehicle. When the hatch is sealed, the interior becomes airtight and watertight, containing enough breathable air for 10 hours, divided among all creatures inside.

The apparatus floats on water and can submerge to a depth of 900 feet. Beyond that depth, it suffers 2d6 Bludgeoning damage per minute due to pressure.

A creature inside may use a Utilize action to shift up to two levers. After being used, each lever returns to its neutral position. The levers function as described in the Apparatus of Kwalish Levers table.

Apparatus of Kwalish Levers
Lever | Up | Down
1 | Legs extend, enabling walking and swimming. | Legs retract, reducing Speed and Swim Speed to 0 and preventing speed bonuses.
2 | Front window shutter opens. | Front window shutter closes.
3 | Side window shutters open (two per side). | Side window shutters close (two per side).
4 | Two claws extend from the front. | Claws retract.
5 | Each extended claw makes a melee attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., dealing 7 (2d6) Bludgeoning damage. | Each extended claw makes a melee attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft.; on a hit, the target is Grappled (escape DC 15).
6 | Moves forward if legs are extended. | Moves backward if legs are extended.
7 | Turns 90 degrees counterclockwise if legs are extended. | Turns 90 degrees clockwise if legs are extended.
8 | Eyelike fixtures emit Bright Light for 30 ft. and Dim Light for another 30 ft. | Light turns off.
9 | Sinks up to 20 feet in liquid. | Rises up to 20 feet in liquid.
10 | Rear hatch opens and unseals. | Rear hatch closes and seals.

Name: Aquamarine
Text:
A gemstone with a pale blue-green hue.

Gemstones are compact, easy to carry, and far simpler to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Arcane Focus
Text:
An Arcane Focus is crafted in a distinct form and often adorned or carved to channel arcane power. Sorcerers, Warlocks, and Wizards may use it as a Spellcasting Focus.

Common variations include:

Crystal
Orb
Rod
Staff
Wand

Name: Armor of Acid Resistance
Text: While wearing this armor, you gain Resistance to Acid damage.

Name: Armor of Cold Resistance
Text: While wearing this armor, you gain Resistance to Cold damage.

Name: Armor of Fire Resistance
Text: While wearing this armor, you gain Resistance to Fire damage.

Name: Armor of Force Resistance
Text: While wearing this armor, you gain Resistance to Force damage.

Name: Armor of Gleaming
Text: This armor remains spotless at all times, never accumulating dirt or grime.

Name: Armor of Invulnerability
Text:
While wearing this armor, you have Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

Metal Shell. You may take a Magic action to gain Immunity to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage for 10 minutes or until you remove the armor. Once used, this feature can't be activated again until the next dawn.

The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

If the wearer’s Strength score is below 15, their Speed is reduced by 10 feet.

Name: Armor of Lightning Resistance
Text: While wearing this armor, you gain Resistance to Lightning damage.

Name: Armor of Necrotic Resistance
Text: While wearing this armor, you gain Resistance to Necrotic damage.

Name: Armor of Poison Resistance
Text: While wearing this armor, you gain Resistance to Poison damage.

Name: Armor of Psychic Resistance
Text: While wearing this armor, you gain Resistance to Psychic damage.

Name: Armor of Radiant Resistance
Text: While wearing this armor, you gain Resistance to Radiant damage.

Name: Armor of Thunder Resistance
Text: While wearing this armor, you gain Resistance to Thunder damage.

Name: Armor of Vulnerability (Bludgeoning)
Text:
While wearing this armor, you gain Resistance to Bludgeoning damage.

Curse. This armor carries a hidden curse, revealed only through the Identify spell or upon attunement. Once attuned, the curse remains until a Remove Curse spell or similar magic is used; removing the armor does not end the curse. While cursed, you have Vulnerability to Piercing and Slashing damage.

Name: Armor of Vulnerability (Piercing)
Text:
While wearing this armor, you gain Resistance to Piercing damage.

Curse. This armor is cursed, a fact revealed only by the Identify spell or upon attunement. Attuning to it binds the curse to you until a Remove Curse spell or similar magic is used; taking off the armor does not break the curse. While cursed, you have Vulnerability to Bludgeoning and Slashing damage.

Name: Armor of Vulnerability (Slashing)
Text:
While wearing this armor, you gain Resistance to Slashing damage.

Curse. This armor is cursed, which becomes known only through the Identify spell or when you attune to it. Once attuned, the curse persists until removed by a Remove Curse spell or similar magic; removing the armor does not end the curse. While cursed, you have Vulnerability to Bludgeoning and Piercing damage.

Name: Arrow
Text:
Arrows are used with weapons that have the Ammunition property to make ranged attacks. Each attack consumes one piece of ammunition. Drawing ammunition from a quiver, case, or similar container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). After combat, you can recover half of your spent ammunition by searching the area for 1 minute.

Arrows are usually carried in a Quiver, purchased separately.

Name: Arrow-Catching Shield
Text:
While wielding this shield, you gain a +2 bonus to Armor Class against ranged attack rolls, in addition to the shield’s normal AC bonus.

When a ranged attack roll is made against a creature within 5 feet of you, you may use your Reaction to redirect the attack so that you become the target instead.

Name: Arrows (20)
Text:
Arrows are used with weapons that have the Ammunition property to make ranged attacks. Each attack consumes one arrow. Drawing ammunition from a quiver, case, or similar container is part of the attack (a free hand is required to load a one-handed weapon). After combat, you may recover half of the arrows you expended by searching the area for 1 minute.

Arrows are usually carried in a Quiver, purchased separately.

Name: Artisan's Tools
Text:
Artisan's Tools are specialized sets designed for crafting goods or practicing a trade. Each tool type requires its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with a tool, you add your Proficiency Bonus to ability checks that use it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Available tool sets include:

Alchemist's Supplies
Brewer's Supplies
Calligrapher's Supplies
Carpenter's Tools
Cartographer's Tools
Cobbler's Tools
Cook's Utensils
Glassblower's Tools
Jeweler's Tools
Leatherworker's Tools
Mason's Tools
Painter's Supplies
Potter's Tools
Smith's Tools
Tinker's Tools
Weaver's Tools
Woodcarver's Tools

Name: Assassin's Blood
Text: A creature exposed to Assassin's Blood must make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 6 (1d12) Poison damage and becomes Poisoned for 24 hours. On a successful save, it takes only half the damage and does not become Poisoned.

Name: Axe of the Dwarvish Lords
Text:
Long ago, a young dwarven prince sought to forge a weapon that would unite his people. He journeyed deeper beneath the mountains than any dwarf before him, reaching the fiery core of a vast volcano. With the guidance of Moradin, the god of creation, he crafted four legendary tools: the Starmetal Pick, the Earthheart Forge, the Anvil of Songs, and the Shaping Hammer. Using these, he forged the Axe of the Dwarvish Lords.

With this Artifact in hand, the prince brought harmony to the clans, ending feuds and settling old disputes. United, the dwarves repelled their foes and ushered in an age of prosperity. The prince became known as the First King, and the axe became the royal symbol passed from heir to heir.

In a later age of betrayal and corruption, the axe vanished during a brutal civil war driven by greed for its power. Centuries later, dwarves still search for it, and many adventurers have spent their lives chasing rumors and exploring ancient vaults.

Magic Weapon. The Axe of the Dwarvish Lords is a magic weapon that grants a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it.

When you hit a creature with the axe and roll a 20 on the attack roll, the axe deals an additional 20 Slashing damage.

The axe has the Thrown property with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet. When you hit with a ranged attack using it, the attack deals an extra 1d8 Force damage, or 2d8 Force damage if the target is a Giant. After the attack hits or misses, the axe immediately returns to your hand.

Blessings of Moradin. While attuned to the axe, you gain the following benefits:

Darkvision. You gain Darkvision out to 60 feet. If you already have Darkvision, its range increases by 60 feet.

Fortitude of Stone. Your Constitution increases by 2, to a maximum of 20.

Gifts of the Creator. You gain proficiency with Brewer's Supplies, Mason's Tools, and Smith's Tools.

One with the Forge. You have Immunity to Poison damage and Resistance to Fire damage.

Sunder. When you hit an object with the axe, it takes the maximum possible damage.

Conjure Earth Elemental. While holding the axe, you can take a Magic action to summon an Earth Elemental. It appears in an unoccupied space within 30 feet, understands your languages, follows your commands, and acts immediately after you in initiative. It remains for 24 hours, until it dies, or until you dismiss it as a Bonus Action. You can't use this property again until the next dawn.

Random Properties. The axe has the following random properties:

2 minor beneficial properties
1 major beneficial property
2 minor detrimental properties

Travel the Depths. You can take a Magic action to touch the axe to a fixed piece of dwarven stonework and cast Teleport from it. If the destination is underground, there is no chance of mishap or arriving incorrectly. This property can't be used again for 3 days.

Destroying the Axe. The only way to destroy the axe is to melt it in the Earthheart Forge where it was created. It must remain in the forge's flames for 50 years before it is finally consumed.

Thrown. If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw it to make a ranged attack, and you can draw it as part of that attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, use the same ability modifier for attack and damage rolls that you would use for a melee attack.

Versatile. A Versatile weapon can be used with one or two hands. The damage value in parentheses is used when attacking with two hands.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can force it to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus the ability modifier used for the attack roll and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature becomes Prone.

Name: Azurite
Text:
A deep blue stone with mottled patterns.

Gemstones are compact, easy to transport, and far simpler to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Dancing Broom
Text:
These enchanted brooms, crafted by the archfey Baba Yaga, each have a unique appearance. While holding the broom, you can take a Magic action to transform it into an Animated Broom under your control. It moves to the nearest unoccupied space next to you and acts immediately after you in initiative. It remains animated until you use a Bonus Action and a command word to return it to its inert form.

If the broom is within 30 feet of you and you aren't Incapacitated, you can mentally direct its actions (no action required). You choose what it does on its next turn or give it a general order, such as attacking foes or guarding an area.

If the broom is reduced to 0 Hit Points, it breaks apart and is destroyed. If it returns to its inanimate state before reaching 0 Hit Points, it regains all lost Hit Points.

Name: Backpack
Text: A sturdy pack capable of holding up to 30 pounds of gear within 1 cubic foot. It can also function as a saddlebag when needed.

Name: Bag of Beans
Text:
This sturdy cloth sack contains 3d4 dried beans when discovered. It always weighs half a pound, no matter how many beans remain, and becomes a mundane bag once all beans are gone.

If you dump one or more beans out at once, they detonate in a 10-foot-radius Sphere centered on them. All dumped beans are destroyed, and each creature in the area—including you—must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking 5d4 Force damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

If you take a single bean, plant it in soil or sand, and water it, the bean vanishes and produces an effect 1 minute later at the spot where it was planted. The DM may choose the effect or roll on the table below.

1d100 | Effect
01 | 5d4 toadstools sprout. If a creature eats one, roll any die. On an odd roll, the creature must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or take 5d6 Poison damage and become Poisoned for 1 hour. On an even roll, the creature gains 5d6 Temporary Hit Points for 1 hour.
02–10 | A geyser erupts, spraying water, beer, mayonnaise, tea, vinegar, wine, or oil (DM’s choice) 30 feet upward for 1d4 minutes.
11–20 | A Treant grows. Roll any die: odd = Chaotic Evil, even = Chaotic Good.
21–30 | A living but immobile stone statue of you rises and loudly threatens you. If you leave, it warns others that you are a terrible villain and urges them to hunt you. It knows your location as long as you remain on the same plane. It becomes inert after 24 hours.
31–40 | A campfire with green flames appears and burns for 24 hours or until extinguished.
41–50 | Three Shrieker Fungi sprout.
51–60 | 1d4 + 4 bright-pink toads emerge. When touched, a toad transforms into a Large or smaller monster of the DM’s choice, acting according to its nature and alignment. It remains for 1 minute before vanishing in pink smoke.
61–70 | A ravenous Bulette bursts from the ground and attacks.
71–80 | A fruit tree grows, bearing 1d10 + 20 fruit. 1d8 of the fruit function as random potions. The tree disappears after 1 hour, but harvested fruit remain magical for 30 days.
81–90 | A nest of 1d4 + 3 rainbow eggs appears. A creature that eats an egg must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw. On a success, its lowest ability score increases by 1 (randomly chosen among tied scores). On a failure, it takes 10d6 Force damage from an internal explosion.
91–95 | A pyramid with a 60-foot-square base erupts upward. Inside is a burial chamber containing a Mummy, a Mummy Lord, or another Undead chosen by the DM. Its sarcophagus holds treasure.
96–00 | A giant beanstalk grows to a height determined by the DM. Its top leads to a destination of the DM’s choosing, such as a scenic overlook, a cloud giant’s castle, or another plane.

Name: Bag of Devouring
Text:
This bag looks identical to a Bag of Holding, but it is actually the feeding maw of a massive extradimensional creature. Turning the bag inside out seals the opening.

The creature connected to the bag senses anything placed inside. Organic material placed fully inside is immediately consumed. When part of a living creature enters the bag—such as when reaching inside—there is a 50 percent chance the creature is dragged in. A creature inside may use an action to attempt escape with a DC 15 Strength (Athletics) check. Another creature may try to pull them out with a DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check, risking being pulled in themselves. Any creature that begins its turn inside is devoured and destroyed.

The bag can store nonliving objects up to 1 cubic foot. However, once per day, the bag swallows all stored items and ejects them onto a random plane of existence at a time determined by the DM.

If the bag is pierced or torn, it is destroyed, and everything inside is transported to a random location on the Astral Plane.

Name: Bag of Holding
Text:
This bag’s interior is far larger than its exterior, measuring roughly 2 feet square and 4 feet deep. It can hold up to 500 pounds, not exceeding 64 cubic feet. The bag always weighs 5 pounds. Retrieving an item requires a Utilize action.

If overloaded, pierced, or torn, the bag is destroyed and its contents are scattered across the Astral Plane. Turning the bag inside out spills its contents harmlessly, but the bag must be righted before use. The bag contains enough air for 10 minutes of breathing, divided among all creatures inside.

Placing a Bag of Holding inside an extradimensional space created by a Handy Haversack, Portable Hole, or similar item destroys both items and opens a one-way gate to the Astral Plane. The gate appears where the items were combined, pulling in any creature within a 10-foot-radius Sphere and depositing them at a random Astral location before closing.

Name: Bag of Tricks, Gray
Text:
This gray cloth bag appears empty, but reaching inside reveals a small fuzzy object.

You may take a Magic action to pull out the object and throw it up to 20 feet. When it lands, it transforms into a creature determined by rolling on the table below. Use the creature’s stat block from the Monster Manual. The creature disappears at the next dawn or when reduced to 0 Hit Points.

The creature is Friendly toward you and your allies and acts immediately after you in initiative. You may use a Bonus Action to direct its movement and actions on its next turn. Without commands, it behaves according to its instincts.

After three creatures have been produced, the bag cannot be used again until the next dawn.

1d8 | Creature
1 | Weasel
2 | Giant Rat
3 | Badger
4 | Boar
5 | Panther
6 | Giant Badger
7 | Dire Wolf
8 | Giant Elk

Name: Bag of Tricks, Rust
Text:
This rust-colored cloth bag looks empty, yet reaching inside reveals a small fuzzy object.

You may take a Magic action to pull the fuzzy object from the bag and throw it up to 20 feet. When it lands, it transforms into a creature determined by rolling on the table below. Use the creature's stat block from the Monster Manual. The creature disappears at the next dawn or when reduced to 0 Hit Points.

The creature is Friendly toward you and your allies and acts immediately after you in initiative. You may use a Bonus Action to direct its movement and actions on its next turn, such as ordering it to attack. Without commands, it behaves according to its instincts.

After three fuzzy objects have been drawn from the bag, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

1d8 | Creature
1 Rat
2 Owl
3 Mastiff
4 Goat
5 Giant Goat
6 Giant Boar
7 Lion
8 Brown Bear

Name: Bag of Tricks, Tan
Text:
This tan cloth bag appears empty, but reaching inside reveals a small fuzzy object.

You may take a Magic action to pull the fuzzy object from the bag and throw it up to 20 feet. When it lands, it becomes a creature determined by rolling on the table below. Refer to the Monster Manual for its stat block. The creature vanishes at the next dawn or when it reaches 0 Hit Points.

The creature is Friendly toward you and your allies and acts immediately after you in initiative. You may use a Bonus Action to command its movement and actions on its next turn. Without instructions, it behaves naturally.

After three fuzzy objects have been drawn, the bag cannot be used again until the next dawn.

1d8 | Creature
1 Jackal
2 Ape
3 Baboon
4 Axe Beak
5 Black Bear
6 Giant Weasel
7 Giant Hyena
8 Tiger

Name: Bagpipes
Text:
Ability: Charisma

Utilize: Perform a known melody (DC 10), or improvise a tune (DC 15)

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check that uses it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Ball Bearings
Text: As a Utilize action, you can scatter Ball Bearings from their pouch. They spread across a flat 10-foot-square area within 10 feet of you. A creature entering the area for the first time on a turn must succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity saving throw or fall Prone. Gathering the Ball Bearings afterward takes 10 minutes.

Name: Banded Agate
Text:
A gemstone marked with natural stripes in shades of brown, blue, white, or red.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and far easier to safeguard than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Barrel
Text: A Barrel can store up to 40 gallons of liquid or as much as 4 cubic feet of dry goods.

Name: Basic Poison
Text: As a Bonus Action, you can apply a vial of Basic Poison to one weapon or up to three pieces of ammunition. A creature that takes Piercing or Slashing damage from the coated weapon or ammunition suffers an additional 1d4 Poison damage. The poison remains effective for 1 minute or until it deals its damage, whichever occurs first.

Name: Basket
Text: A Basket can carry up to 40 pounds of items within a 2-cubic-foot capacity.

Name: Battleaxe
Text:
Versatile. A Versatile weapon can be wielded with one or two hands. The damage value shown in parentheses applies when using two hands to make a melee attack.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, you may force it to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus the ability modifier used for the attack roll and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature falls Prone.

Name: Bead of Force
Text:
This small black bead is 3/4 of an inch across and weighs about an ounce. Typically, 1d4 + 4 Beads of Force are found together.

You may take a Magic action to throw the bead up to 60 feet. Upon impact, it detonates in a 10-foot-radius Sphere and is destroyed. Each creature in the area must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take 5d4 Force damage. A transparent sphere of force then forms around the area for 1 minute. Any creature that failed the save and is entirely inside the area becomes trapped. Creatures that succeeded or are only partially inside are pushed outward until they are no longer within the sphere. Only breathable air can pass through the barrier; no attacks or effects can.

A trapped creature may take a Utilize action to push against the sphere’s wall, moving it up to half its Speed. The sphere can be carried, and its magic causes it to weigh only 1 pound regardless of the weight of creatures inside.

Name: Bead of Nourishment
Text: This tasteless, jelly-like bead dissolves on your tongue and provides the equivalent nourishment of one full day of Rations.

Name: Bead of Refreshment
Text: This flavorless gelatinous bead dissolves when placed in liquid, purifying up to a pint of it into clean, cool drinking water. It has no effect on magical liquids or harmful substances such as poison.

Name: Bedroll
Text: A Bedroll provides sleeping space for one Small or Medium creature. While resting inside it, you automatically succeed on saving throws against extreme cold (see the Dungeon Master's Guide).

Name: Bejeweled gold bracelet
Text: Art objects include items such as solid-gold idols, gemstone-studded jewelry, portraits of ancient rulers, and ornate decorated vessels.

Name: Bejeweled ivory drinking horn with gold filigree
Text: Art objects encompass treasures like carved golden idols, jewelry set with precious stones, royal paintings, and intricately adorned ceremonial items.

Name: Bell
Text: When rung as a Utilize action, a Bell emits a clear sound audible up to 60 feet away.

Name: Belt of Cloud Giant Strength
Text: While wearing this belt, your Strength score becomes 27. If your Strength is already 27 or higher without the belt, it provides no benefit.

Name: Belt of Dwarvenkind
Text:
While wearing this belt, you gain the following benefits:

Dwarvish. You can speak, read, and write Dwarvish.

Friend of Dwarvenkind. You have Advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks made when interacting with dwarves and duergar.

Toughness. Your Constitution increases by 2, to a maximum of 20.

Additionally, while attuned to the belt, there is a 50 percent chance at each dawn that you grow a full beard if you are capable of growing one, or that your existing beard becomes thicker.

If you are not a dwarf or duergar, you gain these extra benefits:

Darkvision. You gain Darkvision out to 60 feet.

Resilience. You have Resistance to Poison damage and Advantage on saving throws made to resist or end the Poisoned condition.

Name: Belt of Fire Giant Strength
Text: While wearing this belt, your Strength score becomes 25. If your Strength is already 25 or higher without the belt, it provides no benefit.

Name: Belt of Frost Giant Strength
Text: While wearing this belt, your Strength score becomes 23. If your Strength is already 23 or higher without the belt, it provides no benefit.

Name: Belt of Hill Giant Strength
Text: While wearing this belt, your Strength score becomes 21. If your Strength is already 21 or higher without the belt, it provides no benefit.

Name: Belt of Stone Giant Strength
Text: While wearing this belt, your Strength score becomes 23. If your Strength is already 23 or higher without the belt, it provides no benefit.

Name: Belt of Storm Giant Strength
Text: While wearing this belt, your Strength score becomes 29. If your Strength is already 29 or higher without the belt, it provides no benefit.

Name: Berserker Axe
Text:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Additionally, while attuned to it, your Hit Point maximum increases by 1 for every level you have.

Curse. This weapon carries a curse that takes hold when you attune to it. While cursed, you refuse to part with the axe and keep it close at all times. You also have Disadvantage on attack rolls made with any weapon other than this one.

Whenever another creature deals damage to you while the axe is in your possession, you must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or enter a berserk frenzy. This state ends only when you begin your turn with no creatures within 60 feet that you can see or hear.

While berserk, you treat the nearest creature you can see or hear as your foe. If multiple creatures are equally close, choose one at random. On each of your turns, you must move toward that creature and use the Attack action against it. If you cannot reach the creature to attack, your turn ends after you expend all available movement. If the creature dies or is no longer visible or audible, the next closest creature becomes your new target.

Name: Black Dragon Scale Mail
Text:
Dragon Scale Mail is crafted from the scales of a specific type of dragon. Some dragons gift their shed scales, while others are harvested from slain dragons. Either way, such armor is highly prized.

While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class, have Advantage on saving throws against Dragon breath weapons, and gain Resistance to Acid damage.

You may also take a Magic action to sense the distance and direction to the nearest black dragon within 30 miles. This ability can't be used again until the next dawn.

The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Black Opal
Text:
A gemstone of deep green coloration with black marbling and shimmering golden flecks.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and far easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Black Pearl
Text:
A perfectly smooth gemstone of pure black hue.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and far easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Black Sapphire
Text:
A gleaming black gemstone with shimmering highlights.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and far easier to safeguard than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Black velvet mask stitched with silver thread
Text: Art objects encompass treasures such as golden idols, gemstone-set jewelry, portraits of ancient rulers, and ornate decorated items.

Name: Blackrazor
Text:
Hidden within the depths of White Plume Mountain, Blackrazor gleams like a fragment of star-filled night. Its black scabbard is adorned with polished obsidian.

You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit an Undead with it, you take 1d10 Necrotic damage and the target regains 1d10 Hit Points. If this Necrotic damage reduces you to 0 Hit Points, Blackrazor consumes your soul (see "Devour Soul").

While holding the sword, you have Immunity to the Charmed and Frightened conditions, and you gain Blindsight out to 30 feet.

Devour Soul. When you use Blackrazor to reduce a creature to 0 Hit Points, the sword slays the creature and devours its soul unless it is a Construct or Undead. A creature whose soul is devoured can be restored only by a Wish spell.

When Blackrazor devours a soul that is not yours, you gain Temporary Hit Points equal to that creature’s Hit Point maximum.

Haste. Blackrazor can cast Haste on you. After doing so, it cannot cast the spell again until the next dawn. The sword chooses when to cast the spell, which activates at the start of your turn. The spell lasts 1 minute (no Concentration required) or until Blackrazor ends it at the end of one of your turns.

Sentience. Blackrazor is a sentient Chaotic Neutral weapon with Intelligence 17, Wisdom 10, and Charisma 19. It has hearing and Darkvision out to 120 feet.

The sword speaks Common and communicates telepathically with its wielder. Its voice is deep and resonant. While attuned, Blackrazor also understands every language you know.

Personality. Blackrazor speaks with commanding authority and exists to consume souls—any souls, including its wielder’s. It believes all existence originates from negative energy and will eventually return to it, and it seeks to hasten that fate.

Despite its nihilistic nature, Blackrazor feels a strange bond with Wave and Whelm, two other weapons sealed beneath White Plume Mountain. It desires their reunion, even though it clashes with Whelm and finds Wave insufferable.

Blackrazor must regularly feed. If it goes 3 days without consuming a soul, it initiates a conflict with its wielder at the next sunset.

Destroying Blackrazor. The sword can be destroyed only by crushing it within the great gears of Mechanus. Primus also knows a sequence of tones that causes Blackrazor to shatter.

Heavy. You have Disadvantage on attack rolls with a Heavy weapon if it is a melee weapon and your Strength is below 13, or if it is a ranged weapon and your Dexterity is below 13.

Two-Handed. A Two-Handed weapon requires both hands to make an attack.

Mastery: Graze. If your attack roll misses a creature, you may still deal damage equal to the ability modifier used for the attack roll. This damage matches the weapon’s type and can only be increased by raising that ability modifier.

Name: Blanket
Text: While wrapped in a blanket, you have Advantage on saving throws against extreme cold (see the Dungeon Master's Guide).

Name: Block and Tackle
Text: A Block and Tackle enables you to lift or pull up to four times the weight you can normally manage.

Name: Bloodstone
Text:
A gemstone of dark gray coloration streaked with red specks.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and far easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Blue Dragon Scale Mail
Text:
Dragon Scale Mail is crafted from the scales of a specific type of dragon. Some dragons gift their shed scales, while others are harvested from slain dragons. Either way, such armor is highly prized.

While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class, have Advantage on saving throws against Dragon breath weapons, and gain Resistance to Lightning damage.

You may also take a Magic action to sense the distance and direction to the nearest blue dragon within 30 miles. This ability can't be used again until the next dawn.

The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Blue Quartz
Text:
A gemstone of pale blue coloration.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and far easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Blue Sapphire
Text:
A gemstone of rich medium-blue hue.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and far easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Blue Spinel
Text:
A deep blue gemstone with a vivid, saturated color.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and far easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Bolt
Text:
Crossbow bolts are used with weapons that have the Ammunition property to make ranged attacks. Each attack consumes one bolt. Drawing ammunition from a quiver, case, or similar container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). After combat, you can recover half of the bolts you expended by searching the area for 1 minute.

Bolts are usually carried in a Crossbow Bolt Case, sold separately.

Name: Bolts (20)
Text:
Crossbow bolts are used with weapons that have the Ammunition property to make ranged attacks. Each attack consumes one bolt. Drawing ammunition from a quiver, case, or similar container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). After combat, you can recover half of the bolts you expended by searching the area for 1 minute.

Bolts are usually carried in a Crossbow Bolt Case, sold separately.

Name: Bomb
Text: As an action, you can ignite a Bomb and throw it to a point within 60 feet, where it detonates. Each creature in a 5-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point must make a DC 12 Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d6 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one.

Name: Book
Text: A Book may contain fiction or nonfiction. When you consult an accurate nonfiction Book on its subject, you gain a +5 bonus to Intelligence (Arcana, History, Nature, or Religion) checks related to that topic.

Name: Book of Exalted Deeds
Text:
The Book of Exalted Deeds is regarded as the ultimate work on goodness within the multiverse, referenced by many faiths. Rather than serving a single religion, its authors filled its pages with their own visions of pure virtue and guidance for combating evil.

The book rarely remains in one place. Once read, it disappears to another corner of existence where its teachings may inspire those in need. Attempts to copy it always fail to reproduce its magic or convey its blessings to the worthy.

A heavy clasp shaped like angelic wings seals the book. Only a creature attuned to it can open the clasp. After opening it, the attuned reader must spend 80 hours studying its contents to gain its benefits.

Other creatures may read the words but gain no deeper insight. A Fiend, an Undead, or a servant of a Lower Planes deity that attempts to read it takes 24d6 Radiant damage, ignoring Resistance and Immunity. This damage cannot be reduced or avoided. A creature reduced to 0 Hit Points by this effect is destroyed in a flash of light, leaving its belongings behind. The book then vanishes, ending the creature’s Attunement.

The book’s blessings last only while you continue to act with goodness. If you fail to perform at least one kind or generous act within 10 days, or if you willingly commit an evil deed, you lose all benefits.

Celestial Calm. While attuned, you have Immunity to the Charmed and Frightened conditions and Resistance to Psychic damage. These benefits become permanent after completing the required study.

Divine Wisdom. After completing the required study, your Wisdom increases by 2, to a maximum of 24. You cannot gain this benefit more than once.

Enlightened Magic. After completing the required study, any spell slot you expend counts as one level higher.

Halo. After completing the required study, you manifest a protective halo. It sheds Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for another 10 feet. You can dismiss or restore it as a Bonus Action. While active, you gain Advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks, and Fiends and Undead within the Bright Light have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you.

Random Properties. The book has the following random properties:

2 minor beneficial properties
2 major beneficial properties

Destroying the Book. The Book of Exalted Deeds cannot be destroyed. However, submerging it in the River Styx erases all writing and imagery, rendering it powerless for 1d100 years.

Name: Book of Vile Darkness
Text:
This abhorrent tome is a repository of wickedness. Its pages contain horrors so profound that even glimpsing them invites ruin.

Most believe the lich-god Vekna authored the Book of Vile Darkness, filling it with every vile idea, corrupt thought, and dark magic he encountered or invented.

Other evil practitioners have added their own writings over time, often stitching pages into the book or scrawling notes over existing text. Some pages are missing, torn, or so heavily stained with ink, blood, or scratches that the original content is unreadable.

Nature recoils from the book. Plants wither near it, animals refuse to approach, and the book slowly corrodes anything it touches—even stone crumbles to dust beneath it.

When a creature that is not a Fiend or Undead attunes to the book, it must make a DC 17 Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the creature is transformed into a Larva under the DM’s control. Only a Wish spell can reverse this transformation.

An attuned creature must spend 80 hours studying the book to gain its Adjusted Ability Scores, Tireless Form, Spells, Vile Lore, and Vile Speech features.

The book remains with you only while you continue to commit evil. If you fail to perform at least one evil act within 10 days, or if you willingly perform a good act, the book disappears, ending your Attunement and removing all benefits. If you die while attuned, a powerful evil entity claims your soul, preventing resurrection by any means.

Adjusted Ability Scores. One ability score of your choice increases by 2 (to a maximum of 24), and another decreases by 2 (to a minimum of 3). This effect cannot occur again.

Tireless Form. While carrying the book, you have Immunity to the Exhaustion condition.

Random Properties. The book has the following random properties:

3 minor beneficial properties
1 major beneficial property
3 minor detrimental properties
2 major detrimental properties

Spells. While holding the book, you can cast the following spells from it (save DC 18): Animate Dead, Circle of Death, Dominate Monster, Finger of Death. Once you cast a spell from the book, you cannot cast that spell again until the next dawn.

Vile Lore. When making an Intelligence check to recall information about evil—such as demon lore—you have Advantage.

At the DM’s discretion, the book may reveal forbidden secrets, such as true names of powerful Fiends, rituals to become a lich or death knight, or lost spells crafted by ancient evildoers.

Vile Speech. While carrying the book, you can take a Magic action to utter words from its pages in a foul, dead language. You take 1d12 Psychic damage, and each creature within 15 feet takes 3d6 Psychic damage unless it is a Fiend or Undead.

Destroying the Book. Pages can be torn from the book, but their evil lore eventually returns when new pages are added.

If a solar tears the book in half, it is destroyed for 1d100 years before reforming elsewhere.

A creature attuned for 100 years may uncover a hidden phrase in the original text. When translated into Celestial and spoken aloud, it destroys both the speaker and the book in a burst of radiance. However, as long as evil exists, the book reforms 1d10 × 100 years later.

Name: Boots of Elvenkind
Text: While wearing these boots, your footsteps make no sound regardless of the surface. You also gain Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Boots of False Tracks
Text: While wearing these boots, you can choose for them to leave footprints resembling those of any Humanoid of your size.

Name: Boots of Levitation
Text: While wearing these boots, you can cast the Levitate spell on yourself.

Name: Boots of Speed
Text:
While wearing these boots, you can use a Bonus Action to click the heels together. Doing so doubles your Speed, and creatures making Opportunity Attacks against you have Disadvantage. Clicking your heels again ends the effect.

After you have used this property for a total of 10 minutes, the boots' magic stops functioning for you until you finish a Long Rest.

Name: Boots of Striding and Springing
Text:
While wearing these boots, your Speed becomes 30 feet unless it is already higher, and your Speed is not reduced by carrying weight beyond your carrying capacity or by wearing Armor.

Once on each of your turns, you can jump up to 30 feet by spending only 10 feet of movement.

Name: Boots of the Winterlands
Text:
These fur-lined boots fit snugly and radiate warmth. While wearing them, you gain the following benefits:

Cold Resistance. You have Resistance to Cold damage and can endure temperatures as low as 0 degrees Fahrenheit without extra protection.

Winter Strider. You ignore Difficult Terrain caused by snow or ice.

Name: Bottle stopper cork embossed with gold leaf and set with amethysts
Text: Art objects include treasures such as golden idols, gemstone-studded jewelry, royal portraits, and ornate decorative pieces.

Name: Bowl of Commanding Water Elementals
Text:
When this bowl is filled with water and you are within 5 feet of it, you can take a Magic action to summon a Water Elemental. It appears in an unoccupied space nearest to the bowl, understands your languages, follows your commands, and acts immediately after you in initiative. The elemental vanishes after 1 hour, when it dies, or when you dismiss it as a Bonus Action. The bowl can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

The bowl is roughly 1 foot across and half as deep, holding about 3 gallons.

Name: Box of turquoise animal figurines
Text: Art objects include treasures such as golden idols, gemstone-studded jewelry, royal portraits, and ornate decorative pieces.

Name: Bracers of Archery
Text: While wearing these bracers, you gain proficiency with the Longbow and Shortbow, and you receive a +2 bonus to damage rolls made with those weapons.

Name: Bracers of Defense
Text: While wearing these bracers, you gain a +2 bonus to Armor Class as long as you are not wearing armor and not using a Shield.

Name: Brass Dragon Scale Mail
Text:
Dragon Scale Mail is crafted from the scales of a specific type of dragon. Some dragons gift their shed scales, while others are harvested from slain dragons. Regardless of origin, this armor is highly prized.

While wearing it, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class, have Advantage on saving throws against Dragon breath weapons, and gain Resistance to Fire damage.

You may also take a Magic action to sense the distance and direction to the nearest brass dragon within 30 miles. This ability can't be used again until the next dawn.

The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Brass mug with jade inlay
Text: Art objects include treasures such as golden idols, gemstone-studded jewelry, royal portraits, and ornate decorative pieces.

Name: Brazier of Commanding Fire Elementals
Text: While you are within 5 feet of this brazier, you can take a Magic action to summon a Fire Elemental. It appears in an unoccupied space nearest to the brazier, understands your languages, follows your commands, and acts immediately after you in initiative. The elemental disappears after 1 hour, when it dies, or when you dismiss it as a Bonus Action. The brazier can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

Name: Bread (loaf)
Text: A simple loaf of bread, commonly eaten as part of everyday meals.

Name: Breastplate
Text: A fitted metal breastplate that protects the torso while allowing more mobility than heavier armor.

Name: Brewer's Supplies
Text:
Ability: Intelligence

Utilize: Detect poisoned drink (DC 15), or identify alcohol (DC 10)

Craft: Antitoxin

Artisan's Tools are specialized kits used for crafting and trade work. Each type requires its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check that uses it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Bronze Dragon Scale Mail
Text:
Dragon Scale Mail is crafted from the scales of a specific type of dragon. Some dragons gift their shed scales, while others are harvested from slain dragons. In either case, the armor is highly prized.

While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class, have Advantage on saving throws against Dragon breath weapons, and gain Resistance to Lightning damage.

You may also take a Magic action to sense the distance and direction to the nearest bronze dragon within 30 miles. This ability can't be used again until the next dawn.

The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Bronze crown
Text: Art objects include treasures such as golden idols, gemstone-studded jewelry, royal portraits, and ornate decorative pieces.

Name: Brooch of Shielding
Text: While wearing this brooch, you gain Resistance to Force damage and complete Immunity to damage from the Magic Missile spell.

Name: Broom of Flying
Text:
This wooden broom behaves like an ordinary broom until you straddle it and take a Magic action to make it hover beneath you, allowing you to ride it through the air. It has a Fly Speed of 50 feet. It can carry up to 400 pounds, though its Fly Speed is reduced to 30 feet if carrying more than 200 pounds. The broom stops hovering when you land or dismount.

As a Magic action, you can command the broom to travel on its own to a familiar location within 1 mile. It returns to you when you take a Magic action and speak its command word, provided it is still within 1 mile.

Name: Bucket
Text: A simple container capable of holding up to half a cubic foot of material.

Name: Bullseye Lantern
Text: This lantern burns Oil to project a 60-foot Cone of Bright Light, with Dim Light extending an additional 60 feet.

Name: Bundle of sheet music representing the lost dirges of a famous composer
Text: Art objects include treasures such as golden idols, gemstone-studded jewelry, royal portraits, and ornate decorative pieces.

Name: Burglar's Pack
Text: A Burglar's Pack contains the following items: Backpack, Ball Bearings, Bell, 10 Candles, Crowbar, Hooded Lantern, 7 flasks of Oil, 5 days of Rations, Rope, Tinderbox, and Waterskin.

Name: Burnt Othur Fumes
Text: A creature exposed to Burnt Othur Fumes must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or take 10 (3d6) Poison damage, then repeat the save at the start of each of its turns. On each failed save, it takes 3 (1d6) Poison damage. After three successful saves, the poison ends.

Name: Calligrapher's Supplies
Text:
Ability: Dexterity

Utilize: Create ornate writing that is difficult to forge (DC 15)

Craft: Ink, Spell Scroll

Artisan's Tools are specialized kits used for crafting and trade work, each requiring its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check that uses it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Caltrops
Text: As a Utilize action, you scatter Caltrops to cover a 5-foot square within 5 feet of you. A creature entering the area for the first time on a turn must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take 1 Piercing damage and have its Speed reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn. Recovering the Caltrops takes 10 minutes.

Name: Camel
Text: A hardy desert mount known for endurance and the ability to carry heavy loads over long distances.

Name: Candle
Text: A lit Candle provides Bright Light in a 5-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 5 feet for 1 hour.

Name: Candle of Invocation
Text:
Lighting this candle requires a Magic action and activates its magic. It burns for 4 hours before being consumed, though you may extinguish it early and resume later, tracking time in 1-minute increments.

While lit, the candle sheds Dim Light in a 30-foot radius. While you remain within that light, you have Advantage on all D20 Tests. Additionally, Clerics and Druids within the light can cast level 1 spells they have prepared without expending spell slots.

Alternatively, when lighting the candle for the first time, you may use it to cast Gate. Doing so destroys the candle. The portal created links to an Outer Plane chosen by the DM or determined by the following table:

1d100 | Outer Plane
01–05 Abyss
06–10 Acheron
11–17 Arborea
18–25 Arcadia
26–33 Beastlands
34–41 Bytopia
42–46 Carceri
47–54 Elysium
55–59 Gehenna
60–64 Hades
65–69 Limbo
70–77 Mechanus
78–85 Mount Celestia
86–90 Nine Hells
91–95 Pandemonium
96–00 Ysgard

Name: Candle of the Deep
Text: This candle’s flame remains lit even when submerged in water, producing the same light and warmth as an ordinary candle.

Name: Canvas (1 sq. yd.)
Text: Merchants often trade goods like this without relying on coinage.

Name: Cap of Water Breathing
Text: While wearing this cap underwater, you can take a Magic action to form a bubble of air around your head, allowing you to breathe normally. The bubble remains until the cap is removed or you leave the water.

Name: Cape of the Mountebank
Text:
This cape carries a faint scent of brimstone. While wearing it, you can use it to cast Dimension Door as a Magic action. This property refreshes at the next dawn.

When you teleport using the spell, you leave behind a cloud of smoke that lightly obscures the space you departed until the end of your next turn.

Name: Carnelian
Text:
A gemstone ranging in color from orange to reddish brown.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Carpenter's Tools
Text:
Ability: Strength

Utilize: Seal or pry open a door or container (DC 20)

Craft: Club, Greatclub, Quarterstaff, Barrel, Chest, Ladder, Pole, Portable Ram, Torch

Artisan's Tools are specialized kits used for crafting and trade work, each requiring its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check that uses it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Carpet of Flying
Text:
By taking a Magic action and speaking its command word, you cause this carpet to hover and fly. It follows your directions as long as you remain within 30 feet of it.

Four sizes of Flying Carpets exist. The DM chooses the size or determines it randomly using the table below. A carpet can carry up to twice its listed capacity, but its Fly Speed is halved if it carries more than its normal limit.

Applicable Carpet Sizes
1d100 | Size | Capacity | Fly Speed
01–20 | Carpet of Flying, 3 ft. × 5 ft. | 200 lb. | 80 ft.
21–55 | Carpet of Flying, 4 ft. × 6 ft. | 400 lb. | 60 ft.
56–80 | Carpet of Flying, 5 ft. × 7 ft. | 600 lb. | 40 ft.
81–00 | Carpet of Flying, 6 ft. × 9 ft. | 800 lb. | 30 ft.

Name: Carriage
Text: A covered four-wheeled vehicle designed to transport passengers comfortably over long distances.

Name: Carrion Crawler Mucus
Text: A creature exposed to Carrion Crawler Mucus must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or become Poisoned for 1 minute. While Poisoned in this way, the creature is also Paralyzed. It repeats the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

Name: Cart
Text: A simple two-wheeled vehicle commonly used for hauling goods or equipment.

Name: Cartographer's Tools
Text:
Ability: Wisdom

Utilize: Create a detailed map of a small region (DC 15)

Craft: Map

Artisan's Tools are specialized kits used for crafting and trade work, each requiring its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check that uses it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Carved bone statuette
Text: Art objects include treasures such as golden idols, gemstone-studded jewelry, royal portraits, and ornate decorative pieces.

Name: Carved ivory statuette
Text: Art objects include treasures such as golden idols, gemstone-studded jewelry, royal portraits, and ornate decorative pieces.

Name: Carved wooden harp with ivory inlay and zircon gems
Text: Art objects include treasures such as golden idols, gemstone-studded jewelry, royal portraits, and ornate decorative pieces.

Name: Cast-Off Armor
Text: You can remove this armor using a Magic action.

Name: Cauldron of Rebirth
Attunement: Requires Attunement By a Druid or Warlock
Text:
This Tiny iron cauldron is decorated with relief carvings of heroic figures.

You may use the cauldron as a Spellcasting Focus, and it also serves as a suitable component for the Scrying spell.

Brew Potion. After finishing a Long Rest, you can use the cauldron to create a Potion of Healing (greater). The process takes 1 minute, and the potion retains its magic for 24 hours.

Raise Dead. As a Magic action, you can enlarge the cauldron to fit a Medium creature. You can shrink it again as a Magic action, safely moving anything that no longer fits to the nearest unoccupied space.

If you place the corpse of a Humanoid inside and cover it with 200 pounds of salt (worth 10 GP) for at least 8 hours, the salt is consumed and the creature is restored to life at the next dawn as if by Raise Dead. This property recharges after 7 days.

Name: Censer of Controlling Air Elementals
Text: By gently swinging this censer, you can take a Magic action to summon an Air Elemental. It appears in the nearest unoccupied space, understands your languages, follows your commands, and acts immediately after you in initiative. The elemental vanishes after 1 hour, when it dies, or when you dismiss it as a Bonus Action. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Ceremonial gold armor with black pearls
Text: Art objects include treasures such as golden idols, gemstone-studded jewelry, royal portraits, and ornate decorative pieces.

Name: Chain
Text: As a Utilize action, you can wrap a Chain around an unwilling creature within 5 feet that is Grappled, Incapacitated, or Restrained, succeeding on a DC 13 Strength (Athletics) check. If the creature's legs are bound, it becomes Restrained until it escapes. Escaping requires a DC 18 Dexterity (Acrobatics) check as an action, while breaking the Chain requires a DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check as an action.

Name: Chain Mail
Text:
The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

If the wearer’s Strength score is below 13, their Speed is reduced by 10 feet.

Name: Chain Shirt
Text: A lighter form of chain armor that offers protection while allowing greater mobility.

Name: Chalcedony
Text:
A white gemstone.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Chariot
Text: A two-wheeled vehicle designed for speed, often pulled by one or more horses.

Name: Charlatan's Die
Text: Whenever you roll this six-sided die, you can choose the result it lands on.

Name: Cheese (wedge)
Text: A small wedge of cheese, commonly eaten as part of a simple meal.

Name: Chest
Text: A sturdy container capable of holding up to 12 cubic feet of items.

Name: Chicken
Text: Merchants often trade livestock and other goods directly without using coinage.

Name: Chime of Opening
Text: This hollow metal tube is about 1 foot long and weighs 1 pound. As a Magic action, you can strike it to cast Knock. Instead of the spell’s usual knocking sound, the chime produces a clear ringing tone audible out to 300 feet. The chime can be used 10 times; after the tenth use, it cracks and becomes useless.

Name: Chrysoberyl
Text:
A gemstone ranging from yellow-green to pale green.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Chrysoprase
Text:
A vivid green gemstone.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Cinnamon
Text: Merchants often trade spices and other goods directly without using currency.

Name: Circlet of Blasting
Text: While wearing this circlet, you can cast Scorching Ray with it (+5 to hit). The circlet regains this ability at the next dawn.

Name: Citrine
Text:
A gemstone of pale yellow-brown coloration.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Climber's Kit
Text: A Climber's Kit includes boot tips, gloves, pitons, and a harness. As a Utilize action, you can anchor yourself; while anchored, you cannot fall more than 25 feet from the point and cannot move farther than 25 feet without removing the anchor as a Bonus Action.

Name: Cloak of Arachnida
Text:
This elegant cloak is woven from black silk threaded with faint silver strands. While wearing it, you gain the following benefits:

Poison Resistance. You have Resistance to Poison damage.

Spider Climb. You gain a Climb Speed equal to your Speed and can move freely on walls and ceilings with your hands unoccupied.

Spider Walk. You cannot be restrained by webs and move through them as if they were Difficult Terrain.

Web. You can cast Web (save DC 13). The spell’s webbing covers twice the normal area. This property recharges at the next dawn.

Name: Cloak of Billowing
Text: While wearing this cloak, you can use a Bonus Action to make it dramatically billow for 1 minute.

Name: Cloak of Displacement
Text: While wearing this cloak, an illusion causes you to appear slightly offset from your true position, giving creatures Disadvantage on attack rolls against you. If you take damage, this effect ends until the start of your next turn. The effect is also suppressed while your Speed is 0.

Name: Cloak of Elvenkind
Text: While wearing this cloak, creatures have Disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks made to notice you, and you have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Cloak of Invisibility
Text: This cloak has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges at dawn. While wearing it, you can take a Magic action to pull the hood over your head and expend 1 charge to become Invisible for 1 hour. The effect ends early if you lower the hood or stop wearing it.

Name: Cloak of Many Fashions
Text: While wearing this cloak, you can use a Bonus Action to alter its style, color, and apparent quality. Its weight never changes, and although it can mimic the look of other magical cloaks, it does not gain their magical abilities.

Name: Cloak of Protection
Text: While wearing this cloak, you gain a +1 bonus to both Armor Class and saving throws.

Name: Cloak of the Bat
Text:
While wearing this cloak, you have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks. In Dim Light or Darkness, you can grasp the cloak’s edges to gain a Fly Speed of 40 feet. You lose this Fly Speed if you fail to hold the cloak’s edges or leave Dim Light or Darkness.

In Dim Light or Darkness, you can also cast Polymorph on yourself to transform into a Bat, retaining your Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Cloak of the Manta Ray
Text: While wearing this cloak, you can breathe underwater and gain a Swim Speed of 60 feet.

Name: Clockwork Amulet
Text:
This copper amulet houses tiny interlocking gears powered by magic from Mechanus. It emits faint ticking sounds.

When you make an attack roll, you may choose to forgo rolling the d20 and instead treat the roll as a 10. This property recharges at the next dawn.

Name: Cloth-of-gold vestments
Text: Art objects include treasures such as golden idols, gemstone-studded jewelry, royal portraits, and ornate decorative pieces.

Name: Clothes of Mending
Text: This fine outfit magically repairs itself from everyday wear and minor damage. Completely destroyed pieces cannot be restored by this magic.

Name: Cloves
Text: Merchants often trade spices and other goods directly without using currency.

Name: Club
Text:
Light. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light weapon, you may make one extra attack as a Bonus Action with a different Light weapon. You do not add your ability modifier to the damage of that extra attack unless it is negative.

Mastery: Slow. When you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage, you can reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. Multiple hits with Slow weapons do not stack beyond a 10-foot reduction.

Name: Cobbler's Tools
Text:
Ability: Dexterity

Utilize: Modify footwear to grant Advantage on the wearer’s next Dexterity (Acrobatics) check (DC 10)

Craft: Climber's Kit

Artisan's Tools are specialized kits used for crafting and trade work, each requiring its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check that uses it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Command Amulet
Text:
Each Shield Guardian is magically linked to a specific amulet. As long as the guardian and its amulet remain on the same plane, the amulet’s wearer can telepathically summon the guardian, and the guardian always knows the amulet’s distance and direction. If the guardian is within 60 feet of the wearer, half of the damage the wearer takes (rounded up) is instead transferred to the guardian.

Such amulets are often treasured heirlooms, passed down through magical families or arcane traditions.

Name: Common Wine (bottle)
Text: A simple bottle of table wine, commonly served in taverns and households.

Name: Component Pouch
Text: A watertight pouch filled with compartments designed to hold all the free Material components required for your spells.

Name: Cook's Utensils
Text:
Ability: Wisdom

Utilize: Enhance the flavor of food (DC 10), or detect spoiled or poisoned food (DC 15)

Craft: Rations

Artisan's Tools are specialized kits used for crafting and trade work, each requiring its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Copper
Text: Merchants often trade raw materials and goods like this without relying on coinage.

Name: Copper Dragon Scale Mail
Text:
Dragon Scale Mail is crafted from the scales of a single type of dragon. Some dragons gift their shed scales, while others are harvested from fallen dragons. Regardless of origin, this armor is highly prized.

While wearing it, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class, have Advantage on saving throws against Dragon breath weapons, and gain Resistance to Acid damage.

You may also take a Magic action to sense the distance and direction to the nearest copper dragon within 30 miles. This ability can't be used again until the next dawn.

The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Copper chalice with silver filigree
Text: Art objects include treasures such as golden idols, gemstone-studded jewelry, royal portraits, and ornate decorative pieces.

Name: Coral
Text:
A deep crimson gemstone.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Costume
Text: While wearing a Costume, you gain Advantage on ability checks made to impersonate the person or type of person it represents.

Name: Cotton Cloth (1 sq. yd.)
Text: Merchants often trade goods like this directly without relying on currency.

Name: Cow
Text: Merchants often trade livestock and other goods directly without relying on coinage.

Name: Crossbow Bolt Case
Text: A Crossbow Bolt Case can store up to 20 bolts securely.

Name: Crowbar
Text: Using a Crowbar grants Advantage on Strength checks where leverage can be applied.

Name: Crystal
Text: An Arcane Focus shaped from crystal, carved or adorned to channel magical energy. Sorcerers, Warlocks, and Wizards can use it as a Spellcasting Focus.

Name: Crystal Ball
Text: A typical crystal ball is about 6 inches across. While touching it, you can use it to cast the Scrying spell (save DC 17).

Name: Crystal Ball of Mind Reading
Text: While holding this crystal sphere, you can cast Scrying (save DC 17). Additionally, you can cast Detect Thoughts (save DC 17) on creatures you can see within 30 feet of the spell’s sensor. You do not need to concentrate on this Detect Thoughts spell, but it ends if the Scrying spell ends.

Name: Crystal Ball of Telepathy
Text: While touching this crystal orb, you can cast Scrying (save DC 17). You can also communicate telepathically with creatures you can see within 30 feet of the sensor. In addition, you may cast Suggestion (save DC 17) through the sensor on one of those creatures. You do not need to concentrate on this Suggestion spell, but it ends if Scrying ends. This Suggestion property can’t be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Crystal Ball of True Seeing
Text: While holding this crystal sphere, you can cast Scrying (save DC 17). In addition, you gain Truesight out to 120 feet, centered on the spell’s sensor.

Name: Cube of Force
Text:
This cube measures roughly an inch across, with each face marked differently. By pressing a face and expending the listed number of charges, you cast the associated spell (save DC 17) as shown in the Cube of Force Faces table.

The cube begins with 10 charges and regains 1d6 expended charges at dawn.

Cube of Force Faces
Spell | Charge Cost
Mage Armor | 1
Shield | 1
Tiny Hut | 3
Private Sanctum | 4
Resilient Sphere | 4
Wall of Force | 5

Name: Cube of Summoning
Text:
This Tiny cube resembles a toy jack-in-the-box. When you wind its crank as a Magic action, it plays a cheerful tune, the lid pops open, a creature appears in the nearest unoccupied space, and the lid snaps shut. The lid cannot be opened by any other means.

Roll on the Cube of Summoning table to determine which spell the cube casts to summon the creature. The spell is cast at level 5 (save DC 17, +9 attack bonus) and does not require Concentration, though you otherwise act as the spell’s caster.

Once the cube summons a creature, it cannot do so again until the next dawn.

Cube of Summoning
1d6 | Spell
1 | Summon Aberration
2 | Summon Beast
3 | Summon Construct
4 | Summon Dragon
5 | Summon Elemental
6 | Summon Fey

Name: Cubic Gate
Text:
This 3-inch cube radiates strong magical power. Each of its six faces is attuned to a different plane of existence, including the Material Plane; the remaining planes are chosen by the DM.

The cube holds 3 charges and regains 1d3 charges at dawn. As a Magic action, you may expend 1 charge to cast a spell linked to the cube.

Gate. Pressing a face once casts Gate, opening a portal to the plane tied to that face.

Plane Shift. Pressing a face twice casts Plane Shift, transporting the targets to the plane associated with that face.

Name: Daern's Instant Fortress
Text:
As a Magic action, you can place this 1-inch adamantine statuette on the ground and speak its command word, causing it to expand into a square adamantine tower. Speaking the command word again returns it to statuette form, but only if the tower is empty. Creatures in the space where the tower appears are pushed to the nearest unoccupied space, and unattended objects are likewise displaced.

The tower measures 20 feet per side and stands 30 feet tall, with arrow slits on all sides and a battlemented roof. Inside, it has two floors connected by a ladder, staircase, or ramp (your choice), ending at a trapdoor to the roof. A single ground-level door appears on the side facing you and opens only at your command (Bonus Action). It is immune to Knock and similar magic.

The tower cannot be tipped over.
