Its roof, walls, and door each have AC 20; HP 100; Immunity to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage except from siege equipment; and Resistance to all other damage. Returning the tower to statuette form does not repair it. Only a Wish spell can restore its Hit Points, counting as replicating a spell of level 8 or lower.

Name: Dagger
Text:
Finesse. You may use either Strength or Dexterity for attack and damage rolls with this weapon, but must use the same ability for both.

Light. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light weapon, you may make one additional attack as a Bonus Action using a different Light weapon. You do not add your ability modifier to the damage of this extra attack unless it is negative.

Thrown. You may throw this weapon to make a ranged attack, drawing it as part of the attack. If it is a melee weapon, use the same ability modifier you would use for a melee attack with it.

Mastery: Nick. When you make the extra attack granted by the Light property, you may instead make that extra attack as part of the Attack action. You may do this only once per turn.

Name: Dagger of Venom
Text:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic dagger.

As a Bonus Action, you can magically coat the blade with poison. The poison lasts for 1 minute or until you hit a creature with the dagger. That creature must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or take 2d10 Poison damage and become Poisoned for 1 minute. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Finesse. You may use Strength or Dexterity for attack and damage rolls, using the same ability for both.

Light. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light weapon, you may make one extra attack as a Bonus Action with a different Light weapon. You do not add your ability modifier to the damage of that extra attack unless it is negative.

Thrown. You can throw this weapon to make a ranged attack, drawing it as part of the attack. If it is a melee weapon, use the same ability modifier you would use for a melee attack.

Mastery: Nick. When you make the extra attack granted by the Light property, you may instead make that extra attack as part of the Attack action. You can do this only once per turn.

Name: Dancing Sword
Text:
As a Bonus Action, you can throw this magic sword into the air. It hovers, flies up to 30 feet, and attacks a creature within 5 feet of it, using your attack roll and adding your ability modifier to damage.

While hovering, you can use a Bonus Action to move it up to 30 feet to another spot within 30 feet of you, and as part of that same Bonus Action, you may have it attack a creature within 5 feet of it.

After the sword makes its fourth attack, it returns to you and attempts to land in your hand. If your hands are full, it drops at your feet. If it cannot reach you, it moves as close as possible before falling. It also stops hovering if you grab it or move more than 30 feet away.

Name: Dark Shard Amulet
Text:
This amulet is crafted from a fragment of a durable substance originating from an otherworldly realm. While wearing it, you gain the following benefits.

Spellcasting Focus. You may use the amulet as a Spellcasting Focus for your Warlock spells.

Unknown Spell. As a Magic action, you may attempt to cast a Warlock cantrip you do not know, provided it has a casting time of 1 action. Make a DC 10 Intelligence (Arcana) check. On success, you cast the spell; on failure, the spell fizzles and the action is wasted. You cannot use this property again until you finish a Long Rest.

Name: Dart
Text:
Range. A ranged weapon lists two distances: normal range and long range. Attacks beyond normal range are made with Disadvantage, and you cannot attack beyond long range.

Finesse. You may use Strength or Dexterity for attack and damage rolls, using the same ability for both.

Thrown. You may throw this weapon to make a ranged attack, drawing it as part of the attack. If it is a melee weapon, use the same ability modifier you would use for a melee attack.

Mastery: Vex. When you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage, you gain Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

Name: Decanter of Endless Water
Text:
This sealed flask sloshes as though filled with water. It weighs 2 pounds.

As a Magic action, you can unstopper it and speak one of three command words, causing either fresh or salt water (your choice) to pour out until the start of your next turn.

Splash. Produces 1 gallon of water.

Fountain. Produces 5 gallons of water.

Geyser. Produces 30 gallons of water in a 30-foot-long, 1-foot-wide Line. If you hold the decanter, you can direct the stream. One creature in the Line must succeed on a DC 13 Strength saving throw or take 1d4 Bludgeoning damage and fall Prone. Instead of a creature, you may target an unattended object weighing up to 200 pounds, which is knocked over.

Name: Deck of Illusions
Text:
This box holds a set of cards. A complete deck contains 34 cards: 32 depicting creatures and two with mirrored faces. A deck found as treasure is often missing 1d20 − 1 cards.

The deck works only when cards are drawn at random. As a Magic action, you draw a random card and throw it onto the ground within 30 feet. An illusion of a creature—determined by the Deck of Illusions table—appears above the card and persists until dispelled. The illusion behaves like the creature it depicts but cannot cause harm. While within 120 feet and able to see it, you may take a Magic action to move the illusion up to 30 feet from its card.

Physical interaction reveals the illusion as false. A creature using a Study action can identify it with a DC 15 Intelligence (Investigation) check. The illusion ends if the card is moved or dispelled. When the illusion ends, the image on the card vanishes, and the card cannot be used again.

Deck of Illusions
1d100 | Illusion
01–03 Adult Red Dragon
04–06 Archmage
07–09 Assassin
10–12 Bandit Captain
13–15 Beholder
16–18 Berserker
19–21 Bugbear Warrior
22–24 Cloud Giant
25–27 Druid
28–30 Erinyes
31–33 Ettin
34–36 Fire Giant
37–39 Frost Giant
40–42 Gnoll Warrior
43–45 Goblin Warrior
46–48 Guardian Naga
49–51 Hill Giant
52–54 Hobgoblin Warrior
55–57 Incubus
58–60 Iron Golem
61–63 Knight
64–66 Kobold Warrior
67–69 Lich
70–72 Medusa
73–75 Night Hag
76–78 Ogre
79–81 Oni
82–84 Priest
85–87 Succubus
88–90 Troll
91–93 Warrior Veteran
94–96 Wyvern
97–00 The card drawer

Name: Deck of Many Things
Text:
Usually stored in a pouch or box, this deck contains ivory or vellum cards. Most decks (75 percent) contain thirteen cards, while some contain twenty-two. Use the appropriate column of the Deck of Many Things table when determining cards.

Before drawing, you must declare how many cards you intend to draw. Any cards drawn beyond that number have no effect. Each card takes effect immediately when drawn. You must draw each card within 1 hour of the previous one; if you fail to do so, the remaining cards fly from the deck and take effect at once.

Drawn cards vanish. Except for the Fool and Jester, cards reappear in the deck, allowing repeats. If Fool or Jester is drawn again, reroll.

Deck of Many Things
(13-Card Deck) | (22-Card Deck) | Card
01–05 | — | Balance
06–10 | — | Comet
11–14 | 01–08 | Donjon
— | 09–16 | Euryale
19–23 | — | Fates
— | 24–27 | Flames
28–31 | — | Fool
32–36 | 17–24 | Gem
37–41 | 25–32 | Jester
42–46 | 33–40 | Key
47–51 | 41–48 | Knight
52–56 | — | Moon
57–60 | 49–56 | Puzzle
61–64 | 57–64 | Rogue
65–68 | — | Ruin
69–73 | 65–72 | Sage
74–77 | 73–80 | Skull
78–82 | 81–88 | Star
83–87 | — | Sun
88–91 | 89–96 | Talons
92–96 | — | Throne
97–00 | 97–00 | Void

Each card’s effect is described below.

Balance. Increase one ability score by 2 (max 22) and decrease another by 2 (cannot reduce a score of 5 or lower). You may instead choose no effect.

Comet. In your next combat, choose one foe when rolling Initiative. If you personally reduce that foe to 0 HP, you gain Advantage on Death Saving Throws for 1 year.

Donjon. You vanish into suspended animation inside an extradimensional sphere. Only a Wish spell can reveal its location. You draw no more cards.

Euryale. You are cursed, taking a −2 penalty to saving throws. Only a god or the Fates card can end the curse.

Fates. You may undo or avoid one event as though it never occurred.

Flames. A powerful devil becomes your sworn enemy until one of you dies.

Fool. You have Disadvantage on all D20 Tests for 72 hours. Draw one additional card.

Gem. Twenty-five pieces of jewelry worth 2000 GP each or fifty gems worth 1000 GP each appear.

Jester. You gain Advantage on D20 Tests for 72 hours, or you may draw two extra cards.

Key. A Rare or rarer magic weapon you are proficient with appears.

Knight. A loyal Knight appears and serves you until death.

Moon. You gain 1d3 castings of Wish.

Puzzle. Permanently reduce Intelligence or Wisdom by 1d4 + 1 (minimum 1). Draw one extra card.

Rogue. An NPC becomes secretly Hostile toward you. Only a Wish or divine intervention can end this.

Ruin. All your wealth except magic items is lost.

Sage. Within one year, you may meditate to receive a truthful answer to one question.

Skull. An Avatar of Death appears and fights only you. Allies who damage it summon their own avatar.

Star. Increase one ability score by 2 (max 24).

Sun. A magic item appears, and you gain 10 Temporary Hit Points at dawn each day.

Talons. All magic items you carry disintegrate; Artifacts vanish.

Throne. You gain proficiency and Expertise in one of several skills and rightful ownership of a small keep (currently occupied by monsters).

Void. Your soul is trapped in an object guarded by powerful beings. Your body becomes inert. Wish cannot restore your soul but reveals its location. You draw no more cards.

Name: Defender
Text:
This magic weapon grants a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

The first time you make an attack with it on each of your turns, you may shift some or all of this bonus to your Armor Class. For example, you could lower the weapon’s attack and damage bonus to +1 and gain a +2 bonus to AC. These adjusted bonuses last until the start of your next turn, and you must be holding the weapon to benefit from the AC increase.

Name: Demon Armor
Text:
While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC and understand the Abyssal language. Its clawed gauntlets cause your Unarmed Strikes to deal 1d8 Slashing damage instead of Bludgeoning, and you gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls with them.

Curse. Once you put on this cursed armor, you cannot remove it unless affected by Remove Curse or similar magic. While wearing it, you have Disadvantage on attack rolls against demons and on saving throws against their spells and abilities.

Name: Demonomicon of Arcantra the Hex-Sage
Text:
This infamous tome, authored by the archmage Arcantra the Hex-Sage, chronicles the layers and denizens of the Abyss. It is considered the most complete and profane demonological work in existence. Though demons have tried to destroy it, the book continually restores its missing pages, fueled by a fragment of the Abyss sealed within its script.

Abyssal Lore. When consulting the Demonomicon for Intelligence checks about demons or Wisdom (Survival) checks related to the Abyss, you gain Advantage.

Containment. The first ten pages are blank. As a Magic action, while holding the book, you may target a Fiend trapped inside a Magic Circle. The Fiend must make a DC 20 Charisma saving throw with Disadvantage or be imprisoned within one of the blank pages, which then fills with its name and misdeeds. This property recharges at dawn.

After each Long Rest, if you and the book are on the same plane, one trapped Fiend may attempt to possess you. Make a DC 20 Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the Fiend controls your body until it chooses to release you as a Magic action, appearing in the nearest unoccupied space. On a success, that Fiend cannot try again for 7 days.

When found, the tome contains 1d4 trapped Fiends, usually demons.

Ensnarement. While carrying the book, when you cast Magic Circle naming only Fiends or Planar Binding targeting a Fiend, the spell is cast at level 9 and the Fiend has Disadvantage on its saving throw.

Fiendish Scourging. When you roll damage for a spell you cast against a Fiend, you use the maximum possible result.

Random Properties. The artifact has: 2 minor beneficial properties, 1 minor detrimental property, and 1 major detrimental property.

Spells. The book holds 8 charges and regains 1d8 charges at dawn. As a Magic action, you may cast one of the following spells (save DC 20), expending the listed charges:
Magic Circle (1), Magic Jar (3), Planar Ally (3), Planar Binding (2), Plane Shift to the Abyss (3), Summon Fiend (3), Tasha's Hideous Laughter (0).

Destroying the Demonomicon. Six different demon lords must each tear out one-sixth of the book. The pages reappear after 24 hours. Before that time passes, opening the empty binding transports the opener to a budding layer of the Abyss hidden within the tome. At its center lies Vraaz-Ulruu's Staff. Removing the staff reduces the Demonomicon to a mundane, outdated copy of the Codex of Zyren, which can be destroyed normally. When the staff is removed, Vraaz-Ulruu becomes instantly aware.

Name: Detailed, life-sized dragonborn skull cast in electrum
Text: Art objects include treasures such as golden idols, gemstone-studded jewelry, royal portraits, and ornate decorative pieces.

Name: Diamond
Text:
A gemstone that may appear blue-white, canary yellow, pink, brown, or deep blue.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Dice Set
Text:
Ability: Wisdom

Utilize: Detect cheating (DC 10) or win the game (DC 20).

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any check using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Dimensional Shackles
Text:
You can take a Utilize action to secure these shackles onto a creature that is Incapacitated. They resize to fit creatures from Small to Large. A creature bound by them cannot use any form of extradimensional movement, such as teleportation or planar travel, though it may still pass through an existing portal.

You and any creature you designate when applying the shackles may take a Utilize action to remove them. Once every 30 days, the restrained creature may attempt a DC 30 Strength (Athletics) check; on success, it breaks free and destroys the shackles.

Name: Diplomat's Pack
Text: A Diplomat's Pack contains: a Chest, Fine Clothes, Ink, 5 Ink Pens, a Lamp, 2 Map or Scroll Cases, 4 flasks of Oil, 5 sheets of Paper, 5 sheets of Parchment, Perfume, and a Tinderbox.

Name: Disguise Kit
Text:
Ability: Charisma

Utilize: Apply makeup or disguise elements (DC 10)

Craft: Costume

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any related ability check. If you are also proficient in a relevant skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Draft Horse
Text: A sturdy working horse commonly used for hauling loads and pulling wagons.

Name: Dragon Scale Mail
Text:
The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Multiple variations of this armor exist, each crafted from the scales of a different dragon type:

Black Dragon Scale Mail
Blue Dragon Scale Mail
Brass Dragon Scale Mail
Bronze Dragon Scale Mail
Copper Dragon Scale Mail
Gold Dragon Scale Mail
Green Dragon Scale Mail
Red Dragon Scale Mail
Silver Dragon Scale Mail
White Dragon Scale Mail

Name: Dragon Slayer
Text:
This magic weapon grants a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

When you hit a Dragon with it, the attack deals an additional 3d6 damage of the weapon’s type.

Name: Dragonchess Set
Text:
Ability: Wisdom

Utilize: Detect cheating (DC 10) or win the match (DC 20)

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any check using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Dread Helm
Text: When you wear this intimidating steel helm, your eyes emit a red glow and the rest of your face is shrouded in shadow.

Name: Driftglobe
Text:
This small glass sphere weighs 1 pound. If you are within 60 feet of it, you may command it to shed light equivalent to the Light or Daylight spell (your choice). The Daylight effect cannot be used again until the next dawn.

As a Magic action, you may command the glowing orb to rise and hover up to 5 feet above the ground. It remains suspended until grasped. If you move more than 60 feet away, it follows you by the shortest route. If blocked, it gently descends and becomes inactive, and its light fades.

Name: Druidic Focus
Text:
A Druidic Focus is crafted in a specific form—carved, painted, or bound with ribbon—to channel primal magic. Druids and Rangers may use it as a Spellcasting Focus.

Common variations include:

Sprig of mistletoe
Wooden staff
yew wand

Name: Drum
Text:
Ability: Charisma

Utilize: Perform a known rhythm (DC 10) or improvise a tune (DC 15)

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any related check. If you are also proficient in a relevant skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Dulcimer
Text:
Ability: Charisma

Utilize: Play a familiar melody (DC 10) or create an improvised piece (DC 15)

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any check using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Dungeoneer's Pack
Text: A Dungeoneer's Pack includes: Backpack, Caltrops, Crowbar, 2 flasks of Oil, 10 days of Rations, Rope, Tinderbox, 10 Torches, and a Waterskin.

Name: Dust of Disappearance
Text: This fine, sand-like powder is sufficient for a single use. When you take a Utilize action to scatter it into the air, you and all creatures and objects within a 10-foot Emanation centered on you gain the Invisible condition for 2d4 minutes. All affected targets share the same duration. The dust is consumed when activated. A creature loses the Invisible condition immediately after making an attack roll, dealing damage, or casting a spell.

Name: Dust of Dryness
Text:
This packet contains 1d6 + 4 pinches of magical dust. As a Utilize action, you may sprinkle a pinch over water, transforming up to a 15-foot Cube of it into a marble-sized pellet that floats or rests nearby. The pellet weighs almost nothing. A creature may take a Utilize action to crush the pellet against a hard surface, releasing the stored water and destroying the pellet.

As a Utilize action, you may sprinkle a pinch onto a water-based Elemental within 5 feet of you. That creature must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw, taking 10d6 Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

Name: Dust of Sneezing and Choking
Text:
This powder resembles Dust of Disappearance, and Identify incorrectly reveals it as such. It contains enough dust for one use.

As a Utilize action, you may throw the dust into the air. You and all creatures in a 30-foot Emanation centered on you must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. Constructs, Elementals, Oozes, Plants, and Undead automatically succeed.

On a failed save, a creature begins violently sneezing, becomes Incapacitated, and starts suffocating. The creature repeats the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. A Lesser Restoration spell also ends the effect.

Name: Dwarven Plate
Text: While wearing this armor, you gain a +2 bonus to Armor Class. Additionally, when an effect forces you to move along the ground against your will, you may use a Reaction to reduce the distance moved by up to 10 feet.

Name: Dwarven Thrower
Attunement: Requires Attunement By a Dwarf or a Creature Attuned to a Belt of Dwarvenkind
Text:
This magic weapon grants a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls. It has the Thrown property with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet. When you hit with a ranged attack using it, the attack deals an additional 1d8 Force damage, or 2d8 Force damage if the target is a Giant. After the attack—hit or miss—the weapon immediately returns to your hand.

Thrown. You may throw this weapon to make a ranged attack, drawing it as part of the attack. If it is a melee weapon, use the same ability modifier you would use for a melee attack.

Versatile. This weapon can be wielded with one or two hands. The damage value in parentheses applies when used with two hands.

Mastery: Push. When you hit a creature with this weapon, you may push it up to 10 feet directly away from you if it is Large or smaller.

Name: Dynamite Stick
Text:
As an action, you may light a Dynamite Stick and throw it to a point up to 60 feet away, where it detonates. Each creature in a 5-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point must make a DC 12 Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d6 Force damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

Binding multiple sticks takes 1 minute. Each additional stick increases the explosion’s damage by 1d6 (up to 10d6 total) and expands the radius by 5 feet (up to a maximum of 20 feet).

You may also spend 1 minute rigging a longer fuse, allowing the dynamite to explode after a set delay such as 1 minute or 10 minutes.

Name: Ear Horn of Perception
Text: When you hold this horn to your ear, it negates the effects of the Deafened condition affecting you. A simple but reliable tool favored by travelers who expect loud environments.

Name: Efreet Bottle
Text:
Using a Magic action to unseal this ornate brass vessel releases a billowing surge of dense smoke. At the end of your turn, the smoke vanishes in a harmless burst of flame, and an Efreet appears in an empty space within 30 feet of you.

The first time the bottle is opened, the GM rolls on the table below to determine the outcome.

1d10  Effect
1  The efreet immediately becomes hostile and attacks you. After 5 rounds of combat, it disappears, and the bottle loses all magical properties.
2–9  The efreet comprehends your languages and follows your commands for 1 hour. It then returns to the bottle, which reseals itself with a new stopper that cannot be removed for 24 hours. The next two openings produce the same effect. On the fourth opening, the efreet breaks free, vanishes, and the bottle becomes nonmagical.
10  The efreet understands your languages and can cast Wish once on your behalf. It disappears after granting the wish or after 1 hour, and the bottle becomes mundane.

Name: Efreet Chainmail
Text: While equipped with this armor, your Armor Class increases by +3, you gain Immunity to Fire damage, and you are able to speak and understand Primordial. Additionally, you can walk atop molten rock as though it were solid stone, unaffected by its heat.

Name: Elemental Gem
Text:
Several forms of this gem exist, each tied to a different elemental force:

Elemental Gem, Blue Sapphire
Elemental Gem, Emerald
Elemental Gem, Red Corundum
Elemental Gem, Yellow Diamond

Name: Elemental Gem, Blue Sapphire
Text: This gemstone holds a fragment of elemental power. When you use a Utilize action to shatter it, an Air Elemental manifests, and the gem loses all magic. The elemental appears in an unoccupied space nearest to where the gem was broken, understands your languages, and follows your commands. It acts immediately after you on your Initiative count. The elemental vanishes after 1 hour, when it drops to 0 hit points, or when you dismiss it using a Bonus Action.

Name: Elemental Gem, Emerald
Text: This gemstone holds a fragment of elemental essence. When you use a Utilize action to crush the gem, a Water Elemental materializes, and the gem loses all magical power. The elemental appears in the nearest unoccupied space to where the gem was broken, understands your languages, and follows your commands. It acts immediately after your turn on your Initiative count. The elemental vanishes after 1 hour, when it is reduced to 0 hit points, or when you dismiss it using a Bonus Action.

Name: Elemental Gem, Red Corundum
Text: This stone contains a spark of elemental force. When you take a Utilize action to shatter it, a Fire Elemental is summoned, and the gem becomes nonmagical. The elemental appears in the closest unoccupied space to the broken gem, understands your languages, and obeys your instructions. It takes its turn immediately after yours on your Initiative count. The elemental disappears after 1 hour, when it dies, or when you dismiss it as a Bonus Action.

Name: Elemental Gem, Yellow Diamond
Text: This crystal contains a sliver of elemental might. When you use a Utilize action to break the gem, an Earth Elemental emerges, and the gem loses its magic. The elemental appears in the nearest unoccupied space to the shattered gem, understands your languages, and follows your commands. It acts immediately after you on your Initiative count. The elemental fades after 1 hour, when it is destroyed, or when you dismiss it with a Bonus Action.

Name: Elephant
Text: A massive and powerful creature used as a mount or beast of burden. Elephants are valued for their strength and endurance.

Name: Elixir of Health
Text:
When consumed, this potion removes all magical diseases affecting you. It also ends the following conditions: Blinded, Deafened, Paralyzed, and Poisoned.

The transparent red liquid contains faint, glowing bubbles suspended within it.

Name: Elven Chainmail
Text: While wearing this armor, your Armor Class increases by +1. You are treated as trained with this armor even if you lack proficiency with Medium Armor or Armor in general.

Name: Emblem
Text: For an Emblem to function as a Holy Symbol, it must be displayed on cloth—such as a tabard, banner, or on a Shield. A Holy Symbol has a distinct design, often adorned or painted, to channel divine magic. Clerics and Paladins can use a Holy Symbol as their Spellcasting Focus.

Name: Embroidered glove set with jewel chips
Text: Art objects encompass a wide range of crafted valuables, including golden idols, gemstone-studded jewelry, regal portraits, and ornate decorated items.

Name: Embroidered silk and velvet mantle set with numerous moonstones
Text: Art objects encompass a wide range of crafted valuables, including golden idols, gemstone-studded jewelry, regal portraits, and ornate decorated items.

Name: Embroidered silk handkerchief
Text: Art objects encompass a wide range of crafted valuables, including golden idols, gemstone-studded jewelry, regal portraits, and ornate decorated items.

Name: Emerald
Text: A vivid green gemstone of high clarity and value. Gemstones are compact, easy to transport, and far simpler to secure than an equivalent amount of coinage.

Name: Enduring Spellbook
Text: This spellbook and all writing within it are impervious to damage from fire or water. Furthermore, the book does not decay or wear out over time.

Name: Energy Bow
Text:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magical bow, which lacks a physical string. Each time you mimic the motion of drawing a bow, a golden arcane arrow forms, ready to be fired. Arrows created by this weapon deal Force damage instead of Piercing damage on a hit, and they vanish after hitting or missing. Until they disappear, the arrows shed Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet.

This weapon has the following additional features.

Arrow of Restraint. When making a ranged attack with this bow, you may choose to restrain the target instead of dealing damage. On a hit, the target must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or become Restrained for 1 minute. A creature Restrained by the arrow can use an action to attempt a DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check to break free, ending the effect on itself if successful.

Arrow of Transport. As a Magic action, you can fire an energy arrow at a visible target within 60 feet. The target must be a willing Medium or smaller creature, or an unattended object small enough to fit inside a 5-foot Cube. The arrow teleports the target to an unoccupied space you can see within 10 feet of you.

Energy Ladder. As a Magic action, you can fire a volley of energy arrows at a wall up to 60 feet away. These arrows form glowing steps protruding from the surface, creating a magical ladder up to 60 feet long. The ladder lasts for 1 minute before fading.

Name: Enspelled Armor
Text:
Several versions of this armor exist, each tied to a different spell level:

Enspelled Armor (Cantrip)
Enspelled Armor (Level 1)
Enspelled Armor (Level 2)
Enspelled Armor (Level 3)
Enspelled Armor (Level 4)
Enspelled Armor (Level 5)
Enspelled Armor (Level 6)
Enspelled Armor (Level 7)
Enspelled Armor (Level 8)

Name: Enspelled Staff
Text:
Versatile. A Versatile weapon may be wielded with one or two hands. The damage value shown in parentheses applies when using two hands to make a melee attack.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this staff, you can force it to attempt a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus the ability modifier used for the attack and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature gains the Prone condition.

Several versions of this staff exist, listed below:

Enspelled Staff (Cantrip)
Enspelled Staff (Level 1)
Enspelled Staff (Level 2)
Enspelled Staff (Level 3)
Enspelled Staff (Level 4)
Enspelled Staff (Level 5)
Enspelled Staff (Level 6)
Enspelled Staff (Level 7)
Enspelled Staff (Level 8)

Name: Enspelled Weapon
Text:
This item exists in multiple spell-bound versions, each tied to a different spell level:

Enspelled Weapon (Cantrip)
Enspelled Weapon (Level 1)
Enspelled Weapon (Level 2)
Enspelled Weapon (Level 3)
Enspelled Weapon (Level 4)
Enspelled Weapon (Level 5)
Enspelled Weapon (Level 6)
Enspelled Weapon (Level 7)
Enspelled Weapon (Level 8)

Name: Entertainer's Pack
Text: This pack includes equipment commonly used by performers: a Backpack, Bedroll, Bell, Bullseye Lantern, 3 Costumes, Mirror, 8 flasks of Oil, 9 days of Rations, a Tinderbox, and a Waterskin.

Name: Ersatz Eye
Text: This magical prosthetic replaces a missing or removed eye. While placed in your eye socket, you can see through it as if it were your natural eye. You may insert or remove it using a Magic action, and it cannot be taken from you against your will while you are alive.

Name: Essence of Ether
Text: A creature exposed to this poison must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or gain the Poisoned condition for 8 hours. While Poisoned in this way, the creature also becomes Unconscious. The creature awakens if it takes damage or if another creature uses an action to shake it awake.

Name: Eversmoking Bottle
Text:
As a Magic action, you may open or close this bottle.

When opened, dense smoke pours out, creating a cloud that fills a 60-foot Emanation centered on the bottle. The area inside the smoke is considered Heavily Obscured.

For every minute the bottle stays open, the Emanation expands by 10 feet until reaching a maximum radius of 120 feet.

Closing the bottle causes the smoke to remain stationary until it dissipates after 10 minutes. A strong wind—such as that produced by the Gust of Wind spell—clears the cloud after 1 minute.

Name: Executioner's Axe
Text:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this enchanted axe.

Whenever you hit a Humanoid with this weapon, it suffers an additional 2d6 Slashing damage, and you gain Temporary Hit Points equal to the extra damage dealt.

Name: Exotic Saddle
Text: A saddle includes a bit, bridle, reins, and any other gear required for proper use. An Exotic Saddle is necessary for riding aquatic or flying mounts.

Name: Explorer's Pack
Text: An Explorer's Pack contains the following equipment: Backpack, Bedroll, 2 flasks of Oil, 10 days of Rations, Rope, Tinderbox, 10 Torches, and a Waterskin.

Name: Eye Agate
Text:
A gemstone marked with circular patterns in shades of gray, white, brown, blue, or green.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and far easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Eye and Hand of Vekna
Text:
Vekna was once a powerful spellcaster who built a brutal dominion through sorcery and conquest. Fearing death, he transformed himself into a lich to escape mortality.

His reign ended when his treacherous lieutenant, Kasss, confronted him in a devastating battle. Only a single hand and eye survived—grisly Artifacts that continue to carry out Vekna's dark intentions.

The Eye of Vekna and the Hand of Vekna are separate Artifacts, sometimes found together. The eye resembles a bloodshot organ torn from its socket, while the hand is a withered left appendage.

Random Properties. Each Artifact possesses the following random traits:

1 minor beneficial property
1 major beneficial property
1 minor detrimental property

Attuning to the Eye. To attune, you must press the eye into an empty socket. It fuses to your skull and remains until your death. Removing it kills you.

Properties of the Eye. While attuned, your alignment becomes Neutral Evil and you gain:

Truesight. You have Truesight out to 240 feet.

Spellcasting. The eye holds 8 charges and regains 1d4 + 4 charges at dawn. You may cast spells from the Eye of Vekna Spells table (save DC 18), expending the listed charges. Each time you cast a spell, there is a 5 percent chance Vekna rips out your soul, consumes it, and puppets your body. If this occurs, you become an NPC under the GM's control.

Eye of Vekna Spells
Spell — Charge Cost
Clairvoyance — 2
Crown of Madness — 1
Disintegrate — 4
Dominate Monster — 5
Eyebite — 4

X-ray Vision. As a Magic action, you gain X-ray vision for 1 minute with a 30-foot range. Solid objects appear transparent. You can see through 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of common metal, or 3 feet of wood or dirt. Thicker materials or a thin sheet of lead block this vision.

Attuning to the Hand. To attune, press the hand against the stump of your missing left hand. It grafts to your arm and functions normally. Removing it kills you.

Properties of the Hand. While attuned, your alignment becomes Neutral Evil and you gain:

Great Strength. Your Strength becomes 20 unless it is already 20 or higher.

Icy Touch. Melee spell attacks made with the hand, or weapon attacks using it, deal an extra 2d8 Cold damage.

Spellcasting. The hand has 8 charges and regains 1d4 + 4 charges at dawn. You may cast spells from the Hand of Vekna Spells table (save DC 18). Each time you cast a spell, the hand casts Suggestion on you (DC 18, no Concentration), urging you to commit an evil act.

Hand of Vekna Spells
Spell — Charge Cost
Finger of Death — 5
Sleep — 1
Slow — 2
Teleport — 3

Properties When Wielding Both. If attuned to both the eye and hand, you gain:

Danger Sense. You have Advantage on Initiative rolls.

Necrotic Reduction. As a Magic action, target a creature within 5 feet. It makes a DC 18 Constitution save, taking 7d6 Necrotic damage on a failure or half on a success. A creature reduced to 0 Hit Points becomes green slime (see chapter 3). Nonmagical metal or organic items it carried are destroyed.

Poison Immunity. You are immune to Poison damage and the Poisoned condition.

Regeneration. If you begin your turn with at least 1 Hit Point, you regain 1d10 Hit Points.

Wish. You may cast Wish. Once used, this property can't be used again for 30 days.

Destroying the Artifacts. If both the Eye and Hand are attached to the same creature and that creature is slain by the Sword of Kasss, both Artifacts ignite, crumble to ash, and are destroyed. Any other attempt appears successful but merely causes the Artifact to reform in one of Vekna's hidden vaults.

Name: Eye of Vekna
Text:
Vekna was a formidable mage who built a ruthless empire through arcane might. To escape death, he embraced undeath and became a lich.

His downfall came when his lieutenant Kasss betrayed him in a catastrophic battle. Only a single hand and eye survived, now infamous Artifacts that still act according to Vekna's will.

The Eye of Vekna and the Hand of Vekna may appear together or separately. The eye resembles a bloodshot organ torn from its socket.

Random Properties. The Eye of Vekna has:

1 minor beneficial property
1 major beneficial property
1 minor detrimental property

Attuning to the Eye. To attune, press the eye into an empty socket. It fuses to your skull until your death. Removing it kills you.

Properties of the Eye. While attuned, your alignment becomes Neutral Evil and you gain:

Truesight. You have Truesight out to 240 feet.

Spellcasting. The eye contains 8 charges and regains 1d4 + 4 charges at dawn. You may cast spells from the Eye of Vekna Spells table (save DC 18), spending the listed charges. Each spell cast carries a 5 percent chance that Vekna devours your soul and takes control of your body.

Eye of Vekna Spells
Spell — Charge Cost
Clairvoyance — 2
Crown of Madness — 1
Disintegrate — 4
Dominate Monster — 5
Eyebite — 4

X-ray Vision. As a Magic action, you gain X-ray vision for 1 minute with a 30-foot range. Objects appear transparent, and the vision penetrates 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of common metal, or 3 feet of wood or dirt. Lead or thicker materials block it.

Destroying the Eye. If both the Eye and Hand of Vekna are attached to the same creature and that creature is slain by the Sword of Kasss, both Artifacts burn away into ash. Any other destruction attempt merely causes the Artifact to reform in one of Vekna's hidden vaults.

Name: Eye patch decorated with tiny blue sapphires and moonstones
Text: Art objects encompass a wide variety of crafted valuables, such as golden idols, gemstone-adorned jewelry, portraits of ancient rulers, and ornate decorated items.

Name: Eyes of Charming
Text: These crystal lenses rest over your eyes and contain 3 charges. While wearing them, you may expend 1 or more charges to cast Charm Person (save DC 13). Spending 1 charge casts the spell at level 1, and each additional charge increases the spell's level by one. All expended charges are restored at dawn.

Name: Eyes of Minute Seeing
Text: These crystal lenses fit over your eyes. While worn, they greatly enhance your close-range sight out to 1 foot, granting you Darkvision within that distance and Advantage on Intelligence (Investigation) checks made to inspect anything within that range.

Name: Eyes of the Eagle
Text: These crystal lenses rest over your eyes. While wearing them, you gain Advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight. In clear conditions, you can discern fine details of distant creatures or objects as small as 2 feet across.

Name: Feed (per day)
Text: Daily feed suitable for most domesticated mounts and beasts of burden.

Name: Figurine of Wondrous Power
Text:
This item exists in multiple magical forms, each carved from a different material and capable of transforming into a creature:

Figurine of Wondrous Power, Bronze Griffon
Figurine of Wondrous Power, Ebony Fly
Figurine of Wondrous Power, Golden Lions
Figurine of Wondrous Power, Ivory Goats
Figurine of Wondrous Power, Marble Elephant
Figurine of Wondrous Power, Obsidian Steed
Figurine of Wondrous Power, Onyx Dog
Figurine of Wondrous Power, Serpentine Owl
Figurine of Wondrous Power, Silver Raven

Name: Figurine of Wondrous Power, Bronze Griffon
Text:
A Figurine of Wondrous Power is a small statuette that fits easily in a pocket. When you take a Magic action to throw it onto the ground within 60 feet, it transforms into the creature described below. If the intended space is occupied or too small, the transformation does not occur.

The creature is Friendly toward you and your allies. It understands your languages, follows your commands, and acts immediately after you on your Initiative count. If you give no orders, it only defends itself.

Each figurine has a specific duration for its creature form. When that duration ends, or if the creature drops to 0 Hit Points, or if you use a Magic action while touching it, it returns to figurine form. Once it reverts, it cannot be used again until the listed time has passed.

Bronze Griffon (Rare). This bronze statuette depicts a rearing griffon. It can transform into a Griffon for up to 6 hours. After use, it cannot be activated again for 5 days.

Name: Figurine of Wondrous Power, Ebony Fly
Text:
A Figurine of Wondrous Power is a pocket-sized statuette. When you take a Magic action to throw it onto the ground within 60 feet, it becomes the creature described below. If the space is occupied or too small, the transformation fails.

The creature is Friendly to you and your allies, understands your languages, obeys your commands, and acts immediately after you on your Initiative count. Without commands, it only defends itself.

The creature remains for the duration listed for that figurine. It reverts early if reduced to 0 Hit Points or if you use a Magic action while touching it. After reverting, it cannot be used again until the specified time passes.

Ebony Fly (Rare). This ebony carving shaped like a horsefly becomes a Giant Fly for up to 12 hours and can serve as a mount. After use, it cannot be activated again for 2 days.

Name: Figurine of Wondrous Power, Golden Lions
Text:
A Figurine of Wondrous Power is a small statuette that can be thrown with a Magic action to a point within 60 feet, transforming into the creature described below. If the space is occupied or too small, the transformation does not occur.

The creature is Friendly to you and your allies, understands your languages, follows your commands, and acts immediately after you on your Initiative count. Without commands, it only defends itself.

The creature lasts for the duration listed for that figurine. It reverts early if reduced to 0 Hit Points or if you take a Magic action while touching it. After reverting, it cannot be used again until the listed time has passed.

Golden Lions (Rare). These paired golden lion statuettes may be used one at a time or together. Each becomes a Lion for up to 1 hour. Once used, a lion cannot be activated again for 7 days.

Name: Figurine of Wondrous Power, Ivory Goats
Text:
A Figurine of Wondrous Power is a pocket-sized statuette. When you take a Magic action to throw it onto the ground within 60 feet, it becomes the creature described below. If the space is occupied or too small, the transformation fails.

The creature is Friendly to you and your allies, understands your languages, obeys your commands, and acts immediately after you on your Initiative count. Without commands, it only defends itself.

The creature lasts for the duration listed for that figurine. It reverts early if reduced to 0 Hit Points or if you take a Magic action while touching it. After reverting, it cannot be used again until the listed time has passed.

Ivory Goats (Rare). These three ivory goat statuettes each have unique properties:

Goat of Terror. Becomes a Giant Goat for up to 3 hours. It cannot attack, but you may harmlessly remove its horns to use as weapons: one becomes a +1 Lance, the other a +2 Longsword. Removing a horn requires a Magic action. The weapons vanish when the goat reverts. While you ride it, any Hostile creature starting its turn within a 30-foot Emanation must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be Frightened for 1 minute, until you dismount, or until the goat reverts. The creature repeats the save at the end of each turn, ending the effect on a success. Once a creature succeeds, it is immune for 24 hours. After use, this figurine cannot be activated again for 15 days.

Goat of Traveling. Becomes a Large goat with the statistics of a Riding Horse. It has 24 charges; each hour or part of an hour in goat form costs 1 charge. It can be used freely while it has charges. When charges reach 0, it reverts and cannot be used again for 7 days, at which point all charges return.

Goat of Travail. Becomes a Giant Goat for up to 3 hours. After use, it cannot be activated again for 30 days.

Name: Figurine of Wondrous Power, Marble Elephant
Text:
A Figurine of Wondrous Power is a statuette small enough to fit in a pocket. If you take a Magic action to throw the figurine to a point on the ground within 60 feet of yourself, the figurine becomes a living creature specified in the figurine's description below. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The creature is Friendly to you and your allies. It understands your languages, obeys your commands, and takes its turn immediately after you on your Initiative count. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but takes no other actions.

The creature exists for a duration specific to each figurine. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if its creature form drops to 0 Hit Points or if you take a Magic action while touching the creature to make it revert to figurine form. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified below.

Marble Elephant (Rare). This marble statuette resembles a trumpeting elephant. It can become an Elephant for up to 24 hours. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 7 days have passed.

Name: Figurine of Wondrous Power, Obsidian Steed
Text:
A Figurine of Wondrous Power is a statuette small enough to fit in a pocket. If you take a Magic action to throw the figurine to a point on the ground within 60 feet of yourself, the figurine becomes a living creature specified in the figurine's description below. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The creature is Friendly to you and your allies. It understands your languages, obeys your commands, and takes its turn immediately after you on your Initiative count. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but takes no other actions.

The creature exists for a duration specific to each figurine. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if its creature form drops to 0 Hit Points or if you take a Magic action while touching the creature to make it revert to figurine form. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified below.

Obsidian Steed (Very Rare). This polished obsidian horse can become a Nightmare for up to 24 hours. The nightmare fights only to defend itself. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 5 days have passed.

The figurine has a 10 percent chance each time you use it to ignore your orders, including a command to revert to figurine form. If you mount the nightmare while it is ignoring your orders, you and the nightmare are instantly transported to a random location on the plane of Hades, where the nightmare reverts to figurine form.

Name: Figurine of Wondrous Power, Onyx Dog
Text:
A Figurine of Wondrous Power is a statuette small enough to fit in a pocket. If you take a Magic action to throw the figurine to a point on the ground within 60 feet of yourself, the figurine becomes a living creature specified in the figurine's description below. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The creature is Friendly to you and your allies. It understands your languages, obeys your commands, and takes its turn immediately after you on your Initiative count. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but takes no other actions.

The creature exists for a duration specific to each figurine. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if its creature form drops to 0 Hit Points or if you take a Magic action while touching the creature to make it revert to figurine form. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified below.

Onyx Dog (Rare). This onyx statuette of a dog can become a Mastiff for up to 6 hours. The mastiff has an Intelligence of 8 and can speak Common. It also has Blindsight with a range of 60 feet. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 7 days have passed.

Name: Figurine of Wondrous Power, Serpentine Owl
Text:
A Figurine of Wondrous Power is a statuette small enough to fit in a pocket. If you take a Magic action to throw the figurine to a point on the ground within 60 feet of yourself, the figurine becomes a living creature specified in the figurine's description below. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.

The creature is Friendly to you and your allies. It understands your languages, obeys your commands, and takes its turn immediately after you on your Initiative count. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but takes no other actions.

The creature exists for a duration specific to each figurine. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if its creature form drops to 0 Hit Points or if you take a Magic action while touching the creature to make it revert to figurine form. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified below.

Serpentine Owl (Rare). This serpentine statuette of an owl can become a Giant Owl for up to 8 hours. The owl can communicate telepathically with you at any range if you and it are on the same plane of existence. Once it has been used, it can't be used again until 2 days have passed.

Name: Figurine of Wondrous Power, Silver Raven
Text:
A Figurine of Wondrous Power is a small statuette that fits easily in a pocket. When you take a Magic action to throw it onto the ground within 60 feet, it transforms into the creature described below. If the intended space is occupied or too small, the transformation does not occur.

The creature is Friendly toward you and your allies. It understands your languages, follows your commands, and acts immediately after you on your Initiative count. If you give no commands, it only defends itself.

The creature remains for the duration listed for that figurine. It reverts early if reduced to 0 Hit Points or if you take a Magic action while touching it. After reverting, it cannot be used again until the listed time has passed.

Silver Raven (Uncommon). This silver raven statuette becomes a Raven for up to 12 hours. After use, it cannot be activated again for 2 days. While in raven form, the figurine allows you to cast Animal Messenger on it.

Name: Fine Clothes
Text: Fine Clothes are crafted from luxurious materials and decorated with high-quality tailoring. Certain social events or exclusive locations require attire of this standard.

Name: Fine Wine (bottle)
Text: A bottle of refined wine, valued for its flavor and quality.

Name: Fine gold chain set with a fire opal
Text: Art objects include a wide range of crafted valuables, such as golden idols, gemstone-studded jewelry, regal portraits, and ornate decorative pieces.

Name: Fire Opal
Text:
A vivid red gemstone with fiery coloration.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Flail
Text: Mastery: Sap. When you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

Name: Flame Tongue
Text: While wielding this enchanted weapon, you may use a Bonus Action and speak a command word to ignite the striking portion of the weapon in magical flames. These flames emit Bright Light in a 40-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 40 feet. While ablaze, the weapon deals an extra 2d6 Fire damage on a hit. The flames persist until you use a Bonus Action to speak the command word again or until you drop, stow, or sheathe the weapon.

Name: Flask
Text: A flask can hold up to 1 pint of liquid.

Name: Flour
Text: Trade goods like flour are often exchanged directly between merchants without the use of coinage.

Name: Flute
Text:
Ability: Charisma

Utilize: Perform a known melody (DC 10), or improvise a tune (DC 15).

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check that uses it. If you are proficient in a skill relevant to that check, you also gain Advantage.

Name: Folding Boat
Text:
This item appears as a wooden box measuring 12 inches by 6 inches by 6 inches. It weighs 4 pounds, floats, and can be opened to store items. It has three command words, each requiring a Magic action:

First Command Word. The box unfolds into a Rowboat.

Second Command Word. The box unfolds into a Keelboat.

Third Command Word. The vessel folds back into a box if no creatures are aboard. Objects too large to fit remain outside; objects that can fit are stored inside.

When transformed into a vessel, it weighs the same as a normal boat of that size, and any stored items remain inside. Statistics for the Rowboat and Keelboat appear in the Player's Handbook. If either vessel is reduced to 0 Hit Points, the Folding Boat is destroyed.

Name: Forgery Kit
Text:
Ability: Dexterity

Utilize: Reproduce 10 or fewer words of another person's handwriting (DC 15), or replicate a wax seal (DC 20).

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check that uses it. If you are proficient in a related skill, you also gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Frost Brand
Text:
When you hit with an attack using this enchanted weapon, the target suffers an additional 1d6 Cold damage. While holding the weapon, you also gain Resistance to Fire damage.

In frigid environments, the weapon emits Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for another 10 feet.

When you draw the weapon, you may extinguish all nonmagical flames within 30 feet. Once used, this effect cannot be used again for 1 hour.

Name: Galley
Damage: Speed: 4 mphCarrying Capacity: 150 tons cargoCrew 80, AC 15, HP 500, Damage Threshold 20
Text:
Speed. A ship sailing against a strong wind moves at half speed. With no wind, waterborne vessels cannot move under sail and must be rowed. Keelboats and Rowboats are used on rivers and lakes. When traveling downstream, add the current's speed (typically 3 mph). These vessels cannot be rowed against a strong current but can be pulled upstream by draft animals. A Rowboat weighs 100 pounds and can be carried.

Crew. Ships larger than Keelboats or Rowboats require skilled hirelings to operate. The minimum number of crew depends on the ship type.

Passengers. The listed capacity assumes hammocks for Small and Medium passengers. Private accommodations reduce capacity to one-fifth. A hammock berth typically costs 5 SP per day, while a small private cabin costs around 2 GP per day.

Damage Threshold. See the rules glossary.

Ship Repair. Repairs can be made while docked. Restoring 1 Hit Point requires 1 day and 20 GP in materials and labor. In well-equipped shipyards, both time and cost are halved.

Name: Gaming Set
Text:
If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check that uses it. If you are proficient in a related skill, you also gain Advantage on that check.

Multiple variations of this item exist, including:

Dice Set
Dragonchess Set
Playing Cards
Three-Dragon Ante Set

Name: Garnet
Text:
A gemstone that may appear in shades of red, brown, green, or violet.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Gauntlets of Ogre Power
Text: While wearing these gauntlets, your Strength score becomes 19. They provide no benefit if your Strength is already 19 or higher without them.

Name: Gem of Brightness
Text:
This prism contains 50 charges. While holding it, you may take a Magic action and speak one of three command words to activate its effects:

First Command Word. The gem emits Bright Light in a 30-foot radius and Dim Light for another 30 feet. This effect costs no charges and lasts until you use a Bonus Action to repeat the command or activate another function.

Second Command Word. By spending 1 charge, the gem fires a dazzling beam at a creature you can see within 60 feet. The target must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be Blinded for 1 minute. It repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

Third Command Word. By spending 5 charges, the gem releases a burst of intense light in a 30-foot Cone. Each creature in the area makes the same saving throw as the beam.

When all charges are spent, the gem becomes a nonmagical jewel worth 50 GP.

Name: Gem of Seeing
Text:
This gem holds 3 charges. As a Magic action, you may expend 1 charge to gain Truesight out to 120 feet for 10 minutes while looking through the gem.

The gem regains 1d3 expended charges each dawn.

Name: Giant Slayer
Text:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this enchanted weapon.

When you hit a Giant with it, the creature takes an additional 2d6 damage of the weapon’s type and must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or fall Prone.

Name: Gilded royal coach or funeral barge
Text: Art objects include a wide range of valuable crafted works, such as golden idols, gemstone-set jewelry, regal portraits, and ornate ceremonial items.

Name: Ginger
Text: Merchants often barter trade goods directly rather than relying on coin currency.

Name: Glaive
Text:
Heavy. You have Disadvantage on attack rolls with a Heavy weapon if it is a Melee weapon and your Strength score is below 13, or if it is a Ranged weapon and your Dexterity score is below 13.

Reach. This weapon increases your reach by 5 feet when you attack with it or make Opportunity Attacks.

Two-Handed. You must use both hands to make attacks with this weapon.

Mastery: Graze. If your attack roll misses a creature, you may still deal damage equal to the ability modifier used for the attack. This damage matches the weapon’s type and can only be increased by raising that ability modifier.

Name: Glamoured Studded Leather
Text: While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class. You may also use a Bonus Action to make the armor appear as ordinary clothing or another type of armor. You choose the appearance—color, style, and accessories—but the armor keeps its usual weight and bulk. The illusion lasts until you use this feature again or remove the armor.

Name: Glass Bottle
Text: A Glass Bottle can hold up to 10.5 pints of liquid.

Name: Glassblower's Tools
Text:
Ability: Intelligence

Utilize: Determine what a glass object was used for within the last 24 hours (DC 15).

Craft: Glass Bottle, Magnifying Glass, Spyglass, Vial.

Artisan's Tools are specialized sets used for crafting and trade work, each requiring its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check that uses it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Gloves of Missile Snaring
Text: While wearing these gloves, when you are hit by an attack made with a Ranged or Thrown weapon, you may use a Reaction—provided you have a free hand—to reduce the damage by 1d10 plus your Dexterity modifier. If this reduces the damage to 0, you can catch the projectile or weapon, assuming it is small enough to hold in that hand.

Name: Gloves of Swimming and Climbing
Text: While wearing these gloves, moving while climbing or swimming costs you no extra movement, and you gain a +5 bonus to Strength (Athletics) checks made for climbing or swimming.

Name: Gloves of Thievery
Text: These gloves become invisible when worn. While wearing them, you gain a +5 bonus to Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) checks.

Name: Goat
Text: Trade goods like livestock are often exchanged directly between merchants without the use of coin.

Name: Goggles of Night
Text: These darkened lenses grant you Darkvision out to 60 feet. If you already possess Darkvision, the goggles extend its range by an additional 60 feet.

Name: Gold
Text: Merchants frequently barter trade goods directly rather than relying on coin currency.

Name: Gold Dragon Scale Mail
Text:
Dragon Scale Mail is crafted from the scales of a specific type of dragon. Some dragons gift their shed scales, while others are harvested from slain dragons. Either way, this armor is highly prized.

While wearing it, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class, have Advantage on saving throws against Dragon breath weapons, and gain Resistance to Fire damage.

You may also use a Magic action to sense the distance and direction to the nearest gold dragon within 30 miles. This ability refreshes at dawn.

The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Gold birdcage with electrum filigree
Text: Art objects include a wide variety of crafted valuables, such as golden idols, gemstone-set jewelry, ornate paintings, and decorative luxury items.

Name: Gold bracelet
Text: Art objects encompass many forms of valuable craftsmanship, including goldwork, jeweled ornaments, fine artwork, and ceremonial pieces.

Name: Gold circlet set with four aquamarines
Text: Art objects encompass many forms of valuable craftsmanship, including golden idols, gemstone-set jewelry, regal artwork, and ornate decorative pieces.

Name: Gold comb shaped like a dragon with red garnets as eyes
Text: Art objects include a wide range of luxury creations, such as goldwork, gem-inlaid ornaments, royal portraits, and ceremonial items.

Name: Gold cup set with emeralds
Text: Art objects span many valuable crafted works, from golden idols to jewel-studded ornaments, fine paintings, and richly decorated vessels.

Name: Gold idol
Text: Art objects include finely crafted valuables such as gold statues, gemstone jewelry, ornate paintings, and other decorative treasures.

Name: Gold jewelry box with platinum filigree
Text: Art objects cover a broad range of luxurious creations, including goldwork, gem-set pieces, royal portraits, and elaborately decorated containers.

Name: Gold locket with a painted portrait inside
Text: Art objects include finely crafted valuables such as gold ornaments, gemstone-set jewelry, detailed paintings, and ceremonial items.

Name: Gold music box
Text: Art objects include many forms of valuable craftsmanship, such as solid-gold idols, gemstone-set jewelry, royal portraits, and ornate decorative pieces.

Name: Gold ring set with bloodstones
Text: Art objects encompass a wide variety of luxury creations, including goldwork, gem-inlaid ornaments, fine paintings, and ceremonial items.

Name: Gold statuette set with rubies
Text: Art objects range from golden idols to jewel-studded ornaments, elaborate artwork, and richly decorated ceremonial pieces.

Name: Grappling Hook
Text: As a Utilize action, you may throw the Grappling Hook toward a railing, ledge, or similar anchor point within 50 feet. On a successful DC 13 Dexterity (Acrobatics) check, the hook catches. If a Rope is tied to it, you can climb the rope afterward.

Name: Greataxe
Text:
Heavy. You have Disadvantage on attack rolls with a Heavy weapon if it is a Melee weapon and your Strength score is below 13, or if it is a Ranged weapon and your Dexterity score is below 13.

Two-Handed. This weapon requires both hands to make attacks.

Mastery: Cleave. When you hit a creature with a melee attack using this weapon, you may make another melee attack with it against a second creature within 5 feet of the first and within your reach. On a hit, the second target takes the weapon’s damage, but you do not add your ability modifier unless it is negative. You may use this extra attack only once per turn.

Name: Greatclub
Text:
Two-Handed. You must use both hands to attack with this weapon.

Mastery: Push. When you hit a creature with this weapon, you may push it up to 10 feet directly away from you, provided it is Large or smaller.

Name: Greatsword
Text:
Heavy. You have Disadvantage on attack rolls with a Heavy weapon if it is a Melee weapon and your Strength score is under 13, or if it is a Ranged weapon and your Dexterity score is under 13.

Two-Handed. This weapon must be wielded with both hands.

Mastery: Graze. If your attack roll misses a creature, you may still deal damage equal to the ability modifier used for the attack. This damage is the same type as the weapon’s and can only be increased by raising that ability modifier.

Name: Green Dragon Scale Mail
Text:
Dragon Scale Mail is crafted from the scales of a specific dragon type. Some dragons gift their shed scales, while others are taken from slain dragons. In either case, this armor is highly prized.

While wearing it, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class, have Advantage on saving throws against Dragon breath weapons, and gain Resistance to Poison damage.

You may also use a Magic action to sense the distance and direction to the nearest green dragon within 30 miles. This ability refreshes at dawn.

The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Gunpowder (keg)
Text: Igniting a full keg of Gunpowder triggers an explosion. When it detonates, every creature within a 10-foot-radius Sphere centered on the keg must make a DC 12 Dexterity saving throw, taking 24 (7d6) Fire damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one.

Name: Gunpowder (powder horn)
Text: Setting fire to a powder horn filled with Gunpowder causes it to explode. When it detonates, each creature in a 10-foot-radius Sphere centered on the horn must make a DC 12 Dexterity saving throw, taking 10 (3d6) Fire damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one.

Name: Hag Eye
Text:
A Hag Eye contains 3 charges. While holding or wearing it, you may expend 1 charge to cast Darkvision (self only) or See Invisibility. All charges are restored at dawn.

Coven Sensor. A hag belonging to the coven that created the Hag Eye may use a Magic action to see through it, provided both are on the same plane. This persists as long as the hag maintains Concentration, and multiple hags in the coven may view through it simultaneously.

Creating a Hag Eye. Only a hag coven can craft this item, using a real eye coated in varnish and often set into a pendant or similar adornment. A coven may possess only one Hag Eye at a time. Creating a new one requires all three hags to perform a special 1-hour rite, which fails if any hag is Incapacitated or if they take any other actions during the ritual.

Name: Halberd
Text:
Heavy. You have Disadvantage on attack rolls with a Heavy weapon if it is a Melee weapon and your Strength score is below 13, or if it is a Ranged weapon and your Dexterity score is below 13.

Reach. This weapon increases your reach by 5 feet when attacking or making Opportunity Attacks.

Two-Handed. You must use both hands to attack with this weapon.

Mastery: Cleave. When you hit a creature with a melee attack using this weapon, you may make another melee attack against a second creature within 5 feet of the first and within your reach. On a hit, the second creature takes the weapon’s damage, but you do not add your ability modifier unless it is negative. You may use this extra attack only once per turn.

Name: Half Plate Armor
Text: The wearer suffers Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks while using this armor.

Name: Hammer of Thunderbolts
Text:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this enchanted hammer.

The hammer holds 5 charges. By expending 1 charge, you may make a ranged attack with it as though it had the Thrown property, with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet.

On a hit, the hammer releases a thunderous blast audible out to 300 feet. The target and all other creatures within 30 feet of it (except you) must succeed on a DC 17 Constitution saving throw or be Stunned until the end of your next turn. After the attack—hit or miss—the hammer immediately returns to your hand. It regains 1d4 + 1 expended charges at dawn.

Giant's Bane. While attuned to the hammer and also attuned to either a Belt of Giant Strength or Gauntlets of Ogre Power, you gain the following benefits:

Giants' Bane. When you roll a natural 20 on an attack roll with this hammer against a Giant, the creature must succeed on a DC 17 Constitution saving throw or die instantly.

Might of Giants. The Strength score granted by your Belt of Giant Strength or Gauntlets of Ogre Power increases by 4, up to a maximum of 30.

Name: Hand Crossbow
Text:
Range. A ranged weapon lists two distances in parentheses after the Ammunition or Thrown property. The first is the normal range; the second is the long range. Attacks beyond normal range are made with Disadvantage, and you cannot attack beyond long range.

Ammunition. You may make a ranged attack with this weapon only if you have the proper ammunition. Each attack consumes one piece of ammunition. Drawing ammunition is part of the attack, and loading a one-handed weapon requires a free hand. After combat, you may spend 1 minute to recover half (rounded down) of the ammunition you used.

Light. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light weapon, you may make one additional attack as a Bonus Action with a different Light weapon. You do not add your ability modifier to the damage of that Bonus Action attack unless the modifier is negative.

Loading. Regardless of how many attacks you can normally make, you may fire only one piece of ammunition from a Loading weapon when you use an action, Bonus Action, or Reaction to fire it.

Mastery: Vex. When you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage, you gain Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

Name: Hand of Vekna
Text:
Vekna was once a formidable archmage who built a fearsome dominion through sorcery and conquest. Terrified of death, he transformed himself into a lich to escape mortality.

His reign ended when his treacherous lieutenant, Kass, turned against him in a devastating battle. Only Vekna’s left hand and eye survived—macabre Artifacts that continue to carry out his dark intentions.

The Eye of Vekna and the Hand of Vekna are separate Artifacts that may appear together or apart. The eye resembles a torn, bloodshot organ, while the hand is a withered left appendage.

Random Properties of the Eye and Hand. The Hand of Vekna possesses the following random properties:

1 minor beneficial property
1 major beneficial property
1 minor detrimental property

Attuning to the Hand. To attune, you must press the hand against the stump of your missing left hand. It fuses to your arm and functions as a living limb. If it is ever removed, you die instantly.

Properties of the Hand. While attuned, your alignment becomes Neutral Evil, and you gain the following benefits:

Great Strength. Your Strength becomes 20 unless it is already 20 or higher.

Icy Touch. Any melee spell attack you make with the hand, or any melee attack made with a weapon held in it, deals an additional 2d8 Cold damage on a hit.

Spellcasting. The hand contains 8 charges and regains 1d4 + 4 charges at dawn. You may cast spells from the Hand of Vekna Spells table (save DC 18) by expending the listed number of charges. Each time you cast a spell this way, the hand casts Suggestion on you (save DC 18; no Concentration), urging you to commit an evil deed.

Hand of Vekna Spells:
Finger of Death — 5 charges
Sleep — 1 charge
Slow — 2 charges
Teleport — 3 charges

Destroying the Eye and Hand. If a creature bearing both the Eye and Hand of Vekna is slain by the Sword of Kass, both Artifacts erupt in flame, crumble to ash, and are destroyed. Any other attempt appears successful but merely causes the Artifact to reform in one of Vekna’s hidden vaults, awaiting rediscovery.

Name: Handaxe
Text:
Light. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light weapon, you may make one additional attack as a Bonus Action with a different Light weapon. You do not add your ability modifier to the damage of that Bonus Action attack unless the modifier is negative.

Thrown. A weapon with the Thrown property can be thrown to make a ranged attack, and drawing it is part of the attack. If it is a Melee weapon, use the same ability modifier you would use for melee attacks.

Mastery: Vex. When you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage, you gain Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before your next turn ends.

Name: Handheld mirror set in a painted wooden frame
Text: Art objects include many forms of valuable craftsmanship, such as gold idols, gemstone jewelry, royal portraits, and ornate decorative items.

Name: Hat of Disguise
Text: While wearing this hat, you may cast Disguise Self. The illusion ends immediately if the hat is removed.

Name: Hat of Many Spells
Text:
This pointed hat grants the following magical features.

Spellcasting Focus. While holding the hat, you may use it as a Spellcasting Focus for Wizard spells. Any spell cast through it requires a special Somatic gesture: reaching into the hat as though pulling the spell from within.

Unknown Spell. While holding the hat, you may attempt to cast a level 1+ Wizard spell you do not know. The spell must be on the Wizard list, be of a level you can cast, and cannot require Material components worth more than 1000 GP. You expend a spell slot of the chosen spell's level, then make an Intelligence (Arcana) check (DC 10 + the spell's level). On success, you cast the spell normally and cannot use this feature again until a Short or Long Rest. On failure, the spell fizzles and a random effect occurs, determined by the table below.

Any spell cast from the hat uses your spell save DC and spell attack bonus.

1d100 Effect
01–50 You cast a random spell determined by rolling 1d10: 1—Enlarge/Reduce (enlarge); 2—Enlarge/Reduce (reduce); 3—Faerie Fire; 4—Fireball; 5—Gust of Wind; 6—Invisibility (self only); 7—Lightning Bolt; 8—Phantasmal Force; 9—Polymorph; 10—Stinking Cloud.
51–55 You are Stunned until the end of your next turn, convinced something amazing just happened.
56–60 A harmless swarm of butterflies fills a 10-foot Cube within 30 feet and disperses after 1 minute.
61–65 You pull a mundane item from the hat. Roll 1d4: 1—vial of Acid; 2—flask of Alchemist's Fire; 3—Crowbar; 4—lit Torch.
66–70 You suffer “magic sickness” and are Poisoned for 1 hour.
71–75 You become Petrified until the end of your next turn.
76–80 You pull a mundane item from the hat. Roll 1d4: 1—Dagger; 2—Rope with Grappling Hook; 3—bag of Caltrops; 4—50-GP gem.
81–85 A creature appears in an unoccupied space nearest you. It is not under your control and behaves normally. It vanishes after 1 hour or at 0 HP. Roll 1d4: 1—Camel; 2—Constrictor Snake; 3—Elephant; 4—Mule.
86–90 A Hostile Swarm of Bats bursts from the hat, occupies your space, and attacks you.
91–95 A 10-foot-diameter two-way portal to another plane opens within 30 feet and remains until the end of your next turn. The DM chooses the destination.
96–00 You pull a magic item from the hat. Roll 1d6 to determine rarity: 1–3 Common; 4–5 Uncommon; 6 Rare. The item disappears after 1 hour unless consumed or destroyed.

Name: Hat of Vermin
Text: This hat contains 3 charges. While holding it, you may take a Magic action to expend 1 charge and summon a Bat, Frog, or Rat. The creature appears inside the hat and immediately attempts to flee. It is Indifferent toward all creatures, not under your control, behaves as a normal creature of its type, and disappears after 1 hour or when reduced to 0 HP. All charges are restored at dawn.

Name: Hat of Wizardry
Text:
This star-and-moon-decorated hat grants the following benefits.

Spellcasting Focus. You may use the hat as a Spellcasting Focus for Wizard spells.

Unknown Spell. As a Magic action, you may attempt to cast a Wizard cantrip you do not know, provided it has a casting time of 1 action. Make a DC 10 Intelligence (Arcana) check. On success, you cast the cantrip. On failure, the spell fizzles and the action is wasted. You cannot use this feature again until you finish a Long Rest.

Name: Headband of Intellect
Text: While wearing this headband, your Intelligence score becomes 19. The item has no effect if your Intelligence is already 19 or higher.

Name: Healer's Kit
Text: A Healer's Kit contains ten uses. As a Utilize action, you may expend one use to stabilize an Unconscious creature at 0 Hit Points without needing to make a Wisdom (Medicine) check.

Name: Heavy Crossbow
Properties: Ammunition (Range 100/400 ft.; Bolt), Heavy, Loading, Two-Handed
Text:
Range. A ranged weapon lists two distances: the first is normal range, the second is long range. Attacks beyond normal range are made with Disadvantage, and you cannot attack beyond long range.

Ammunition. You may fire this weapon only if you have the correct ammunition. Each attack consumes one piece of ammunition. Drawing ammunition is part of the attack, and loading a one-handed weapon requires a free hand. After combat, you may spend 1 minute to recover half (rounded down) of the ammunition used.

Heavy. You have Disadvantage on attack rolls with a Heavy weapon if it is a Melee weapon and your Strength is below 13, or if it is a Ranged weapon and your Dexterity is below 13.

Loading. Regardless of how many attacks you can normally make, you may fire only one piece of ammunition when using an action, Bonus Action, or Reaction.

Two-Handed. This weapon requires both hands to attack.

Mastery: Push. When you hit a creature with this weapon, you may push it up to 10 feet directly away from you if it is Large or smaller.

Name: Helm of Brilliance
Text:
This helm is adorned with 1d10 diamonds, 2d10 rubies, 3d10 fire opals, and 4d10 opals. Any gem removed from the helm disintegrates. When all gems are gone, the helm loses its magic.

Diamond Light. If at least one diamond remains, the helm emits a 30-foot Emanation. When an Undead is within that area, it becomes Dim Light, and any Undead starting its turn there takes 1d6 Radiant damage.

Fire Opal Flames. If at least one fire opal remains, you may take a Magic action to ignite a weapon you hold. The flames shed Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for another 10 feet. They do not harm you or the weapon. On a hit, the attack deals an extra 1d6 Fire damage. The flames last until you use a Bonus Action to extinguish them or drop/stow the weapon.

Ruby Resistance. If at least one ruby remains, you gain Resistance to Fire damage.

Spells. You may cast one of the following spells (save DC 18), consuming a gem of the listed type: Daylight (opal), Fireball (fire opal), Prismatic Spray (diamond), Wall of Fire (ruby). The gem is destroyed when used.

Taking Fire Damage. If you fail a saving throw against a spell that deals Fire damage while wearing the helm, roll 1d20. On a 1, the helm releases beams of light from its remaining gems and is destroyed. Each creature within a 60-foot Emanation must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw or take Radiant damage equal to the number of gems remaining.

Name: Helm of Comprehending Languages
Text: While wearing this helm, you may cast Comprehend Languages from it.

Name: Helm of Telepathy
Text: While wearing this helm, you gain telepathy out to 30 feet and may cast Detect Thoughts or Suggestion (save DC 13) from it. Once either spell is cast, it cannot be cast again from the helm until the next dawn.

Name: Helm of Teleportation
