Text: This helm contains 3 charges. While wearing it, you may expend 1 charge to cast Teleport from the helm. It regains 1d3 expended charges each dawn.

Name: Hematite
Text: A dark gray-black stone. Gemstones are compact, light, and easier to transport than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Herbalism Kit
Text:
Ability: Intelligence

Utilize: Identify a plant (DC 10)

Craft: Antitoxin, Candle, Healer's Kit, Potion of Healing

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to checks using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Handy Haversack
Text:
This backpack contains a central compartment and two side pouches, each functioning as an extradimensional space. Each side pouch can hold up to 200 pounds of items within 25 cubic feet, while the central pouch can hold up to 500 pounds within 64 cubic feet. The haversack always weighs 5 pounds regardless of contents.

Retrieving an item requires a Utilize action or a Bonus Action. When reaching inside for a specific object, it is always magically positioned on top.

If any pouch is overloaded, punctured, or torn, the haversack ruptures and is destroyed. Its contents are lost forever, except for Artifacts, which eventually reappear elsewhere. Turning the haversack inside out spills its contents harmlessly; it must be righted before further use.

Each pouch contains enough air for 10 minutes of breathing, divided among any creatures inside.

Placing the haversack inside an extradimensional space created by a Bag of Holding, Portable Hole, or similar item destroys both items and opens a gate to the Astral Plane. The gate appears where the interaction occurred, pulls in creatures within 10 feet lacking Total Cover, deposits them at a random Astral location, and then closes permanently.

Name: Handy Spice Pouch
Text: This belt pouch looks empty but contains 10 charges. While holding it, you may take a Magic action to expend 1 charge, name any nonmagical seasoning (such as salt, pepper, saffron, or cilantro), and draw a pinch of that spice from the pouch. One pinch seasons a single meal. The pouch regains 1d6 + 4 charges at dawn.

Name: Hide Armor
Text: Armor made from thick animal hide, offering modest protection while remaining flexible enough for travel and combat.

Name: Holy Avenger
Text:
You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls with this enchanted weapon. When you strike a Fiend or Undead, the creature takes an additional 2d10 Radiant damage.

While holding the drawn weapon, it radiates a 10-foot Emanation centered on you. You and Friendly creatures within the area gain Advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects. If you have 17 or more Paladin levels, the Emanation expands to 30 feet.

Name: Holy Symbol
Text:
A Holy Symbol is crafted in a distinctive form and decorated to channel divine power. Clerics and Paladins may use it as a Spellcasting Focus.

Common forms include:

Amulet
Emblem
Reliquary

Name: Holy Water
Text: When taking the Attack action, you may replace one attack by throwing a flask of Holy Water. Choose a creature you can see within 20 feet. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 + your Dexterity modifier + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, a Fiend or Undead takes 2d8 Radiant damage.

Name: Hooded Lantern
Text: A Hooded Lantern burns Oil to produce Bright Light in a 30-foot radius and Dim Light for another 30 feet. As a Bonus Action, you may lower the hood to reduce the light to Dim Light in a 5-foot radius, or raise it again.

Name: Horn
Text:
Ability: Charisma

Utilize: Perform a known tune (DC 10) or improvise a melody (DC 15)

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any check using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Horn of Blasting
Text:
You may take a Magic action to blow the horn, releasing a thunderous blast in a 30-foot Cone audible out to 600 feet. Each creature in the Cone must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes 5d8 Thunder damage and is Deafened for 1 minute; on a successful save, it takes half damage only. Glass or crystal objects in the Cone not worn or carried take 10d8 Thunder damage.

Each use has a 20 percent chance to make the horn explode, dealing 10d6 Force damage to you and destroying the horn.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any check using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Horn of Silent Alarm
Text:
This horn holds 4 charges and regains 1d4 charges at dawn. As a Magic action, you may blow the horn while expending 1 charge. One creature of your choice within 600 feet hears the horn’s call; no other creature hears anything.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any check using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Horn of Valhalla
Text:
You may take a Magic action to blow this horn, calling forth warrior spirits from the plane of Ysgard. They appear in unoccupied spaces within 60 feet of you, use the Berserker stat block, and vanish after 1 hour or when reduced to 0 Hit Points. They resemble living warriors and are Immune to the Charmed and Frightened conditions. Once used, the horn cannot be used again for 7 days.

Four variants of this horn exist, each crafted from a different metal. The horn’s type determines how many spirits appear and what requirement must be met to use it. The DM may choose the type or roll on the table below. If you blow the horn without meeting its requirement, the spirits attack you. If you meet it, they are Friendly to you and follow your commands.

1d100  Horn Type   Spirits   Requirement
01–40  Silver       2        None
41–75  Brass        3        Proficiency with all Simple weapons
76–90  Bronze       4        Training with all Armor
91–00  Iron         5        Proficiency with all Martial weapons

Name: Horn of Valhalla, Brass
Text:
You may take a Magic action to blow this horn, summoning warrior spirits from Ysgard into unoccupied spaces within 60 feet. Each spirit uses the Berserker stat block, appears as a living warrior, is Immune to the Charmed and Frightened conditions, and disappears after 1 hour or at 0 Hit Points. The horn cannot be used again for 7 days.

A brass horn summons 3 Berserkers. To use it, you must have Proficiency with all Simple weapons. If you do not meet this requirement, the spirits attack you; if you do, they are Friendly and obey your commands.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to checks using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Horn of Valhalla, Bronze
Text:
You may take a Magic action to blow this horn, calling forth warrior spirits from Ysgard into unoccupied spaces within 60 feet. Each spirit uses the Berserker stat block, appears as a living warrior, is Immune to the Charmed and Frightened conditions, and disappears after 1 hour or at 0 Hit Points. The horn cannot be used again for 7 days.

A bronze horn summons 4 Berserkers. To use it, you must have training with all Armor. If you do not meet this requirement, the spirits attack you; if you do, they are Friendly and follow your commands.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to checks using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Horn of Valhalla, Iron
Text:
You may take a Magic action to blow this horn, summoning warrior spirits from Ysgard into unoccupied spaces within 60 feet. Each spirit uses the Berserker stat block, appears as a living warrior, is Immune to the Charmed and Frightened conditions, and disappears after 1 hour or when reduced to 0 Hit Points. Once used, the horn cannot be used again for 7 days.

An iron horn summons 5 Berserkers. To use it, you must have Proficiency with all Martial weapons.

If you do not meet this requirement, the summoned Berserkers attack you. If you do meet it, they are Friendly to you and your allies and follow your commands.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any check using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Horn of Valhalla, Silver
Text:
You may take a Magic action to blow this horn, calling forth warrior spirits from Ysgard into unoccupied spaces within 60 feet. Each spirit uses the Berserker stat block, appears as a living warrior, is Immune to the Charmed and Frightened conditions, and disappears after 1 hour or when reduced to 0 Hit Points. Once used, the horn cannot be used again for 7 days.

A silver horn summons 2 Berserkers. They are Friendly to you and your allies and follow your commands.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any check using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Horseshoes of Speed
Text:
This set contains four magical horseshoes. As a Magic action, you may touch one horseshoe to the hoof of a horse or similar creature, causing it to attach itself. Removing a horseshoe also requires a Magic action.

When all four horseshoes are affixed to the same creature, that creature’s Speed increases by 30 feet.

Name: Horseshoes of a Zephyr
Text:
This set includes four enchanted horseshoes. As a Magic action, you may touch a horseshoe to the hoof of a horse or similar creature, causing it to attach itself. Removing one also requires a Magic action.

When all four shoes are affixed to the same creature, it moves normally while hovering 4 inches above any surface. This allows it to cross unstable or nonsolid terrain—such as water or lava—without sinking. The creature leaves no tracks, ignores Difficult Terrain, and can travel up to 12 hours per day without gaining Exhaustion from long-distance travel.

Name: Hunting Trap
Text: As a Utilize action, you may set this trap—a steel ring with serrated jaws that snaps shut when a creature steps on its pressure plate. The trap is secured by a heavy chain to a fixed object such as a tree or ground spike. A creature stepping on the plate must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or take 1d4 Piercing damage and have its Speed reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn. Afterward, its movement is restricted by the chain’s length (typically 3 feet) until it escapes. A creature may use its action to attempt a DC 13 Strength (Athletics) check to free itself or another creature; each failed attempt deals 1 Piercing damage to the trapped creature.

Name: Immovable Rod
Text: This iron rod features a button on one end. As a Utilize action, you may press the button to cause the rod to become magically fixed in place. It remains immobile—even defying gravity—until another creature uses a Utilize action to press the button again. The rod can support up to 8000 pounds; exceeding this weight causes it to deactivate and fall. A creature may take a Utilize action to attempt a DC 30 Strength (Athletics) check, moving the fixed rod up to 10 feet on a success.

Name: Ink
Text: This 1-ounce bottle of ink contains enough fluid to write approximately 500 pages.

Name: Ink Pen
Text: An Ink Pen is used for writing or drawing when paired with a supply of Ink.

Name: Instrument of Illusions
Text: While playing this instrument, you may take a Magic action to create harmless visual illusions within a 5-foot Emanation centered on it. If you are a Bard, the Emanation expands to 15 feet. Examples include glowing notes, a spectral dancer, drifting snow, or fluttering butterflies. These illusions have no sound or substance and are clearly unreal. They end when you stop playing.

Name: Instrument of Scribing
Text: This instrument holds 3 charges and regains all charges at dawn. While playing it, you may take a Magic action to expend 1 charge and inscribe a magical message on a nonmagical surface within 30 feet. The message may contain up to six words in a language you know. If you are a Bard, you may add seven more words and make the message faintly glow, visible even in nonmagical Darkness. Dispel Magic removes the message; otherwise, it fades after 24 hours.

Name: Instrument of the Bards
Text:
These exceptional instruments are linked to ancient bardic traditions. Seven variants exist, each named after a bardic college. A creature playing one without attunement must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or take 2d4 Psychic damage.

You may use the instrument to cast one of its spells. After casting a spell through the instrument, that spell cannot be cast again until the next dawn. All spells use your spellcasting ability and spell save DC.

All Instruments of the Bards can cast: Fly, Invisibility, Levitate, Protection from Evil and Good.

Known variants include:
Instrument of the Bards, Anstrun Harp
Instrument of the Bards, Canaithh Mandolin
Instrument of the Bards, Cly Lyre
Instrument of the Bards, Dossar Lute
Instrument of the Bards, Fochluran Bandore
Instrument of the Bards, Mac-Fuirminh Cittern
Instrument of the Bards, Ollam Harp

Name: Instrument of the Bards, Anstrun Harp
Text:
This harp is one of the famed Instruments of the Bards. A creature attempting to play it without attunement must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or take 2d4 Psychic damage.

You may play the Anstrun Harp to cast any of the following spells: Fly, Invisibility, Levitate, Protection from Evil and Good, Cure Wounds (level 5), Ice Storm, and Wall of Thorns. Once the harp casts a spell, it cannot cast that spell again until the next dawn. All spells use your spellcasting ability and spell save DC.

Name: Instrument of the Bards, Canaithh Mandolin
Text:
This mandolin is one of the Instruments of the Bards. A creature playing it without attunement must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or take 2d4 Psychic damage.

You may play the Canaithh Mandolin to cast one of the following spells: Fly, Invisibility, Levitate, Protection from Evil and Good, Cure Wounds (level 3), Dispel Magic, and Protection from Energy (Lightning only). After casting a spell through the mandolin, that spell cannot be cast again until the next dawn. All spells use your spellcasting ability and spell save DC.

Name: Instrument of the Bards, Cly Lyre
Text:
This lyre is one of the Instruments of the Bards. A creature attempting to play it without attunement must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or take 2d4 Psychic damage.

You may play the Cly Lyre to cast one of the following spells: Fly, Invisibility, Levitate, Protection from Evil and Good, Stone Shape, Wall of Fire, and Wind Wall. After casting a spell through the lyre, that spell cannot be cast again until the next dawn. All spells use your spellcasting ability and spell save DC.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any check using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Instrument of the Bards, Dossar Lute
Text:
This lute is one of the Instruments of the Bards, crafted far beyond the quality of ordinary instruments. Seven such instruments exist, each tied to a bardic college. A creature attempting to play it without attunement must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or take 2d4 Psychic damage.

You may play the Dossar Lute to cast one of the following spells: Fly, Invisibility, Levitate, Protection from Evil and Good, Animal Friendship, Protection from Energy (Fire only), and Protection from Poison. After casting a spell through the lute, that spell cannot be cast again until the next dawn. All spells use your spellcasting ability and spell save DC.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to checks using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Instrument of the Bards, Fochluran Bandore
Text:
This bandore is one of the famed Instruments of the Bards, superior to mundane instruments in every respect. Seven varieties exist, each named after a bardic college. A creature playing it without attunement must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or take 2d4 Psychic damage.

You may play the Fochluran Bandore to cast one of the following spells: Fly, Invisibility, Levitate, Protection from Evil and Good, Entangle, Faerie Fire, Shillelagh, and Speak with Animals. After casting a spell through the bandore, that spell cannot be cast again until the next dawn. All spells use your spellcasting ability and spell save DC.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to checks using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Instrument of the Bards, Mac-Fuirminh Cittern
Text:
This cittern is one of the Instruments of the Bards, far superior to ordinary instruments. Seven types exist, each tied to a bardic college. A creature attempting to play it without attunement must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or take 2d4 Psychic damage.

You may play the Mac-Fuirminh Cittern to cast one of the following spells: Fly, Invisibility, Levitate, Protection from Evil and Good, Barkskin, Cure Wounds, and Fog Cloud. After casting a spell through the cittern, that spell cannot be cast again until the next dawn. All spells use your spellcasting ability and spell save DC.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to checks using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Instrument of the Bards, Ollam Harp
Text:
This harp is one of the most powerful Instruments of the Bards, crafted to exceptional standards. Seven such instruments exist, each named after a bardic college. A creature attempting to play it without attunement must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or take 2d4 Psychic damage.

You may play the Ollam Harp to cast one of the following spells: Fly, Invisibility, Levitate, Protection from Evil and Good, Confusion, Control Weather, and Fire Storm. After casting a spell through the harp, that spell cannot be cast again until the next dawn. All spells use your spellcasting ability and spell save DC.

Name: Iuon Stone
Text:
About the size of a marble, a Iuon Stone is named after the deity of knowledge and foresight on certain worlds. Each type has its own distinct color and shape.

When you take a Magic action to toss a Iuon Stone into the air, it begins circling your head at a distance of 1d3 feet, granting its benefit while orbiting. You may have up to three Iuon Stones orbiting at once.

Each orbiting stone counts as a worn object. The stone instinctively avoids collisions, shifting its path to evade contact and preventing other creatures from grabbing or striking it.

As a Utilize action, you may grab and stow any number of orbiting stones. If your Attunement ends while a stone is orbiting, it drops as if released.

The stone’s type determines its rarity and effect.

Known variants include:
Iuon Stone, Absorption
Iuon Stone, Agility
Iuon Stone, Awareness
Iuon Stone, Fortitude
Iuon Stone, Greater Absorption
Iuon Stone, Insight
Iuon Stone, Intellect
Iuon Stone, Leadership
Iuon Stone, Mastery
Iuon Stone, Protection
Iuon Stone, Regeneration
Iuon Stone, Reserve
Iuon Stone, Strength
Iuon Stone, Sustenance

Name: Iuon Stone, Absorption
Text:
This pale lavender ellipsoid functions like all Iuon Stones: when tossed with a Magic action, it orbits your head at 1d3 feet, granting its benefit. Up to three stones may orbit at once, and each counts as a worn object. The stone avoids collisions and cannot be grabbed or struck by other creatures.

As a Utilize action, you may seize and store any orbiting stones. If Attunement ends while it orbits, it falls.

While this stone circles your head, you may use a Reaction to negate a spell of level 4 or lower cast by a creature you can see. The spell is canceled entirely, and its resources are lost. After the stone has negated a total of 20 spell levels, it burns out, turns dull gray, and loses its magic.

Name: Iuon Stone, Agility
Text:
This deep-red sphere behaves like all Iuon Stones: when thrown with a Magic action, it orbits your head at 1d3 feet, granting its effect. Up to three stones may orbit simultaneously, and each is treated as a worn object. The stone shifts its path to avoid collisions and cannot be seized by other creatures.

You may use a Utilize action to grab and store any orbiting stones. If Attunement ends while it orbits, it drops.

While this stone circles your head, your Dexterity increases by 2, to a maximum of 20.

Name: Iuon Stone, Awareness
Text:
This dark-blue rhomboid follows the standard behavior of all Iuon Stones: when tossed with a Magic action, it orbits your head at 1d3 feet, granting its benefit. You may have up to three stones orbiting at once, and each counts as a worn object. The stone instinctively avoids collisions and cannot be grabbed or struck by other creatures.

You may use a Utilize action to seize and stow any orbiting stones. If Attunement ends while it orbits, it falls.

While this stone circles your head, you gain Advantage on Initiative rolls and on Wisdom (Perception) checks.

Name: Iuon Stone, Fortitude
Text:
This pink rhomboid follows the standard behavior of all Iuon Stones: when tossed with a Magic action, it begins circling your head at a distance of 1d3 feet, granting its benefit. Up to three stones may orbit at once, and each counts as a worn object. The stone instinctively avoids collisions and cannot be grabbed or struck by other creatures.

You may use a Utilize action to seize and store any orbiting stones. If Attunement ends while it orbits, it falls.

While this stone circles your head, your Constitution increases by 2, to a maximum of 20.

Name: Iuon Stone, Greater Absorption
Text:
This marbled lavender-and-green ellipsoid behaves like all Iuon Stones: when thrown with a Magic action, it orbits your head at 1d3 feet, granting its effect. Up to three stones may orbit at once, and each is treated as a worn object. The stone shifts its path to avoid collisions and cannot be seized by other creatures.

You may use a Utilize action to grab and store any orbiting stones. If Attunement ends while it orbits, it drops.

While this stone circles your head, you may use a Reaction to negate a spell of level 8 or lower cast by a creature you can see. The spell is completely canceled, and its resources are lost. After the stone has negated 20 spell levels, it burns out, becomes dull gray, and loses its magic.

Name: Iuon Stone, Insight
Text:
This incandescent blue sphere functions like all Iuon Stones: when tossed with a Magic action, it begins orbiting your head at 1d3 feet, granting its benefit. Up to three stones may orbit at once, and each counts as a worn object. The stone instinctively avoids collisions and cannot be grabbed or struck by other creatures.

You may use a Utilize action to seize and store any orbiting stones. If Attunement ends while it orbits, it falls.

While this stone circles your head, your Wisdom increases by 2, to a maximum of 20.

Name: Iuon Stone, Intellect
Text:
This marbled scarlet-and-blue sphere behaves like all Iuon Stones: when tossed with a Magic action, it orbits your head at 1d3 feet, granting its effect. Up to three stones may orbit at once, and each is treated as a worn object. The stone shifts its path to avoid collisions and cannot be seized by other creatures.

You may use a Utilize action to grab and store any orbiting stones. If Attunement ends while it orbits, it drops.

While this stone circles your head, your Intelligence increases by 2, to a maximum of 20.

Name: Iuon Stone, Leadership
Text:
This marbled pink-and-green sphere functions like all Iuon Stones: when tossed with a Magic action, it begins orbiting your head at a distance of 1d3 feet, granting its benefit. Up to three stones may orbit at once, and each counts as a worn object. The stone instinctively avoids collisions and cannot be grabbed or struck by other creatures.

You may use a Utilize action to seize and store any orbiting stones. If Attunement ends while it orbits, it falls.

While this stone circles your head, your Charisma increases by 2, to a maximum of 20.

Name: Iuon Stone, Mastery
Text:
This pale green prism behaves like all Iuon Stones: when tossed with a Magic action, it begins orbiting your head at 1d3 feet, granting its benefit. Up to three stones may orbit at once, and each is treated as a worn object. The stone shifts its path to avoid collisions and cannot be seized by other creatures.

You may use a Utilize action to grab and store any orbiting stones. If Attunement ends while it orbits, it drops.

While this stone circles your head, your Proficiency Bonus increases by 1.

Name: Iuon Stone, Protection
Text:
This dusty-rose prism follows the standard behavior of all Iuon Stones: when tossed with a Magic action, it begins orbiting your head at 1d3 feet, granting its benefit. Up to three stones may orbit at once, and each counts as a worn object. The stone instinctively avoids collisions and cannot be grabbed or struck by other creatures.

You may use a Utilize action to seize and store any orbiting stones. If Attunement ends while it orbits, it falls.

While this stone circles your head, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.

Name: Iuon Stone, Regeneration
Text:
This pearly white spindle behaves like all Iuon Stones: when tossed with a Magic action, it begins orbiting your head at 1d3 feet, granting its benefit. Up to three stones may orbit at once, and each is treated as a worn object. The stone shifts its path to avoid collisions and cannot be seized by other creatures.

You may use a Utilize action to grab and store any orbiting stones. If Attunement ends while it orbits, it drops.

While this stone circles your head, you regain 15 Hit Points at the end of each hour, provided you have at least 1 Hit Point.

Name: Iuon Stone, Reserve
Text:
This vibrant purple prism behaves like all Iuon Stones: when tossed with a Magic action, it begins orbiting your head at 1d3 feet, granting its benefit. Up to three stones may orbit at once, and each counts as a worn object. The stone instinctively avoids collisions and cannot be grabbed or struck by other creatures.

You may use a Utilize action to seize and store any orbiting stones. If Attunement ends while it orbits, it falls.

This stone can store spells cast into it, holding up to 4 total spell levels. When found, it contains 1d4 levels of spells chosen by the DM.

Any creature may cast a spell of level 1–4 into the stone by touching it as the spell is cast. The spell has no effect except being stored. If the stone lacks capacity, the spell is wasted. The slot level used determines how much space it occupies.

While this stone circles your head, you may cast any spell stored within it. The spell uses the original caster’s slot level, save DC, attack bonus, and spellcasting ability. Once cast, the spell is removed from the stone, freeing space.

Name: Iuon Stone, Strength
Text:
This pale blue rhomboid follows the standard behavior of all Iuon Stones: when tossed with a Magic action, it begins orbiting your head at 1d3 feet, granting its benefit. Up to three stones may orbit at once, and each counts as a worn object. The stone instinctively avoids collisions and cannot be grabbed or struck by other creatures.

You may use a Utilize action to seize and store any orbiting stones. If Attunement ends while it orbits, it falls.

While this stone circles your head, your Strength increases by 2, to a maximum of 20.

Name: Iuon Stone, Sustenance
Text:
This clear spindle behaves like all Iuon Stones: when tossed with a Magic action, it begins orbiting your head at 1d3 feet, granting its benefit. Up to three stones may orbit at once, and each counts as a worn object. The stone instinctively avoids collisions and cannot be grabbed or struck by other creatures.

You may use a Utilize action to seize and store any orbiting stones. If Attunement ends while it orbits, it falls.

While this stone circles your head, you no longer need food or drink.

Name: Iron
Text: A common trade commodity often exchanged by merchants without the use of coinage.

Name: Iron Bands of Bilarron
Text:
This corroded iron sphere is 3 inches across and weighs 1 pound. You may take a Magic action to hurl it at a Huge or smaller creature you can see within 60 feet. As it travels, the sphere unfurls into a mass of metal bands.

Make a ranged attack roll using an attack bonus equal to your Dexterity modifier plus your Proficiency Bonus. On a hit, the target becomes Restrained until you use a Bonus Action to command the bands to release. Missing the attack or issuing the release command causes the bands to collapse back into a sphere.

Any creature touching the bands—including the Restrained one—may use an action to attempt a DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check to break them. On a success, the item is destroyed and the creature is freed. On a failure, that creature cannot attempt again for 24 hours.

Once used, the bands cannot be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Iron Flask
Text:
While holding this iron flask sealed with a brass stopper, you may take a Magic action to target a creature you can see within 60 feet. If the flask is empty and the target is native to another plane, it must succeed on a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw or be imprisoned inside. A creature previously trapped by this flask has Advantage on the save. A trapped creature remains contained until released and does not age, breathe, eat, or drink.

You may take a Magic action to unstopper the flask and release the creature. For 1 hour, it obeys your commands, understanding them even if it does not know your language. If you give no commands or issue one that would clearly lead to its death or capture, it acts only to defend itself. After the hour ends, it behaves according to its usual nature.

An Identify spell reveals whether the flask contains a creature, but only opening it reveals which one. A newly found Iron Flask may already contain a creature chosen by the DM or determined randomly.

1d100  Contents
01–50  No creature
51     Arcanaloth
52–54  Bone Devil

Name: Iron Pot
Text: A sturdy metal pot capable of holding up to 1 gallon of liquid or food.

Name: Iron Spike
Text: Iron spikes are sold in bundles of ten. As a Utilize action, you may drive a spike into wood, soil, or similar material using a blunt tool such as a Light Hammer. They can secure doors or serve as anchor points for Ropes or Chains.

Name: Iron Spikes
Text: Bundles of ten sturdy metal spikes. As a Utilize action, you may drive a spike into wood, soil, or similar material using a blunt tool such as a Light Hammer. They can secure doors or serve as anchor points for Ropes or Chains.

Name: Jacinth
Text:
A vivid orange gemstone with a fiery sheen.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to transport or conceal than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Jade
Text:
A gemstone found in pale green, deep green, or white varieties.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to transport or conceal than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Jade game board with gold playing pieces
Text:
A finely carved jade board accompanied by ornate gold pieces.

Art objects include valuable works such as sculptures, jewelry, paintings, and other crafted treasures.

Name: Jasper
Text:
A gemstone that may appear in blue, black, or brown hues.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to transport or conceal than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Javelin
Text:
Thrown. A weapon with the Thrown property may be thrown to make a ranged attack, and drawing it is part of that attack. If the weapon is a Melee weapon, use the same ability modifier for attack and damage rolls as you would for a melee strike.

Mastery: Slow. When you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage, you may reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. Multiple hits from Slow-property weapons do not stack beyond a 10-foot reduction.

Name: Javelin of Lightning
Text:
Whenever you hit with this magical javelin, you may choose for it to deal Lightning damage instead of Piercing damage.

Lightning Bolt. When you throw this weapon at a target within 120 feet, you may choose not to make a ranged attack roll. Instead, the javelin transforms into a bolt of lightning that travels in a 5-foot-wide Line between you and the target. The target and all other creatures in the Line (except you) must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw, taking 4d6 Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. After the effect resolves, the javelin instantly returns to your hand. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Thrown. A weapon with the Thrown property can be thrown to make a ranged attack, and drawing it is part of that attack. If the weapon is a Melee weapon, use the same ability modifier for attack and damage rolls as you would for a melee attack.

Mastery: Slow. When you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage, you may reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. Multiple hits from Slow-property weapons do not stack beyond a 10-foot reduction.

Name: Jet
Text:
A gemstone of deep, glossy black.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to transport or conceal than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Jeweled anklet
Text:
A decorative anklet adorned with precious stones.

Art objects include crafted valuables such as jewelry, sculptures, paintings, and other ornate works.

Name: Jeweled gold crown
Text:
A regal crown of solid gold set with glittering jewels.

Art objects include crafted valuables such as jewelry, sculptures, paintings, and other ornate works.

Name: Jeweled platinum ring
Text:
A platinum ring set with fine gemstones.

Art objects include crafted valuables such as jewelry, sculptures, paintings, and other ornate works.

Name: Jeweler's Tools
Text:
Ability: Intelligence

Utilize: Discern a gem's value (DC 15)

Craft: Arcane Focus, Holy Symbol

Artisan's Tools are specialized kits used to create items and practice a trade. Each tool type requires its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any check using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Jug
Text: A sturdy container capable of holding up to 1 gallon of liquid.

Name: Keelboat
Damage:
Speed: 1 mph
Carrying Capacity: 1/2 ton cargo, 6 passengers
Crew 1, AC 15, HP 100, Damage Threshold 10
Text:
Speed. Sailing against strong wind halves speed. With no wind, watercraft can't move under sail and must be rowed. Keelboats and Rowboats operate on lakes and rivers. When traveling downstream, add the current's speed (usually 3 mph). These vessels can't be rowed against strong currents but can be pulled upstream by animals on shore. A Rowboat weighs 100 pounds and can be carried.

Crew. Ships larger than Keelboats or Rowboats require skilled hirelings to operate. The minimum number depends on the ship type.

Passengers. The listed capacity assumes hammock accommodations for Small or Medium passengers. Private quarters reduce capacity to one-fifth. A hammock berth typically costs 5 SP per day; a small private cabin costs about 2 GP per day.

Damage Threshold. See the rules glossary.

Ship Repair. Repairs can be made while docked. Restoring 1 HP requires 1 day and 20 GP in materials and labor. In well-equipped shipyards, both time and cost are halved.

Name: Keoghtom's Ointment
Text:
This small glass jar, 3 inches wide, contains 1d4 + 1 doses of a thick aloe-scented salve. Jar and contents weigh 1/2 pound.

As a Utilize action, you may swallow a dose or apply it to a creature within 5 feet. The recipient regains 2d8 + 2 Hit Points and ends the Poisoned condition.

Name: Ladder
Text: A 10-foot wooden ladder requiring climbing to ascend or descend.

Name: Lamp
Text: A lamp burns Oil to shed Bright Light in a 15-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 30 feet.

Name: Lance
Text:
A Lance must be wielded with two hands unless you are mounted.

Heavy. You have Disadvantage on attack rolls with a Heavy weapon if it's a Melee weapon and your Strength score is below 13, or if it's a Ranged weapon and your Dexterity score is below 13.

Reach. This weapon increases your reach by 5 feet for attacks and Opportunity Attacks.

Two-Handed. You must use both hands to attack with this weapon.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + the ability modifier used for the attack + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failure, the creature becomes Prone.

Name: Lantern of Revealing
Text: When lit, this hooded lantern burns for 6 hours on 1 pint of oil, producing Bright Light in a 30-foot radius and Dim Light for another 30 feet. Invisible creatures and objects become visible while in its Bright Light. As a Utilize action, you may lower the hood, reducing illumination to Dim Light in a 5-foot radius.

Name: Lapis Lazuli
Text:
A gemstone of mixed light and dark blue tones with flecks of yellow.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Leather Armor
Text: Simple protective armor crafted from treated leather, offering basic defense without restricting movement.

Name: Leatherworker's Tools
Text:
Ability: Dexterity

Utilize: Add a decorative design to a leather item (DC 10)

Craft: Sling, Whip, Hide Armor, Leather Armor, Studded Leather Armor, Backpack, Crossbow Bolt Case, Map or Scroll Case, Parchment, Pouch, Quiver, Waterskin

Artisan's Tools are specialized kits for crafting and trade work, each requiring its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to checks using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Light Crossbow
Text:
Range. A ranged weapon lists two distances: the first is normal range, the second is long range. Attacks beyond normal range are made with Disadvantage, and you cannot attack beyond long range.

Ammunition. You may fire this weapon only if you have the proper ammunition. Each attack consumes one bolt. Drawing ammunition is part of the attack, and after combat you may recover half of the expended ammunition (rounded down) by spending 1 minute.

Loading. Regardless of how many attacks you can normally make, you may fire only one piece of ammunition when using an action, Bonus Action, or Reaction to fire this weapon.

Two-Handed. You must use both hands to attack with this weapon.

Mastery: Slow. When you hit a creature and deal damage, you may reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. Multiple hits from Slow-property weapons do not stack beyond a 10-foot reduction.

Name: Light Hammer
Text:
Light. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light weapon, you may make one extra attack as a Bonus Action with a different Light weapon. You do not add your ability modifier to the damage of that Bonus Action attack unless it is negative.

Thrown. You may throw this weapon to make a ranged attack, using the same ability modifier you would use for a melee attack with it.

Mastery: Nick. When you would make the extra attack granted by the Light property, you may instead make that extra attack as part of the Attack action. You may do this only once per turn.

Name: Linen (1 sq. yd.)
Text: A square yard of woven linen cloth, commonly traded as a basic textile good.

Name: Lock
Text: This lock includes a matching key. Without the key, a creature may attempt to pick the lock using Thieves' Tools with a successful DC 15 Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check.

Name: Lock of Trickery
Text: This magical lock resembles an ordinary lock and includes a single key. Its tumblers shift magically to foil intruders. Dexterity checks made to pick this lock are made with Disadvantage.

Name: Lolth's Sting
Text: A creature exposed to this poison must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or become Poisoned for 1 hour. If it fails the save by 5 or more, it also falls Unconscious while Poisoned. The creature awakens if it takes damage or if another creature uses an action to shake it awake.

Name: Longbow
Text:
Range. Ranged weapons list two distances: normal range and long range. Attacks beyond normal range are made with Disadvantage, and you cannot attack beyond long range.

Ammunition. You must have the correct ammunition to make a ranged attack with this weapon. Each attack consumes one arrow. Drawing ammunition is part of the attack, and after combat you may recover half of the expended ammunition (rounded down) by spending 1 minute.

Heavy. You have Disadvantage on attack rolls with a Heavy weapon if your Strength (for melee) or Dexterity (for ranged) is below 13.

Two-Handed. You must use both hands to attack with this weapon.

Mastery: Slow. When you hit a creature and deal damage, you may reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. Multiple Slow effects do not stack beyond a 10-foot reduction.

Name: Longship
Damage:
Speed: 3 mph
Carrying Capacity: 10 tons cargo, 150 passengers
Crew 40, AC 15, HP 300, Damage Threshold 15
Text:
Speed. Sailing against strong wind halves speed. With no wind, ships cannot move under sail and must be rowed. When traveling downstream, add the current's speed (typically 3 mph). These vessels cannot be rowed against strong currents but may be pulled upstream by animals on shore.

Crew. Ships larger than Keelboats or Rowboats require skilled hirelings to operate.

Passengers. Capacity assumes hammock accommodations for Small or Medium passengers. Private quarters reduce capacity to one-fifth. Hammock space typically costs 5 SP per day; a private cabin costs about 2 GP per day.

Damage Threshold. See the rules glossary.

Ship Repair. Repairs can be made while docked. Restoring 1 HP requires 1 day and 20 GP in materials and labor. In well-equipped shipyards, both time and cost are halved.

Name: Longsword
Text:
Versatile. This weapon may be used with one or two hands. When used with two hands, it deals the damage shown in parentheses.

Mastery: Sap. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

Name: Luck Blade
Text:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While you carry it, you also gain a +1 bonus to all saving throws.

Luck. While the blade is on your person, you may invoke its luck (no action required) to reroll one failed D20 Test, provided you aren't Incapacitated. You must use the second result. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Wish. The blade holds 1d3 charges. While holding it, you may expend 1 charge to cast Wish. This property can't be used again until the next dawn. If the blade has no remaining charges, it permanently loses this property.

Name: Lute
Text:
Ability: Charisma

Utilize: Perform a known melody (DC 10) or improvise a tune (DC 15)

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any check using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Lute of Thunderous Thumping
Text:
This reinforced lute functions as a magical Club and deals an extra 2d8 Thunder damage on a hit.

Sing and Swing. If you are a Bard, you may use your Charisma modifier instead of Strength for melee attack rolls made with this lute, provided you sing or hum while attacking.

Light. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light weapon, you may make one extra attack as a Bonus Action with a different Light weapon. You do not add your ability modifier to the damage of that Bonus Action attack unless it is negative.

Mastery: Slow. When you hit a creature and deal damage, you may reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. Multiple Slow effects do not stack beyond a 10-foot reduction.

Name: Lyre
Text:
Ability: Charisma

Utilize: Perform a familiar tune (DC 10) or improvise a melody (DC 15)

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any check using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Mace
Text: Mastery: Sap. When you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

Name: Mace of Disruption
Text:
When you hit a Fiend or Undead with this magic mace, it takes an extra 2d6 Radiant damage. If the target has 25 Hit Points or fewer after taking this damage, it must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be destroyed. On a successful save, it becomes Frightened until the end of your next turn.

Light. This weapon emits Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet.

Mastery: Sap. When you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

Name: Mace of Smiting
Text:
This magic mace grants a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls. When used against Constructs, the bonus increases to +3.

If you roll a 20 on an attack roll with this weapon, the target takes an extra 7 Bludgeoning damage, or 14 if it is a Construct. If a Construct has 25 Hit Points or fewer after taking this damage, it is destroyed.

Mastery: Sap. When you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

Name: Mace of Terror
Text:
This magical mace has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges at dawn. While holding it, you may take a Magic action and expend 1 charge to unleash a wave of terror. Each creature of your choice within 30 feet must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or become Frightened for 1 minute. While Frightened in this way, a creature must use its turns to move as far from you as possible and cannot make Opportunity Attacks. Its action is limited to Dash or attempting to escape movement-restricting effects; if unable to move, it may take the Dodge action. At the end of each of its turns, it repeats the saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

Mastery: Sap. When you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

Name: Magnifying Glass
Text: A magnifying lens that grants Advantage on checks made to closely examine or appraise intricate objects. It can also be used to start a fire if bright sunlight is available, along with tinder, requiring about 5 minutes of focus.

Name: Malachite
Text:
A banded gemstone of alternating light and dark green hues.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Malice
Text: A creature exposed to this poison must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or become Poisoned for 1 hour. While Poisoned in this way, the creature is also Blinded.

Name: Manacles
Text:
As a Utilize action, you may use these restraints to bind an unwilling Small or Medium creature within 5 feet that is Grappled, Incapacitated, or Restrained, succeeding on a DC 13 Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check. A bound creature has Disadvantage on attack rolls, and becomes Restrained if the manacles are secured to a fixed chain or hook.

Escaping requires a DC 20 Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check as an action. Breaking them requires a DC 25 Strength (Athletics) check as an action.

Each set includes a key. Without it, a creature may attempt to pick the lock with Thieves' Tools using a DC 15 Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check.

Name: Mantle of Spell Resistance
Text: While wearing this cloak, you have Advantage on all saving throws made against spells.

Name: Manual of Bodily Health
Text: This enchanted tome contains magical guidance on physical well-being. If you devote 48 hours within a 6-day period to studying and practicing its teachings, your Constitution score increases by 2, to a maximum of 30. Afterward, the book loses its magic for 100 years.

Name: Manual of Clay Golems
Text:
This manual contains the rituals and arcane knowledge required to craft a clay golem. Only a spellcaster with at least two 5th-level spell slots can use it. A creature unable to use the manual that attempts to read it suffers 6d6 Psychic damage.

To create a clay golem, you must work for 30 uninterrupted days with the manual at hand, resting no more than 8 hours per day, and spend 65000 gp on materials.

When the golem is completed, the book is consumed in eldritch fire. Sprinkling its ashes on the construct animates it, placing it under your command and able to understand and obey your spoken instructions.

Name: Manual of Flesh Golems
Text:
This manual contains the arcane procedures needed to craft a flesh golem. Only a spellcaster with at least two 5th-level spell slots can use it. A creature unable to use the manual that tries to read it takes 6d6 Psychic damage.

Creating a flesh golem requires 60 days of uninterrupted work with the manual, resting no more than 8 hours per day, and 50000gp in materials.

Once the golem is completed, the book is consumed in eldritch flames. Sprinkling its ashes on the construct animates it, placing it under your control and allowing it to understand and follow your spoken commands.

Name: Manual of Gainful Exercise
Text: This enchanted volume outlines magical strength-training techniques. If you devote 48 hours within a 6-day period to studying and practicing its methods, your Strength score increases by 2, to a maximum of 30. Afterward, the book loses its magic for 100 years.

Name: Manual of Golems
Text:
This tome contains the arcane procedures required to craft a specific type of golem. Only a spellcaster with at least two 5th-level spell slots can use it. A creature unable to use the manual that attempts to read it suffers 6d6 Psychic damage.

d20  Golem Type            Time        Cost
1–5  Manual of Clay Golems   30 days    65000 GP
6–17 Manual of Flesh Golems  60 days    50000GP
18   Manual of Iron Golems   120 days   100000GP
19–20 Manual of Stone Golems 90 days    80000GP

To create a golem, you must work for the listed duration without interruption, resting no more than 8 hours per day, and spend the required gold on materials.

When the golem is completed, the book is consumed in eldritch fire. Sprinkling its ashes on the construct animates it, placing it under your control and allowing it to understand and obey your spoken commands.

Name: Manual of Iron Golems
Text:
This manual details the rituals and magical knowledge needed to craft an iron golem. Only a spellcaster with at least two 5th-level spell slots can use it. A creature unable to use the manual that attempts to read it takes 6d6 Psychic damage.

Creating an iron golem requires 120 days of uninterrupted work with the manual at hand, resting no more than 8 hours per day, and 100000gp in materials.

Once the golem is finished, the book is consumed in eldritch flames. Sprinkling its ashes on the construct animates it, placing it under your command and enabling it to understand and obey your spoken instructions.

Name: Manual of Quickness of Action
Text: This magical book teaches exercises that enhance agility and coordination. If you spend 48 hours within a 6-day period studying and practicing its techniques, your Dexterity score increases by 2, to a maximum of 30. Afterward, the manual loses its magic for 100 years.

Name: Manual of Stone Golems
Text:
This tome contains the arcane lore and rituals needed to craft a stone golem. Only a spellcaster with at least two 5th-level spell slots can use it. A creature unable to use the manual that attempts to read it suffers 6d6 Psychic damage.

Crafting a stone golem requires 90 days of uninterrupted work with the manual at hand, resting no more than 8 hours per day, and 80000gp in materials.

When the golem is complete, the book is consumed in eldritch fire. Sprinkling its ashes on the construct animates it, placing it under your control and allowing it to understand and obey your spoken commands.

Name: Map
Text: Consulting an accurate map grants a +5 bonus to Wisdom (Survival) checks made to navigate the area it depicts.

Name: Map or Scroll Case
Text: A protective case capable of holding up to 10 sheets of paper or 5 sheets of parchment.

Name: Mariner's Armor
Text:
While wearing this armor, your Swim Speed equals your walking Speed. Additionally, if you begin your turn underwater at 0 Hit Points, you immediately regain 1d4 Hit Points. This healing can't occur again until the next dawn.

The armor features decorative motifs of shells and aquatic creatures.

Name: Mason's Tools
Text:
Ability: Strength

Utilize: Carve a symbol or hole into stone (DC 10)

Craft: Block and Tackle

Artisan's Tools are specialized kits for crafting and trade work, each requiring its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any check using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Mastiff
Text: A powerful and loyal canine mount capable of carrying a rider or gear across varied terrain.

Name: Maul
Text:
Heavy. You have Disadvantage on attack rolls with a Heavy weapon if it's a Melee weapon and your Strength score is below 13, or if it's a Ranged weapon and your Dexterity score is below 13.

Two-Handed. This weapon requires both hands to make an attack.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can force it to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + the ability modifier used for the attack + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature falls Prone.

Name: Medallion of Thoughts
Text: This medallion holds 5 charges. While wearing it, you may expend 1 charge to cast Detect Thoughts (save DC 13). The medallion regains 1d4 expended charges each dawn.

Name: Midnight Tears
Text: A creature that consumes this poison suffers no immediate effect. Any effect that ends the Poisoned condition neutralizes it. If not neutralized before midnight, the creature must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw, taking 31 (9d6) Poison damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

Name: Military Saddle
Text: This saddle includes all necessary riding gear such as bit, bridle, and reins. A Military Saddle grants Advantage on ability checks made to stay mounted.

Name: Mirror
Text: A small steel mirror useful for grooming, checking corners safely, or reflecting light as a signal.

Name: Mirror of Life Trapping
Text:
This 4-foot-tall, 2-foot-wide mirror shows faint images of creatures when viewed indirectly. It weighs 50 pounds and has AC 11, HP 10, Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage, and Vulnerability to Bludgeoning damage. It shatters at 0 HP.

If hung on a vertical surface and you are within 5 feet, you may take a Magic action and speak a command word to activate it. It remains active until you repeat the command word.

Any creature other than you that sees its reflection in the activated mirror within 30 feet must make a DC 15 Charisma saving throw or be trapped—along with its gear—in one of the mirror’s twelve extradimensional cells. A creature aware of the mirror’s nature has Advantage on the save, and Constructs automatically succeed.

Each cell is an endless fog-filled space with visibility limited to 10 feet. Trapped creatures do not age and do not need food, drink, or sleep. They may escape only via magic that allows planar travel.

If all twelve cells are full when a new creature is trapped, one imprisoned creature is released at random. Freed creatures appear in an unoccupied space facing away from the mirror. If the mirror is destroyed, all trapped creatures appear nearby.

While within 5 feet of the mirror, you may take a Magic action to name a trapped creature or specify a cell number, causing the creature to appear as an image on the mirror’s surface, allowing communication.

You may also take a Magic action and speak a second command word to release a trapped creature, which appears with its belongings in the nearest unoccupied space facing away from the mirror.

Placing the mirror inside an extradimensional space created by a Bag of Holding, Portable Hole, or similar item destroys both items and opens a one-way gate to the Astral Plane. Any creature within 10 feet not behind Total Cover is drawn through to a random location on the Astral Plane before the gate closes.

Name: Mithral Armor
Text:
Mithral is an exceptionally light and flexible metal. A mithral chain shirt or breastplate can be worn beneath ordinary clothing.

If the base armor normally imposes Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks or requires a minimum Strength score, the mithral version removes those penalties.

Name: Moon-Touched Sword
Text: When drawn in Darkness, this blade emits soft moonlight, shedding Bright Light in a 15-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 15 feet.

Name: Moonblade
Attunement: Requires Attunement By a Creature of the Weapon's Choice
Text:
Among all elven-crafted magic items, Moonblades are among the most treasured. In ages past, nearly every noble elven house possessed one. Many have since vanished—lost with their wielders or fading as bloodlines died out.

A Moonblade seeks a bearer whose nature aligns with its own. If you attempt to attune to a Moonblade that rejects you, it curses you, imposing Disadvantage on all D20 Tests for 24 hours or until the curse is ended by Remove Curse or similar magic. If accepted, attunement is instantaneous and a new rune appears on the blade. You remain attuned until death or the weapon’s destruction. To all others, the Moonblade functions as a mundane weapon of its type.

Each Moonblade bears one rune for every willing bearer it has served (typically 1d6 + 1). The first rune grants a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls. Each additional rune grants one property, chosen by the DM or determined randomly from the Moonblade Properties table.

Minor Property. Each Moonblade also has a minor magical trait determined by rolling on the Magic Item Minor Property table.

Sentience. A Moonblade is sentient, with Intelligence 12, Wisdom 10, and Charisma 12. It perceives through hearing and Darkvision out to 120 feet. Its alignment matches that of its creator.

The weapon communicates through emotional impulses, tingling sensations, and occasionally visions or dreams when the wielder sleeps or enters a trance.

Personality. A Moonblade’s personality reflects its creator. Once it chooses a bearer, it believes only that individual should wield it, even if their alignment or goals later diverge.

Moonblade Properties
1d100  Property
01–60  Increase the weapon’s bonus to attack and damage rolls by 1 (max +3). Reroll if already +3.
61–75  On a hit, deal +1d6 Force damage. Each additional time this property is gained, increase by +1d6 (max +3d6). Reroll if already at +3d6.
76–80  Gains the Thrown property (20/60 ft.) and returns to your hand after each throw.
81–85  Scores a Critical Hit on a 19 or 20.
86–95  As a Bonus Action, cause the blade to flare with light. Each creature within 30 feet not behind Total Cover must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution save or be Blinded for 1 minute. They repeat the save at the end of each turn. Recharge on a Short or Long Rest.
96–99  Gains the properties of a Ring of Spell Storing.
100    You may take a Magic action to summon a spectral elf-like entity if none is currently serving you. It appears within 120 feet, uses the Shadow stat block with modifications (Fey, Neutral, no shadow creation), and obeys your commands until reduced to 0 HP or dismissed.

Base items. This variant can be applied to:
• Greatsword (Moonblade Greatsword)
• Longsword (Moonblade Longsword)
• Rapier (Moonblade Rapier)
• Scimitar (Moonblade Scimitar)
• Shortsword (Moonblade Shortsword)

Name: Moonstone
Text:
A pale white gemstone with a faint blue luminescence.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Morningstar
Text: Mastery: Sap. When you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

Name: Moss Agate
Text:
A gemstone of pink or yellow-white coloration streaked with moss-like gray or green patterns.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Mule
Text: A hardy and dependable pack animal capable of carrying heavy loads over long distances.

Name: Musical Instrument
Text:
If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check that uses it. If a related skill is also one you are proficient in, you gain Advantage on that check.

Multiple forms of this item exist, including:

Bagpipes, Drum, Dulcimer, Flute, Horn, Lute, Lyre, Pan Flute, Shawm, Viol.

Name: Mystery Key
Text: This key bears a question mark engraved into its head. It has a 5 percent chance to unlock any lock it is inserted into. Once it successfully opens a lock, the key vanishes.

Name: Nature's Mantle
Attunement: Requires Attunement By a Druid or Ranger
Text:
This cloak shifts its appearance to match the surrounding terrain. While wearing it, you may use it as a Spellcasting Focus for Druid and Ranger spells.

While in an area that is Lightly Obscured, you may take the Hide action as a Bonus Action even if you are being directly observed.

Name: Navigator's Tools
Text:
Ability: Wisdom

Utilize: Chart a travel route (DC 10), or determine your position by observing the stars (DC 15)

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any check using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Necklace of Adaptation
Text: While wearing this necklace, you can breathe normally in any environment, and you have Advantage on saving throws made to avoid or end the Poisoned condition.

Name: Necklace of Fireballs
Text:
This necklace bears 1d6 + 3 beads. As a Magic action, you can detach a bead and throw it up to 60 feet. When it reaches its destination, it explodes as a level 3 Fireball (save DC 15).

You may throw multiple beads—or the entire necklace—at once. When doing so, increase the Fireball’s damage by 1d6 for each bead beyond the first (maximum 12d6).

Name: Necklace of Prayer Beads
Attunement: Requires Attunement By a Cleric, Druid, or Paladin
Text:
This necklace contains 1d4 + 2 magical beads crafted from aquamarine, black pearl, or topaz, along with numerous mundane beads of various stones. Removing a magical bead from the necklace causes it to lose its magic.

Six types of magical beads exist. The DM chooses each bead’s type or determines it randomly. A necklace may contain duplicates. To use a bead, you must be wearing the necklace. Each bead holds a spell you can cast from it as a Bonus Action (using your spell save DC if applicable). Once used, a bead can't be used again until the next dawn.

1d20  Bead Type        Spell
1–6   Bead of Blessing     Bless
7–12  Bead of Curing       Cure Wounds (level 2)
13–16 Bead of Favor        Greater Restoration
17–18 Bead of Smiting      Shining Smite
19    Bead of Summons      Guardian of Faith
20    Bead of Wind Walking Wind Walk

Name: Necklace string of small pink pearls
Text:
A delicate string of small pink pearls, valued for its craftsmanship and rarity.

Art objects include fine jewelry, ornate sculptures, paintings, and other luxurious creations.

Name: Needle
Text:
Blowgun needles are ammunition used with weapons that have the Ammunition property. Each attack consumes one needle. Drawing a needle from a container is part of the attack, though loading a one-handed weapon requires a free hand. After combat, you can recover half the expended ammunition by spending 1 minute searching the area.

Needles are usually kept in a separate Pouch.

Name: Needles (50)
Text:
Blowgun needles are ammunition used with weapons that have the Ammunition property. Each attack consumes one needle. Drawing a needle from a container is part of the attack, though loading a one-handed weapon requires a free hand. After combat, you can recover half the expended ammunition by spending 1 minute searching the area.

Needles are usually kept in a separate Pouch.

Name: Net
Text:
When taking the Attack action, you may replace one attack with a thrown Net targeting a creature within 15 feet. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 + your Dexterity modifier + your Proficiency Bonus) or become Restrained until it escapes. Huge or larger creatures automatically succeed.

A creature can escape by using an action to make a DC 10 Strength (Athletics) check. Destroying the Net (AC 10; 5 HP; immune to Bludgeoning, Poison, and Psychic damage) also frees the target.

Name: Nine Lives Stealer
Text:
This magic weapon grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

Life Stealing. The weapon contains 1d8 + 1 charges. When you attack a creature with fewer than 100 Hit Points and roll a 20 on the attack roll, the target must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or die instantly as its life force is torn away. Constructs and Undead automatically succeed. If the creature dies, the weapon expends 1 charge. When all charges are gone, this property is lost.

Name: Nolzur's Marvelous Pigments
Text:
This wooden box contains 1d4 pots of magical pigment and a brush (total weight 1 pound). By using the brush and expending one pot, you can paint any number of three-dimensional objects or terrain features within a 20-foot Cube. The process takes 10 minutes, requires Concentration, and you must remain inside the Cube. If Concentration ends or you leave the Cube early, the creations vanish and the pigment is wasted.

When completed, the painted objects become real. A painted door becomes an actual door; a painted pit becomes a real pit, provided its full depth fits within the Cube.

No created object can exceed a value of 25 GP, and the total value of all objects from one pot cannot exceed 500 GP. More valuable items appear convincing but are made of worthless material.

Painting energy forms such as fire or lightning causes them to dissipate harmlessly upon completion.

Name: Oathbow
Text:
When you place an arrow on this bowstring, it whispers in Elvish, "Swift defeat to my enemies." When making a ranged attack with this weapon, you may speak or sign the command phrase: "Swift death to you who have wronged me." The target of that attack becomes your sworn enemy until it dies or until dawn seven days later. You can have only one sworn enemy at a time. When your sworn enemy dies, you may choose a new one after the next dawn.

When you make a ranged attack roll against your sworn enemy, you have Advantage. The target gains no benefit from Half Cover or Three-Quarters Cover, and you suffer no Disadvantage from long-range attacks. On a hit, your sworn enemy takes an additional 3d6 Piercing damage.

While your sworn enemy remains alive, you have Disadvantage on attack rolls made with all other weapons.

Name: Obsidian
Text:
A glossy black volcanic gemstone.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Obsidian statuette with gold fittings and inlay
Text:
A carved obsidian figurine adorned with gold inlays and fittings.

Art objects include fine jewelry, ornate sculptures, paintings, and other valuable crafted works.

Name: Oil
Text:
You may splash a creature, object, or area with Oil or use it as fuel, as described below.

Dousing a Creature or Object. When taking the Attack action, you may replace one attack with throwing a flask of Oil at a creature or object within 20 feet. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 + your Dexterity modifier + your Proficiency Bonus) or be coated in oil. If the target takes Fire damage before the oil dries (after 1 minute), it suffers an additional 5 Fire damage.

Dousing a Space. As a Utilize action, you may pour a flask of Oil on level ground to cover a 5-foot square within 5 feet. If ignited, the oil burns until the end of the turn two rounds later (12 seconds), dealing 5 Fire damage to any creature entering or ending its turn in the area (once per turn).

Fuel. Oil can fuel Lamps and Lanterns. A flask burns for 6 hours total, which may be divided across multiple uses by extinguishing and relighting it.

Name: Oil of Etherealness
Text:
One vial of this oil coats one Medium or smaller creature and its worn or carried equipment (larger creatures require one additional vial per size category). Applying the oil takes 10 minutes. The affected creature gains the effect of the Etherealness spell for 1 hour.

Droplets of this cloudy gray oil bead on the outside of the container and evaporate quickly.

Name: Oil of Sharpness
Text:
A single vial of this oil can coat one nonmagical Melee weapon or up to twenty pieces of nonmagical ammunition, but only if they deal Slashing or Piercing damage. Applying the oil requires 1 minute, after which it is absorbed into the item, transforming the coated weapon into a +3 Weapon or the coated ammunition into +3 Ammunition.

This transparent, gel-like oil glitters with minuscule silver fragments.

Name: Oil of Slipperiness
Text:
One vial of this oil coats one Medium or smaller creature and its worn or carried gear (larger creatures require one extra vial per size category). Applying the oil takes 10 minutes. The coated creature gains the effect of the Freedom of Movement spell for 8 hours.

Alternatively, you may pour the oil onto the ground as a Magic action, covering a 10-foot square and replicating the Grease spell in that area for 8 hours.

This thick, tar-black substance is heavy and sticky, yet flows rapidly when poured.

Name: Oil of Taggit
Text: A creature exposed to Oil of Taggit must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or become Poisoned for 24 hours. While Poisoned in this way, the creature is also Unconscious. It awakens if it takes damage.

Name: Old masterpiece painting
Text:
A finely crafted painting of exceptional age and artistry, often depicting historical figures or mythic scenes.

Art objects encompass jewelry, sculptures, paintings, and other valuable crafted works.

Name: Onyx
Text:
A gemstone featuring alternating bands of black and white, or sometimes entirely black or entirely white.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Opal
Text:
A pale blue gemstone marked with shifting green and golden patterns.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Orb
Text: An Arcane Focus shaped as a polished orb, often adorned with carvings or gemstones to help channel arcane power. Sorcerers, Warlocks, and Wizards can use it as a Spellcasting Focus.

Name: Orb of Direction
Text:
This orb also functions as an Arcane Focus.

While holding it, you may take a Magic action to determine the direction of magnetic north. If the location lacks a magnetic north, the orb provides no result.

Name: Orb of Dragonkind
Text:
Forged long ago during a devastating war against chromatic dragons, these orbs were created by the wizards of the Towers of High Sorcery to lure dragons and destroy them with powerful magic. Five were made, one for each tower, though only three are believed to remain.

Each orb contains the essence of an evil dragon, a willful presence that resists being controlled. Those who attempt to wield its power without sufficient force of personality risk falling under its influence.

The orb is a crystal sphere roughly 10 inches across, expanding to about 20 inches when activated, swirling with internal mist.

While attuned, you may take a Magic action to gaze into the orb. You must then make a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success, you control the orb while attuned. On a failure, the orb Charms you for as long as you remain attuned.

While Charmed, you cannot willingly end your Attunement, and the orb casts Suggestion (save DC 18) on you at will, urging you toward its malevolent goals—such as promoting the worship of Tiamat, spreading suffering, or pursuing other destructive aims.

Random Properties. An Orb of Dragonkind has:

• 2 minor beneficial properties
• 1 minor detrimental property
• 1 major detrimental property

Spells. The orb has 7 charges and regains 1d4 + 3 charges at dawn. If you control the orb, you may cast the following spells from it:

Spell | Charge Cost
Cure Wounds (level 9) | 4
Daylight | 1
Death Ward | 2
Detect Magic | 0
Scrying (DC 18) | 3

Call Dragons. While controlling the orb, you may take a Magic action to send out a telepathic summons extending 40 miles in all directions. Chromatic dragons feel compelled to travel directly toward the orb. Dragon deities such as Tiamat are unaffected. Dragons forced to answer the call may be Hostile toward you. This ability recharges after 1 hour.

Destroying an Orb. An Orb of Dragonkind has AC 20 and can be destroyed only by damage from a +3 Weapon or a Disintegrate spell.

Name: Orb of Time
Text:
This orb also functions as an Arcane Focus.

While holding it, you may take a Magic action to determine whether the current time is morning, afternoon, evening, or night. This ability works only on the Material Plane.

Name: Ox
Text: A sturdy draft animal commonly exchanged as a trade good in place of coin.

Name: Padded Armor
Text: This lightly cushioned armor is inexpensive but cumbersome, imposing Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Painted gold war mask
Text:
A ceremonial mask crafted of gold and decorated with paint, often used in rituals or as a symbol of status.

Art objects include jewelry, sculptures, paintings, and other valuable crafted works.

Name: Painter's Supplies
Text:
Ability: Wisdom

Utilize: Paint a recognizable image of something you've seen (DC 10)

Craft: Druidic Focus, Holy Symbol

Artisan's Tools are specialized kits for crafting and trade work, each requiring its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any related ability check. If you are also proficient in a relevant skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Pair of engraved bone dice
Text:
A set of carved bone dice etched with decorative markings.

Art objects encompass fine jewelry, sculptures, paintings, and other crafted valuables.

Name: Pale Tincture
Text: A creature exposed to Pale Tincture must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or take 3 (1d6) Poison damage and become Poisoned. A Poisoned creature repeats the saving throw every 24 hours, taking 3 (1d6) Poison damage on a failure. Damage from this poison cannot be healed while the creature remains Poisoned. After seven successful saving throws, the creature recovers from the poison.

Name: Pan Flute
Text:
Ability: Charisma

Utilize: Perform a familiar melody (DC 10), or improvise a tune on the spot (DC 15)

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check that uses it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Paper
Text: A single sheet of paper can contain roughly 250 handwritten words.

Name: Parchment
Text: A sheet of parchment can hold about 250 handwritten words.

Name: Pearl
Text:
A smooth, lustrous gemstone that may appear white, yellow, or pink.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Pearl of Power
Text: While carrying this pearl, you may take a Magic action to regain one expended spell slot of level 3 or lower. Once used, the pearl cannot be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Pepper
Text: A valuable spice often traded directly as a commodity rather than exchanged for coin.

Name: Perfume
Text: This 4-ounce vial of scented liquid grants Advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks made to influence an Indifferent Humanoid within 5 feet of you for 1 hour after application.

Name: Perfume of Bewitching
Text: This small vial contains enchanted perfume with enough for a single use. As a Magic action, you may apply it to yourself. For 1 hour, you gain Advantage on Charisma (Deception and Persuasion) checks made to influence a creature within 5 feet of you.

Name: Periapt of Health
Text:
While wearing this pendant, you may take a Magic action to restore 2d4 + 2 Hit Points. Once this ability is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Additionally, you have Advantage on saving throws made to resist or end the Poisoned condition while wearing the periapt.

Name: Periapt of Proof against Poison
Text: This fine silver chain bears a faceted black gemstone. While you wear it, you gain Immunity to both Poison damage and the Poisoned condition.

Name: Periapt of Wound Closure
Text:
While wearing this pendant, you gain the following benefits:

Life Preservation. When you make a Death Saving Throw, you may treat any roll of 9 or lower as a 10, turning a failure into a success.

Natural Healing Boost. Whenever you roll a Hit Die to regain Hit Points, double the amount of Hit Points restored.

Name: Peridot
Text:
A vibrant olive-green gemstone.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Philter of Love
Text:
After drinking this potion, the next creature you see within 10 minutes becomes the target of your Charmed condition for 1 hour.

The liquid is rose-colored and bubbly, containing a tiny heart-shaped bubble that is easy to overlook.

Name: Pig
Text: A common livestock animal often traded directly as a commodity rather than exchanged for coin.

Name: Pike
Text:
Heavy. You have Disadvantage on attack rolls with a Heavy weapon if it's a Melee weapon and your Strength score is below 13, or if it's a Ranged weapon and your Dexterity score is below 13.

Reach. This weapon increases your reach by 5 feet for attacks and Opportunity Attacks.

Two-Handed. You must use both hands to attack with this weapon.

Mastery: Push. When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push it up to 10 feet directly away from you if it is Large or smaller.

Name: Pipe of Smoke Monsters
Text: While smoking this pipe, you may take a Magic action to exhale a puff of smoke shaped like a creature—such as a dragon, flumph, or slaad—small enough to fit in a 1-foot cube. The shape dissipates after a few seconds, becoming ordinary smoke.

Name: Pipes of Haunting
Text: These pipes hold 3 charges and regain 1d3 expended charges at dawn. As a Magic action, you can play them and expend 1 charge to produce a haunting melody. Each creature of your choice within 30 feet must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or become Frightened for 1 minute. A creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. A creature that succeeds is immune to these pipes for 24 hours.

Name: Pipes of the Sewers
Text:
While you carry these pipes, ordinary rats and giant rats treat you as Indifferent and won't attack unless provoked.

The pipes have 3 charges and regain 1d3 expended charges at dawn. If you play them as a Magic action, you may take a Bonus Action to expend 1–3 charges, summoning one Swarm of Rats per charge if enough rats exist within half a mile (DM's discretion). If insufficient rats are present, the charge is wasted. Summoned swarms move toward the music but are otherwise uncontrolled.

Any uncontrolled Swarm of Rats that comes within 30 feet of you while you play must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw. On a success, it behaves normally and cannot be influenced again for 24 hours. On a failure, it becomes Friendly and obeys your commands as long as you continue playing each round. If you give no commands, it only defends itself.
