If a Friendly swarm begins its turn more than 30 feet from you, control ends and it cannot be influenced again for 24 hours.

Name: Plate Armor
Text:
This heavy armor imposes Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

If the wearer has a Strength score below 15, their Speed is reduced by 10 feet.

Name: Plate Armor of Etherealness (*)
Text:
* This Wondrous Item shares its name and source with the item Plate Armor of Etherealness.

While wearing this armor, you may take a Magic action and speak a command word to gain the effect of the Etherealness spell. The effect ends immediately if you remove the armor or take a Magic action to repeat the command word. This ability can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Platinum
Text: A valuable precious metal often traded directly as a commodity rather than exchanged for coin.

Name: Platinum bracelet set with an emerald
Text:
A finely crafted platinum bracelet adorned with a polished emerald.

Art objects include jewelry, sculptures, paintings, and other valuable crafted works.

Name: Playing Cards
Text:
Ability: Wisdom

Utilize: Detect cheating (DC 10), or win the game (DC 20)

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any check using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Poisoner's Kit
Text:
Ability: Intelligence

Utilize: Identify a poisoned object (DC 10)

Craft: Basic Poison

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any check using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Pole
Text: This 10-foot pole allows you to touch or test objects up to 10 feet away. When making a Strength (Athletics) check as part of a High or Long Jump, you can vault with the pole, gaining Advantage on the check.

Name: Pole of Angling
Text: This item functions as a standard Pole. While holding it, you may take a Magic action to transform it into a fishing pole complete with hook, line, and reel, or revert it back into a Pole.

Name: Pole of Collapsing
Text: This item functions as a Pole. While holding it, you may take a Magic action to collapse it into a 1-foot rod for easy storage (its weight remains unchanged) or extend it back into a full Pole. The rod only extends as far as surrounding space allows.

Name: Pony
Text: A small but sturdy mount well-suited for carrying loads or riders over moderate distances.

Name: Portable Hole
Text:
This soft black cloth folds to the size of a handkerchief but unfolds into a 6-foot-diameter sheet.

Taking a Magic action to place it on a solid surface creates a 10-foot-deep extradimensional cylindrical space. The space exists on another plane and cannot form open tunnels. Creatures inside can climb out normally.

You may take a Magic action to fold the cloth, closing the hole. Any creatures or objects inside remain stored in the extradimensional space, and the hole weighs almost nothing regardless of contents.

A creature trapped inside a closed Portable Hole can attempt a DC 10 Strength (Athletics) check to escape, appearing within 5 feet of the cloth on a success. A closed hole contains enough air for 1 hour of breathing, divided among all creatures inside.

Placing a Portable Hole inside an extradimensional space created by a Bag of Holding, Heward's Handy Haversack, or similar item destroys both items and opens a one-way gate to the Astral Plane. Creatures within 10 feet not behind Total Cover are drawn through and deposited at a random location before the gate closes.

Name: Portable Ram
Text: This heavy tool is designed to break doors or barriers. When using it, you gain a +4 bonus to Strength checks made to break objects. Another creature can assist you, granting Advantage on the check.

Name: Pot of Awakening
Text:
If you plant a normal shrub in this 10-pound clay pot and allow it to grow for 30 days, it magically becomes an Awakened Shrub at the end of that period. When it awakens, its roots break the pot, destroying it.

The awakened shrub is Friendly toward you and follows your commands. Without commands, it takes no actions.

Name: Potion of Acid Resistance
Text: After drinking this potion, you gain Resistance to Acid damage for 1 hour.

Name: Potion of Animal Friendship
Text:
Upon drinking this potion, you can cast the level 3 version of the Animal Friendship spell (save DC 13).

Shaking the muddy liquid reveals tiny components such as a fish scale, a hummingbird feather, a cat claw, or a squirrel hair.

Name: Potion of Clairvoyance
Text:
Drinking this potion grants you the effect of the Clairvoyance spell, without requiring Concentration.

A floating eyeball drifts within the yellowish liquid but disappears when the bottle is opened.

Name: Potion of Climbing
Text:
After drinking this potion, you gain a Climb Speed equal to your Speed for 1 hour. During this time, you also have Advantage on Strength (Athletics) checks made to climb.

The potion separates into brown, silver, and gray layers resembling stone strata, and shaking it does not mix them.

Name: Potion of Cloud Giant Strength
Text:
When you drink this potion, your Strength score becomes 27 for 1 hour. The potion has no effect if your Strength is already 27 or higher.

The clear liquid contains a floating sliver of fingernail from a cloud giant.

Name: Potion of Cold Resistance
Text: After drinking this potion, you gain Resistance to Cold damage for 1 hour.

Name: Potion of Comprehension
Text:
When consumed, this potion grants you the effect of the Comprehend Languages spell for 1 hour.

The liquid is clear, with grains of salt and soot drifting within it.

Name: Potion of Diminution
Text:
Upon drinking this potion, you gain the "reduce" effect of the Enlarge/Reduce spell for 1d4 hours, without requiring Concentration.

The red coloration inside the potion repeatedly shrinks into a tiny bead before expanding again, and shaking the bottle does not interrupt this cycle.

Name: Potion of Fire Breath
Text:
After drinking this potion, you may use a Bonus Action to exhale fire at a creature within 30 feet. The target must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw, taking 4d6 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. The effect ends after three uses or after 1 hour.

The orange liquid flickers like flame, and smoke gathers at the top of the container, escaping when opened.

Name: Potion of Fire Giant Strength
Text:
Drinking this potion sets your Strength score to 25 for 1 hour. If your Strength is already 25 or higher, the potion has no effect.

The clear liquid contains a floating sliver of fingernail from a fire giant.

Name: Potion of Fire Resistance
Text: After drinking this potion, you gain Resistance to Fire damage for 1 hour.

Name: Potion of Flying
Text:
After drinking this potion, you gain a Fly Speed equal to your Speed for 1 hour and can hover. If you are airborne when the effect ends, you fall unless another effect keeps you aloft.

The clear liquid floats at the top of the container, with cloudy white streaks drifting through it.

Name: Potion of Force Resistance
Text: After drinking this potion, you gain Resistance to Force damage for 1 hour.

Name: Potion of Frost Giant Strength
Text:
When you drink this potion, your Strength score becomes 23 for 1 hour. If your Strength is already 23 or higher, the potion has no effect.

The transparent liquid contains a floating shard of fingernail from a frost giant.

Name: Potion of Gaseous Form
Text:
After drinking this potion, you gain the effect of the Gaseous Form spell for 1 hour (no Concentration required), or until you end the effect as a Bonus Action.

The container appears to hold swirling fog that moves like liquid.

Name: Potion of Giant Strength
Text:
Multiple versions of this potion exist, each tied to a different type of giant:

Potion of Hill Giant Strength
Potion of Frost Giant Strength
Potion of Stone Giant Strength
Potion of Fire Giant Strength
Potion of Cloud Giant Strength
Potion of Storm Giant Strength

Name: Potion of Greater Healing
Text: Drinking this potion restores 4d4 + 4 Hit Points. The red liquid shimmers when moved.

Name: Potion of Greater Invisibility
Text: This potion appears empty but feels as though it contains liquid. After drinking it, you gain the Invisible condition for 1 hour.

Name: Potion of Growth
Text:
After drinking this potion, you gain the "enlarge" effect of the Enlarge/Reduce spell for 10 minutes (no Concentration required).

The red coloration inside the potion expands outward from a tiny bead before contracting again, a cycle unaffected by shaking.

Name: Potion of Healing
Text: This magical potion can be consumed as a Bonus Action or administered to a creature within 5 feet. The drinker regains 2d4 + 2 Hit Points. The red liquid sparkles when disturbed.

Name: Potion of Heroism
Text:
After drinking this potion, you gain 10 Temporary Hit Points for 1 hour. For the same duration, you are affected by the Bless spell without needing Concentration.

The potion’s blue liquid bubbles and steams as though boiling.

Name: Potion of Hill Giant Strength
Text:
When consumed, this potion sets your Strength score to 21 for 1 hour. If your Strength is already 21 or higher, the potion has no effect.

A sliver of hill giant fingernail floats within the clear liquid.

Name: Potion of Invisibility
Text: This potion appears empty but feels as if it contains liquid. After drinking it, you gain the Invisible condition for 1 hour. The effect ends early if you make an attack roll, deal damage, or cast a spell.

Name: Potion of Invulnerability
Text:
For 1 minute after drinking this potion, you gain Resistance to all damage types.

The thick, syrup-like liquid resembles molten iron.

Name: Potion of Lightning Resistance
Text: After drinking this potion, you gain Resistance to Lightning damage for 1 hour.

Name: Potion of Longevity
Text:
Drinking this potion reduces your physical age by 1d6 + 6 years, to a minimum age of 13. Each additional Potion of Longevity you drink carries a cumulative 10 percent chance of instead aging you by 1d6 + 6 years.

Suspended in the amber liquid is a tiny, impossibly beating heart, which disappears when the potion is opened.

Name: Potion of Mind Reading
Text:
When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the Detect Thoughts spell (save DC 13) for 10 minutes, without requiring Concentration.

The potion contains a thick purple liquid with a floating pink, egg-shaped cloud.

Name: Potion of Necrotic Resistance
Text: After drinking this potion, you gain Resistance to Necrotic damage for 1 hour.

Name: Potion of Poison
Text:
This potion mimics the appearance, scent, and taste of a Potion of Healing or another beneficial potion, but is actually poison concealed by illusion magic. Identify reveals its true nature.

If consumed, the drinker takes 4d6 Poison damage and must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or become Poisoned for 1 hour.

Name: Potion of Poison Resistance
Text: After drinking this potion, you gain Resistance to Poison damage for 1 hour.

Name: Potion of Psychic Resistance
Text: After drinking this potion, you gain Resistance to Psychic damage for 1 hour.

Name: Potion of Pugilism
Text:
For 10 minutes after drinking this potion, each Unarmed Strike you make deals an additional 1d6 Force damage on a hit.

The potion is a thick green mixture with a spinach-like flavor.

Name: Potion of Radiant Resistance
Text: After drinking this potion, you gain Resistance to Radiant damage for 1 hour.

Name: Potion of Resistance
Text:
When you drink this potion, you gain Resistance to one damage type for 1 hour. The DM either chooses the type or rolls on the following table:

d10 | Damage Type
1 Acid
2 Cold
3 Fire
4 Force
5 Lightning
6 Necrotic
7 Poison
8 Psychic
9 Radiant
10 Thunder

Name: Potion of Speed
Text:
After drinking this potion, you gain the effect of the Haste spell for 1 minute, without requiring Concentration and without suffering the usual lethargy afterward.

The yellow liquid is streaked with black and swirls on its own.

Name: Potion of Stone Giant Strength
Text:
When consumed, this potion sets your Strength score to 23 for 1 hour. If your Strength is already 23 or higher, the potion has no effect.

A sliver of stone giant fingernail floats within the clear liquid.

Name: Potion of Storm Giant Strength
Text:
After drinking this potion, your Strength score becomes 29 for 1 hour. If your Strength is already 29 or higher, the potion has no effect.

The transparent liquid contains a floating sliver of fingernail from a storm giant.

Name: Potion of Superior Healing
Text: Drinking this potion restores 8d4 + 8 Hit Points. The shimmering red liquid glows faintly when moved.

Name: Potion of Supreme Healing
Text: Drinking this potion restores 10d4 + 20 Hit Points. The bright red liquid sparkles when agitated.

Name: Potion of Thunder Resistance
Text: After drinking this potion, you gain Resistance to Thunder damage for 1 hour.

Name: Potion of Vitality
Text:
Drinking this potion removes all your levels of Exhaustion and ends the Poisoned condition. For the next 24 hours, any Hit Point Die you spend restores the maximum possible amount.

Its deep crimson liquid pulses with a dim light, reminiscent of a heartbeat.

Name: Potion of Water Breathing
Text:
After drinking this potion, you can breathe underwater for 24 hours.

The cloudy green liquid smells of seawater and contains a bubble that resembles a tiny jellyfish.

Name: Potions of Healing
Text:
Several versions of this potion exist, each restoring different amounts of Hit Points:

Potion of Healing
Potion of Greater Healing
Potion of Superior Healing
Potion of Supreme Healing

Name: Potter's Tools
Text:
Ability: Intelligence

Utilize: Determine what a ceramic object was used for in the past 24 hours (DC 15)

Craft: Jug, Lamp

Artisan's Tools are specialized kits for crafting and trade work, each requiring its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any related ability check. If you are also proficient in a relevant skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Pouch
Text: A small pouch capable of holding up to 6 pounds of items within one-fifth of a cubic foot.

Name: Priest's Pack
Text: A Priest's Pack includes: a Backpack, Blanket, Holy Water, Lamp, 7 days of Rations, a Robe, and a Tinderbox.

Name: Prosthetic Limb
Text: This magical prosthetic replaces a missing limb—such as a hand, arm, foot, or leg. While attached, it functions exactly like the original limb. You can detach or reattach it as a Magic action, and it cannot be forcibly removed while you are alive.

Name: Psychic Blade
Mastery: Mastery: Vex (you can use this property, and it doesn't count against the number of properties you can use with Weapon Mastery)
Text:
Finesse. When attacking with a Finesse weapon, you may use either Strength or Dexterity for attack and damage rolls, but must use the same ability for both.

Thrown. A weapon with this property can be thrown to make a ranged attack, and drawing it is part of the attack. If it is a Melee weapon, use the same ability modifier as you would for a melee attack.

Mastery: Vex. When you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage, you gain Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

Name: Purple Worm Poison
Text: A creature exposed to Purple Worm Poison must make a DC 21 Constitution saving throw, taking 35 (10d6) Poison damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

Name: Feather Token
Text:
This item resembles a simple feather. Several varieties exist, each providing a unique single-use magical effect. The DM either selects the type or rolls on the Feather Tokens table. The token’s type determines its rarity.

Feather Tokens
1d100 | Token | Rarity
01–20 Anchor (Uncommon)
21–35 Bird (Rare)
36–50 Fan (Uncommon)
51–65 Swan Boat (Rare)
66–90 Tree (Uncommon)
91–00 Whip (Rare)

Name: Feather Token, Anchor
Text:
This token appears as a feather.

By taking a Magic action to touch it to a boat or ship, the vessel becomes immovable for 24 hours. Touching the token to the vessel again ends the effect early. When the effect ends, the token vanishes.

Name: Feather Token, Bird
Text:
This token looks like a feather.

By taking a Magic action to toss it 5 feet into the air, the token disappears and becomes a massive multicolored bird. It uses the statistics of a Roc but cannot attack. It follows simple commands and can carry up to 500 pounds at full speed (16 mph, up to 144 miles per day with 1 hour of rest per 3 hours of flight) or 1000 pounds at half speed. The bird disappears after reaching its daily travel limit, dropping to 0 Hit Points, or when dismissed with a Magic action.

Name: Feather Token, Fan
Text:
This token resembles a feather.

If you are aboard a boat or ship, you may take a Magic action to toss the token up to 10 feet into the air. It disappears and becomes a giant floating fan that produces a strong wind. This wind fills the sails of one ship, increasing its speed by 5 miles per hour for 8 hours. You may dismiss the fan with a Magic action.

Name: Feather Token, Swan Boat
Text:
This token appears as a feather.

By taking a Magic action to touch it to a body of water at least 60 feet across, the token vanishes and becomes a 50-foot-long, 20-foot-wide swan-shaped boat. The vessel is self-propelled and moves at 6 miles per hour. While aboard, you may take a Magic action to command it to move or turn up to 90 degrees. The boat lasts for 24 hours or until dismissed with a Magic action.

Name: Feather Token, Tree
Text:
This token resembles a simple feather.

You must be outdoors to activate it. By taking a Magic action and touching it to an unoccupied patch of ground, the token vanishes and a nonmagical oak tree instantly grows in its place. The tree stands 60 feet tall, with a trunk 5 feet in diameter and branches spreading in a 20-foot radius.

Name: Feather Token, Whip
Text:
This token appears as a feather.

By taking a Magic action to throw it at a point within 10 feet, the token disappears and becomes a floating whip. You may then take a Bonus Action to make a melee spell attack against a creature within 10 feet of the whip, using a +9 attack bonus. On a hit, the target takes 1d6 + 5 Force damage.

As a Bonus Action, you can command the whip to fly up to 20 feet and repeat the attack. The whip lasts for 1 hour, until dismissed with a Magic action, or until you die or become Incapacitated.

Name: Quarterstaff
Text:
Versatile. This weapon can be wielded with one or two hands. The damage value in parentheses is used when attacking with two hands.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, you may force it to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + the ability modifier used for the attack + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature gains the Prone condition.

Name: Quarterstaff of the Acrobat
Text:
You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

While holding it, you may cause it to emit green Dim Light out to 10 feet as a Bonus Action or immediately after rolling Initiative. You may also extinguish the light as a Bonus Action.

As a Bonus Action, you can change its form into a 6-inch rod for storage, a 10-foot pole, or revert it to a Quarterstaff. It only extends as far as space allows.

Additional properties by form:

Acrobatic Assist (Quarterstaff and 10-Foot Pole Only). You have Advantage on Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks while holding it.

Attack Deflection (Quarterstaff Only). When hit by an attack, you may use a Reaction to spin the staff defensively, gaining +5 AC against the triggering attack. Once used, this property can't be used again until a Short or Long Rest.

Ranged Weapon (Quarterstaff Only). The weapon has the Thrown property (30/120 ft.). After making a ranged attack, it immediately returns to your hand.

Thrown. A thrown melee weapon uses the same ability modifier as its melee attack.

Versatile. When used with two hands, it deals the damage listed in parentheses.

Mastery: Topple. On a hit, you may force the target to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + your attack ability modifier + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failure, the target becomes Prone.

Name: Quartz
Text:
A gemstone that may appear white, smoky gray, or yellow.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Quiver
Text: A quiver designed to hold up to 20 arrows securely.

Name: Feather Token, Tree
Text:
This token resembles a delicate feather.

You must be outdoors to activate it. By taking a Magic action and touching it to an empty spot on the ground, the token vanishes and a nonmagical oak tree instantly grows in its place. The tree reaches 60 feet in height, has a trunk 5 feet thick, and its upper branches spread in a 20-foot radius.

Name: Feather Token, Whip
Text:
This token appears as a feather.

By taking a Magic action to throw it at a point within 10 feet, the token disappears and becomes a floating whip. You may then take a Bonus Action to make a melee spell attack against a creature within 10 feet of the whip, using a +9 attack bonus. On a hit, the target takes 1d6 + 5 Force damage.

As a Bonus Action, you can command the whip to fly up to 20 feet and repeat the attack. The whip lasts for 1 hour, until dismissed with a Magic action, or until you die or become Incapacitated.

Name: Quarterstaff
Text:
Versatile. This weapon can be wielded with one or two hands. The damage value in parentheses applies when used with two hands.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, you may force it to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + the ability modifier used for the attack + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature gains the Prone condition.

Name: Quarterstaff of the Acrobat
Text:
You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

While holding it, you may cause it to emit green Dim Light out to 10 feet as a Bonus Action or immediately after rolling Initiative. You may also extinguish the light as a Bonus Action.

As a Bonus Action, you can change its form into a 6-inch rod for storage, a 10-foot pole, or revert it to a Quarterstaff. It only extends as far as space allows.

Additional properties by form:

Acrobatic Assist (Quarterstaff and 10-Foot Pole Only). You have Advantage on Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks while holding it.

Attack Deflection (Quarterstaff Only). When hit by an attack, you may use a Reaction to spin the staff defensively, gaining +5 AC against the triggering attack. Once used, this property can't be used again until a Short or Long Rest.

Ranged Weapon (Quarterstaff Only). The weapon has the Thrown property (30/120 ft.). After making a ranged attack, it immediately returns to your hand.

Thrown. A thrown melee weapon uses the same ability modifier as its melee attack.

Versatile. When used with two hands, it deals the damage listed in parentheses.

Mastery: Topple. On a hit, you may force the target to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + your attack ability modifier + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failure, the target becomes Prone.

Name: Quartz
Text:
A gemstone that may appear white, smoky gray, or yellow.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Quiver
Text: A quiver designed to hold up to 20 arrows securely.

Name: Quiver of Ehlonna
Text:
This quiver contains three compartments, each linked to an extradimensional space, allowing it to store numerous items while never weighing more than 2 pounds.

The shortest compartment holds up to 60 arrows, bolts, or similar ammunition. The middle compartment can store up to 18 javelins or comparable items. The longest compartment accommodates up to 6 long objects, such as bows, spears, or quarterstaffs.

You can draw any stored item as though retrieving it from a normal quiver or scabbard.

Name: Rapier
Text:
Finesse. When attacking with this weapon, you may use either Strength or Dexterity for attack and damage rolls, but must use the same ability for both.

Mastery: Vex. When you hit a creature and deal damage, you gain Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

Name: Rations
Text: A pack of preserved travel food, typically including jerky, dried fruit, hardtack, and nuts. See "Malnutrition" for the consequences of not eating.

Name: Red Dragon Scale Mail
Text:
Dragon Scale Mail is crafted from the scales of a specific type of dragon. Some dragons gift their shed scales, while others are harvested from slain dragons. Either way, such armor is highly prized.

While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC, have Advantage on saving throws against dragon breath weapons, and gain Resistance to Fire damage.

You may also take a Magic action to sense the distance and direction to the nearest red dragon within 30 miles. This ability can't be used again until the next dawn.

The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Reliquary
Text:
A reliquary must be held to function as a Holy Symbol.

A Holy Symbol is crafted in a specific form and decorated to channel divine magic. Clerics and Paladins can use it as a Spellcasting Focus.

Name: Rhodochrosite
Text:
A pale pink gemstone.

Gemstones are compact, lightweight, and easier to safeguard than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Riding Horse
Text: A reliable mount commonly used for travel and transport.

Name: Riding Saddle
Text: A riding saddle includes a bit, bridle, reins, and all necessary gear to properly equip a mount.

Name: Ring Mail
Text: The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Ring of Acid Resistance
Text: While wearing this ring, you gain Resistance to Acid damage. The ring is adorned with a pearl.

Name: Ring of Animal Influence
Text:
This ring contains 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges at dawn. While wearing it, you may expend 1 charge to cast one of the following spells (save DC 13):

Animal Friendship
Fear (affects Beasts only)
Speak with Animals

Name: Ring of Cold Resistance
Text: While wearing this ring, you gain Resistance to Cold damage. The ring is adorned with a piece of tourmaline.

Name: Ring of Djinni Summoning
Text:
While attuned to and wearing this ring, you may take a Magic action to summon a specific Djinni from the Elemental Plane of Air. It appears in an unoccupied space you choose within 120 feet. The djinni remains for as long as you maintain Concentration (up to 1 hour) or until it reaches 0 Hit Points.

The summoned djinni is Friendly toward you and your allies and follows your commands. If you issue no commands, it defends itself but takes no other actions.

Once the djinni departs, it cannot be summoned again for 24 hours. If the djinni dies, the ring permanently loses its magic.

Such rings are often crafted by djinn themselves and bestowed upon mortals as marks of friendship or honor.

Name: Ring of Elemental Command
Text:
Several versions of this ring exist, each tied to a different elemental force:

Ring of Elemental Command (Air)
Ring of Elemental Command (Earth)
Ring of Elemental Command (Fire)
Ring of Elemental Command (Water)

Name: Ring of Elemental Command (Air)
Text:
This ring is attuned to the Elemental Plane of Air. All Rings of Elemental Command share the following properties:

Elemental Bane. While wearing the ring, you have Advantage on attack rolls against Elementals, and Elementals have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you.

Elemental Compulsion. While wearing the ring, you can take a Magic action to attempt to compel an Elemental you can see within 60 feet. The Elemental must make a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it becomes Charmed until the start of your next turn, and you choose how it uses its movement and action on its next turn.

Elemental Focus (Air). You can speak Auran, you gain Resistance to Lightning damage, and you have a Fly Speed equal to your Speed and can hover.

Spellcasting. The ring has 5 charges and regains 1d4 + 1 expended charges at dawn. While wearing it, you can cast one of the following spells (save DC 18): Chain Lightning (3 charges), Feather Fall (0 charges), Gust of Wind (2 charges), Wind Wall (1 charge).

Name: Ring of Elemental Command (Earth)
Text:
This ring is attuned to the Elemental Plane of Earth. All Rings of Elemental Command share the following properties:

Elemental Bane. While wearing the ring, you have Advantage on attack rolls against Elementals, and Elementals have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you.

Elemental Compulsion. While wearing the ring, you can take a Magic action to attempt to compel an Elemental you can see within 60 feet. The Elemental must make a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it becomes Charmed until the start of your next turn, and you choose how it uses its movement and action on its next turn.

Elemental Focus (Earth). You can speak Terran, you gain Resistance to Acid damage, and rubble, rocks, or dirt do not count as Difficult Terrain for you. You can also move through solid earth or stone as if it were Difficult Terrain without disturbing it. If you end your turn inside solid material, you are pushed out to the nearest unoccupied space you previously occupied.

Spellcasting. The ring has 5 charges and regains 1d4 + 1 expended charges at dawn. While wearing it, you can cast one of the following spells (save DC 18): Earthquake (5 charges), Stone Shape (2 charges), Stoneskin (3 charges), Wall of Stone (3 charges).

Name: Ring of Elemental Command (Fire)
Text:
This ring is attuned to the Elemental Plane of Fire. All Rings of Elemental Command share the following properties:

Elemental Bane. While wearing the ring, you have Advantage on attack rolls against Elementals, and Elementals have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you.

Elemental Compulsion. While wearing the ring, you can take a Magic action to attempt to compel an Elemental you can see within 60 feet. The Elemental must make a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it becomes Charmed until the start of your next turn, and you choose how it uses its movement and action on its next turn.

Elemental Focus (Fire). You can speak Ignan, and you gain Immunity to Fire damage.

Spellcasting. The ring has 5 charges and regains 1d4 + 1 expended charges at dawn. While wearing it, you can cast one of the following spells (save DC 18): Burning Hands (1 charge), Fireball (2 charges), Fire Storm (4 charges), Wall of Fire (3 charges).

Name: Ring of Elemental Command (Water)
Text:
This ring is attuned to the Elemental Plane of Water. All Rings of Elemental Command share the following properties:

Elemental Bane. While wearing the ring, you have Advantage on attack rolls against Elementals, and Elementals have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you.

Elemental Compulsion. While wearing the ring, you can take a Magic action to attempt to compel an Elemental you can see within 60 feet. The Elemental must make a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it becomes Charmed until the start of your next turn, and you choose how it uses its movement and action on its next turn.

Elemental Focus (Water). You can speak Aquan, you gain a Swim Speed of 60 feet, and you can breathe underwater.

Spellcasting. The ring has 5 charges and regains 1d4 + 1 expended charges at dawn. While wearing it, you can cast one of the following spells (save DC 18): Create or Destroy Water (1 charge), Ice Storm (2 charges), Tsunami (5 charges), Wall of Ice (3 charges), Water Walk (2 charges).

Name: Ring of Evasion
Text: This ring contains 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges at dawn. When you fail a Dexterity saving throw while wearing it, you may use a Reaction to expend 1 charge to succeed on that saving throw instead.

Name: Ring of Feather Falling
Text: While wearing this ring, if you fall, your descent slows to 60 feet per round and you take no damage from the fall.

Name: Ring of Fire Resistance
Text: While wearing this ring, you gain Resistance to Fire damage. The ring features a garnet set into its band.

Name: Ring of Force Resistance
Text: While wearing this ring, you gain Resistance to Force damage. A sapphire is mounted in the ring.

Name: Ring of Free Action
Text: While attuned to and wearing this ring, Difficult Terrain does not slow your movement. Additionally, magic cannot reduce any of your Speeds or impose the Paralyzed or Restrained conditions on you.

Name: Ring of Invisibility
Text: While wearing this ring, you may take a Magic action to become Invisible. You remain Invisible until you remove the ring or take a Bonus Action to end the effect and become visible again.

Name: Ring of Jumping
Text: While wearing this ring, you can cast the Jump spell from it, but only targeting yourself.

Name: Ring of Lightning Resistance
Text: While wearing this ring, you gain Resistance to Lightning damage. A citrine gemstone is set into the band.

Name: Ring of Mind Shielding
Text:
While wearing this ring, you are immune to magic that reads your thoughts, detects lies, reveals your alignment, or identifies your creature type. Telepathic communication with you is possible only if you permit it.

You may take a Magic action to render the ring invisible until you use another Magic action to reveal it, remove it, or die.

If you die while wearing the ring, your soul enters it unless another soul already resides within. You may remain inside or move on to the afterlife. While your soul is in the ring, you can telepathically communicate with any creature wearing it, and the wearer cannot block this communication.

Name: Ring of Necrotic Resistance
Text: While wearing this ring, you gain Resistance to Necrotic damage. The ring is adorned with a jet stone.

Name: Ring of Poison Resistance
Text: While wearing this ring, you gain Resistance to Poison damage. An amethyst is set into the ring.

Name: Ring of Protection
Text: While wearing this ring, you gain a +1 bonus to both Armor Class and saving throws.

Name: Ring of Psychic Resistance
Text: While wearing this ring, you gain Resistance to Psychic damage. A jade gemstone is set into the band.

Name: Ring of Radiant Resistance
Text: While wearing this ring, you gain Resistance to Radiant damage. The ring is adorned with a topaz.

Name: Ring of Regeneration
Text: While wearing this ring, you regain 1d6 Hit Points every 10 minutes as long as you have at least 1 Hit Point. If you lose a body part, the ring causes it to regrow and fully function again after 1d6 + 1 days, provided you remain at 1 Hit Point or more throughout the entire period.

Name: Ring of Resistance
Text:
While wearing this ring, you gain Resistance to one type of damage. The gemstone set into the ring indicates the type, chosen by the DM or determined randomly using the table below.

d10 | Damage Type | Gem
1 Acid — Pearl
2 Cold — Tourmaline
3 Fire — Garnet
4 Force — Sapphire
5 Lightning — Citrine
6 Necrotic — Jet
7 Poison — Amethyst
8 Psychic — Jade
9 Radiant — Topaz
10 Thunder — Spinel

Name: Ring of Shooting Stars
Text:
You can cast Dancing Lights or Light from the ring.

The ring has 6 charges and regains 1d6 expended charges at dawn. You may expend charges to activate the following effects:

Faerie Fire. Spend 1 charge to cast Faerie Fire.

Lightning Spheres. Spend 2 charges as a Magic action to create up to four 3-foot-wide lightning spheres in unoccupied spaces within 120 feet. They last while you maintain Concentration (up to 1 minute) and shed Dim Light in a 30-foot radius. As a Bonus Action, you can move each sphere up to 30 feet, remaining within 120 feet of you. The first time a sphere comes within 5 feet of a creature not behind Total Cover, it discharges and vanishes. The target makes a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking Lightning damage based on the number of spheres created:

1 sphere: 4d12
2 spheres: 5d4
3 spheres: 6d4
4 spheres: 7d4

Shooting Stars. Spend 1–3 charges as a Magic action. For each charge, you launch a glowing mote at a point within 60 feet. Each creature in a 15-foot Cube originating from that point makes a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking 5d4 Radiant damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

Name: Ring of Spell Storing
Text:
This ring can hold spells cast into it, storing them until the attuned wearer releases them. It can contain up to 5 total spell levels at once. When discovered, it holds 1d6 − 1 levels of spells chosen by the DM.

Any creature can cast a spell of level 1–5 into the ring by touching it while casting. The spell has no effect except being stored. If the ring lacks capacity, the spell is wasted. The spell slot used determines how many levels it occupies.

While wearing the ring, you may cast any stored spell. It uses the original caster’s slot level, save DC, attack bonus, and spellcasting ability, but otherwise behaves as if you cast it. Once cast, the spell is removed from the ring, freeing space.

Name: Ring of Spell Turning
Text: While wearing this ring, you have Advantage on saving throws against spells. If you succeed on a save against a spell of level 7 or lower, the spell has no effect on you. If the spell targeted only you and did not create an area of effect, you may use a Reaction to turn the spell back on its caster, who must then make a saving throw against their own spell save DC.

Name: Ring of Swimming
Text: While wearing this ring, you gain a Swim Speed of 40 feet.

Name: Ring of Telekinesis
Text: While wearing this ring, you can cast the Telekinesis spell from it.

Name: Ring of Three Wishes
Text: While wearing this ring, you may expend one of its 3 charges to cast the Wish spell. When the final charge is used, the ring permanently loses its magic.

Name: Ring of Thunder Resistance
Text: While wearing this ring, you gain Resistance to Thunder damage. A spinel gemstone is set into the ring.

Name: Ring of Warmth
Text:
While wearing this ring, whenever you take Cold damage, the ring reduces that damage by 2d8.

Additionally, you and everything you carry or wear are unaffected by temperatures of 0 degrees Fahrenheit or lower.

Name: Ring of Water Walking
Text: While wearing this ring, you can cast Water Walk from it, but only targeting yourself.

Name: Ring of X-ray Vision
Text:
While wearing this ring, you may take a Magic action to gain X-ray vision out to 30 feet for 1 minute. Solid objects within range appear transparent and do not block light. This vision penetrates up to 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of common metal, or 3 feet of wood or dirt. Thicker materials or even a thin sheet of lead block the effect.

If you use the ring again before completing a Long Rest, you must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or gain 1 level of Exhaustion.

Name: Ring of the Ram
Text:
This ring holds 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges at dawn. While wearing it, you may take a Magic action to expend 1–3 charges to make a ranged spell attack against a creature within 60 feet. A spectral ram’s head appears and makes the attack with a +7 bonus. On a hit, the target takes 2d10 Force damage per charge spent and is pushed 5 feet away from you for each charge.

Alternatively, you may expend 1–3 charges as a Magic action to attempt to break a nonmagical object within 60 feet that is not worn or carried. The ring makes a Strength check with a +5 bonus per charge spent.

Name: Rival Coin
Text:
This gold coin bears the images of two well-known rivals or enemies—one on each side. One figure represents “heads,” the other “tails.”

The coin has 1 charge and regains its charge at dawn. As a Magic action, you may toss the coin, expending its charge. Roll any die: even results indicate heads, odd results indicate tails.

Heads. Choose a creature you can see within 60 feet. It must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 2d4 Psychic damage and has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the end of its next turn. On a successful save, it takes half damage only.

Tails. You take 1d4 Psychic damage.

Name: Robe
Text: A robe often carries ceremonial or professional meaning. Certain places or events may require robes of specific colors or symbols for entry.

Name: Robe of Eyes
Text:
This robe is covered in eye-shaped motifs. While wearing it, you gain the following benefits:

All-Around Vision. You have Advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks relying on sight.

Special Senses. You gain Darkvision and Truesight out to 120 feet.

Drawbacks. A Light spell cast on the robe or a Daylight spell cast within 5 feet blinds you for 1 minute. At the end of each of your turns, make a Constitution saving throw (DC 11 for Light, DC 15 for Daylight), ending the condition on a success.

Name: Robe of Scintillating Colors
Text:
This robe contains 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges at dawn. While wearing it, you may take a Magic action and expend 1 charge to cause the robe to shimmer with shifting, brilliant colors until the end of your next turn. During this effect, the robe emits Bright Light in a 30-foot radius and Dim Light for another 30 feet, and creatures that can see you have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you.

Any creature in the Bright Light when the effect activates must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or become Stunned until the effect ends.

Name: Robe of Stars
Text:
This dark robe is decorated with tiny white or silver stars. While wearing it, you gain a +1 bonus to saving throws.

Six larger stars on the robe can be removed as a Magic action to cast Magic Missile at level 5. At dusk, 1d6 expended stars reappear.

You may also take a Magic action to shift yourself—and everything you carry—into the Astral Plane. You remain there until you take another Magic action to return, reappearing in your last occupied space or the nearest unoccupied one.

Name: Robe of Useful Items
Text:
This robe is covered in cloth patches of various shapes and colors. While wearing it, you can take a Magic action to detach a patch, which becomes the object or creature it depicts. Once all patches are removed, the robe becomes a normal garment.

The robe contains two of each of the following patches:

Bullseye Lantern (lit)
Dagger
Mirror
Pole
Rope (coiled)
Sack

It also contains 4d4 additional patches chosen by the DM or determined randomly using the table:

1d100 | Patch
01–08 Bag of 100 GP
09–15 Silver coffer (1 ft. × 6 in. × 6 in.) worth 500 GP
16–22 Iron door (up to 10 ft. × 10 ft., barred on one side), which fits and attaches itself to an opening
23–30 Ten gems worth 100 GP each
31–44 Wooden ladder (24 ft.)
45–51 Riding Horse with Saddle
52–59 Open pit (10-foot Cube), placed on ground within 10 feet
60–68 Four Potions of Healing
69–75 Rowboat (12 ft.)
76–83 Spell Scroll (one spell of level 1–3)
84–90 Two Mastiffs
91–96 Window (2 ft. × 4 ft., up to 2 ft. deep), placed on a vertical surface
97–00 Portable Ram

Name: Robe of the Archmagi
Attunement: Requires Attunement By a Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard
Text:
This finely crafted robe is woven from exceptional fabric and inscribed with arcane runes.

You gain the following benefits while wearing it:

Armor. If you are not wearing armor, your base Armor Class becomes 15 + your Dexterity modifier.

Magic Resistance. You have Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

War Mage. Your spell save DC and spell attack bonus each increase by 2.

Name: Rod
Text: An Arcane Focus shaped as a rod, often decorated or carved to channel magical energy. Sorcerers, Warlocks, and Wizards can use it as a Spellcasting Focus.

Name: Rod of Absorption
Text:
While holding this rod, you may use a Reaction to absorb a spell that targets only you and does not create an area of effect. The spell is negated, and its energy—equal to the spell’s level—is stored within the rod. The rod can store up to 50 levels of energy over its lifetime. Once it reaches this limit, it cannot absorb more. If a spell cannot be stored, the rod has no effect on it.

Upon attuning to the rod, you instantly know how many levels of energy it has absorbed historically and how many it currently holds.

If you are a spellcaster, you may convert stored energy into spell slots of levels you can normally cast, up to level 5. You expend stored levels equal to the slot level you wish to create. For example, 3 stored levels can be used as a level 3 spell slot.

A newly discovered rod typically contains 1d10 levels of stored energy. A rod that has exhausted its ability to absorb energy and has no stored energy remaining becomes nonmagical.

Name: Rod of Alertness
Text:
This rod grants several abilities.

Alertness. While holding the rod, you have Advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks and on Initiative rolls.

Spells. While holding the rod, you can cast the following spells from it: Detect Evil and Good, Detect Magic, Detect Poison and Disease, and See Invisibility.

Protective Aura. As a Magic action, you can plant the rod’s haft into the ground. Its head emits Bright Light in a 60-foot radius and Dim Light for another 60 feet. While in the Bright Light, you and your allies gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class and saving throws, and you can sense the location of any Invisible creature also within the Bright Light.

The glow ends after 10 minutes or when a creature uses a Magic action to remove the rod. This property can’t be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Rod of Lordly Might
Text:
This rod has a flanged head and functions as a +3 magic Mace. Six buttons along the haft activate different transformations, and the rod has three additional powers.

Buttons. Pressing a button is a Bonus Action. The effect lasts until you press another button or press the same button again.

Button 1. A fiery blade emerges from the opposite end. It sheds Bright Light in a 40-foot radius and Dim Light for another 40 feet. It functions as a magic Longsword or Shortsword (your choice) and deals an extra 2d6 Fire damage on a hit.

Button 2. The rod becomes a magic Battleaxe with a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

Button 3. The rod becomes a magic Spear with a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls and extends into a 6-foot haft.

Button 4. The rod becomes a climbing pole up to 50 feet long. A spike anchors the bottom, hooks secure the top, and bars fold out to form a ladder. It supports up to 4000 pounds.

Button 5. The rod becomes a handheld battering ram, granting a +10 bonus to Strength (Athletics) checks made to break barriers.

Button 6. The rod returns to its normal form and points toward magnetic north. It also reveals your approximate depth below or height above ground.

Drain Life. When you hit a creature with a melee attack using the rod, you may force a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the target takes 4d6 Necrotic damage, and you regain Hit Points equal to half the Necrotic damage dealt. This property recharges at dawn.

Paralyze. When you hit a creature with a melee attack using the rod, you may force a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the target is Paralyzed for 1 minute. It repeats the save at the end of each of its turns. This property recharges at dawn.

Terrify. As a Magic action, you can force each creature you can see within 30 feet to make a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the creature is Frightened for 1 minute. It repeats the save at the end of each of its turns. This property recharges at dawn.

Name: Rod of Resurrection
Attunement: Requires Attunement By a Cleric, Druid, or Paladin
Text:
This rod holds 5 charges. While holding it, you may cast one of the following spells: Heal (1 charge) or Resurrection (5 charges).

The rod regains 1 charge at dawn. If you expend the final charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the rod vanishes in a harmless burst of radiant light.

Name: Rod of Rulership
Text: You can take a Magic action to raise the rod and command obedience from any number of creatures you choose within 120 feet that you can see. Each target must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or become Charmed by you for 8 hours. While Charmed in this way, the creature views you as its trusted leader. If you or your allies harm the creature, or if you order it to act against its nature, the effect ends for that creature. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Rod of Security
Text:
While holding this rod, you can take a Magic action to activate it. You and up to 199 willing creatures you can see are instantly transported to a demiplane of your choosing. The demiplane can resemble any peaceful environment you imagine—such as a serene garden, a lively tavern, a grand palace, a tropical island, or a wondrous carnival. The demiplane provides unlimited food and water, and its environment cannot harm its occupants. Objects created or found there cannot exist outside it.

Each hour spent in the demiplane restores Hit Points as if the creature had spent 1 Hit Point Die. Time passes normally, but creatures do not age. Visitors may remain for up to 200 days divided by the number of creatures present (round down).

When the duration ends or you take a Magic action to end the effect, all creatures reappear in the space they occupied when the rod was activated or the nearest unoccupied space. This property can't be used again for 10 days.

Name: Rod of the Pact Keeper
Text:
Several versions of this rod exist, each granting a different enhancement:

+1 Rod of the Pact Keeper
+2 Rod of the Pact Keeper
+3 Rod of the Pact Keeper

Name: Rope
Text:
As a Utilize action, you can tie a knot in the rope with a successful DC 10 Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check. A creature can break the rope with a successful DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check.

You can bind an unwilling creature only if it is Grappled, Incapacitated, or Restrained. If its legs are bound, it becomes Restrained until it escapes. Escaping requires a successful DC 15 Dexterity (Acrobatics) check as an action.

Name: Rope of Climbing
Text:
This 60-foot rope can support up to 3000 pounds. While holding one end, you can take a Magic action to command the other end to move toward a point you choose within its length. It moves 10 feet immediately and 10 feet at the start of each of your turns until it reaches the destination or you command it to stop.

You can instruct the rope to secure itself to an object, release itself, tie or untie knots, or coil for storage. If commanded to knot, it forms large knots at 1-foot intervals, shortening to 50 feet and granting Advantage on checks made to climb it.

The rope has AC 20, 20 Hit Points, and Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage. It regains 1 Hit Point every 5 minutes if it has at least 1 remaining. If reduced to 0 Hit Points, it is destroyed.

Name: Rope of Entanglement
Text:
This rope measures 30 feet in length. While holding one end, you can take a Magic action to command the opposite end to lash out and ensnare a creature you can see within 20 feet. The target must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or become Restrained. You can free the target by releasing your end of the rope (causing it to coil in the target’s space) or by using a Bonus Action to repeat the command (causing it to coil back into your hand).

A creature Restrained by the rope can use its action to attempt a DC 15 Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) check, ending the effect on a success. If you are still holding the rope when the creature escapes, you may use a Reaction to command it to coil back into your hand; otherwise, it coils in the creature’s space.

The rope has AC 20, 20 Hit Points, and Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage. It regains 1 Hit Point every 5 minutes if it has at least 1 HP. If reduced to 0 HP, it is destroyed.

Name: Rope of Mending
Text: This 50-foot rope can magically repair itself when cut into multiple pieces. As a Magic action, you can cause all touching segments not currently in use to rejoin seamlessly. If any portion is lost or destroyed, the rope becomes permanently shorter.

Name: Rowboat
Damage: Speed: 10.5 mph, Carrying Capacity: 3 passengers, Crew 1, AC 11, HP 50
Text:
A small watercraft commonly used on rivers and lakes. A Rowboat weighs 100 pounds and can be carried. When traveling downstream, add the current’s speed (typically 3 mph). Rowboats cannot be rowed against strong currents but can be pulled upstream from shore.

Larger ships require skilled hirelings to operate. Passenger capacity depends on available hammock space, with private cabins reducing capacity to one-fifth. Typical costs are 5 SP per day for a hammock or 2 GP per day for a private cabin.

Repairs require 1 day and 20 GP per Hit Point restored, halved in well-equipped shipyards.

Name: Ruby
Text:
A gemstone ranging from bright red to deep crimson.

Gemstones are compact, durable, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Ruby of the War Mage
Text: This 1-inch ruby is etched with arcane sigils. It allows you to use a Simple or Martial weapon as a Spellcasting Focus. To attune it to a weapon, press the ruby against the weapon for at least 10 minutes. It remains attached until you remove it with a Magic action, the weapon is destroyed, or your Attunement ends.

Name: Sack
Text: A simple container capable of holding up to 30 pounds within 1 cubic foot of space.

Name: Saddle of the Cavalier
Text: While seated in this saddle, you cannot be forcibly dismounted as long as you remain conscious, and attack rolls made against your mount have Disadvantage.

Name: Saffron
Text: A valuable trade spice often exchanged directly between merchants without the use of coin.

Name: Sailing Ship
Damage: Speed: 2 mph, Carrying Capacity: 100 tons cargo, 20 passengers, Crew 20, AC 15, HP 300, Damage Threshold 15
Text: A large sailing vessel used for long-distance travel and cargo transport. Ships move at half speed against strong winds and cannot sail in a dead calm. Downstream travel adds the current’s speed (typically 3 mph). Larger ships require skilled hirelings to operate. Passenger capacity depends on hammock space or private cabins. Repairs cost 20 GP and 1 day per Hit Point restored, halved in well-equipped shipyards.

Name: Salt
Text: A common trade commodity frequently exchanged without the use of currency.

Name: Sardonyx
Text:
A gemstone featuring layered bands of red and white.

Gemstones are compact, durable, and easier to secure than an equivalent value in coins.

Name: Scale Mail
Text: The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Scarab of Protection
Text:
This beetle-shaped talisman grants three protective benefits while carried.

Defense. You gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.

Preservation. The scarab holds 12 charges. When you fail a saving throw against a Necromancy spell or a harmful effect from an Undead, you may use a Reaction to expend 1 charge and succeed instead. When the final charge is used, the scarab crumbles into dust.

Spell Resistance. You have Advantage on saving throws against spells.

Name: Scholar's Pack
Text: A Scholar's Pack includes the following: a Backpack, a Book, Ink, an Ink Pen, a Lamp, ten flasks of Oil, ten sheets of Parchment, and a Tinderbox.

Name: Scimitar
Text:
Finesse. When attacking with a Finesse weapon, you may use either Strength or Dexterity for attack and damage rolls, but must use the same ability for both.

Light. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light weapon, you may make one extra attack as a Bonus Action with a different Light weapon. You do not add your ability modifier to the damage of that Bonus Action attack unless the modifier is negative.

Mastery: Nick. When you make the extra attack granted by the Light property, you may instead make it as part of the Attack action. You can use this extra attack only once per turn.

Name: Scimitar of Speed
Text:
This magical blade grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls. Additionally, you may make one attack with it as a Bonus Action on each of your turns.

Finesse. You may use Strength or Dexterity for attack and damage rolls, using the same ability for both.

Light. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light weapon, you may make an extra attack as a Bonus Action with a different Light weapon, without adding your ability modifier to the damage unless it is negative.

Mastery: Nick. You may make the extra attack from the Light property as part of the Attack action instead of as a Bonus Action, once per turn.

Name: Scroll of Protection
Text:
Each Scroll of Protection wards against a specific creature type, chosen by the DM or determined randomly.

1d100 | Creature Type
01–10 Aberrations
11–15 Beasts
16–20 Celestials
21–25 Constructs
26–35 Dragons
36–45 Elementals
46–50 Humanoids
51–60 Fey
61–70 Fiends
71–75 Giants
76–80 Monstrosities
81–85 Oozes
86–90 Plants
91–00 Undead

Using a Magic action to read the scroll creates a 5‑foot Emanation centered on you for 5 minutes. Creatures of the chosen type cannot enter or affect anything within the area. If you move in a way that would place such a creature inside the area, the effect ends.

A creature within 5 feet of the Emanation may take a Magic action to attempt to overcome it, making a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success, it is no longer affected by the Emanation.

Name: Scroll of Protection (Aberrations)
Text:
When you use a Magic action to read this scroll, a 5‑foot Emanation centered on you appears. For 5 minutes, Aberrations cannot enter the area or affect anything within it. If you move in a way that would place an Aberration inside the Emanation, the effect immediately ends.

A creature within 5 feet of the Emanation may take a Magic action to challenge it, making a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success, that creature is no longer affected by the Emanation.

Name: Scroll of Protection (Beasts)
Text:
Reading this scroll as a Magic action creates a 5‑foot Emanation centered on you. For 5 minutes, Beasts are unable to enter the area or influence anything inside it. If your movement would place a Beast within the Emanation, the effect ends.

A creature within 5 feet of the Emanation may take a Magic action to resist it, making a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success, the creature is no longer affected.

Name: Scroll of Protection (Celestials)
Text:
Using a Magic action to read this scroll produces a 5‑foot Emanation centered on you. For 5 minutes, Celestials cannot enter the area or affect anything within it. If you move in a way that would place a Celestial inside the Emanation, the effect ends.

A creature within 5 feet of the Emanation may take a Magic action to attempt to break through, making a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success, it is no longer affected by the Emanation.

Name: Scroll of Protection (Constructs)
Text:
Reading this scroll as a Magic action creates a 5‑foot Emanation centered on you. For 5 minutes, Constructs cannot enter the area or affect anything within it. If your movement would cause a Construct to be inside the Emanation, the effect ends.

A creature within 5 feet of the Emanation may take a Magic action to oppose it, making a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success, the creature is no longer affected by the Emanation.

Name: Scroll of Protection (Dragons)
Text:
When you use a Magic action to read this scroll, a 5‑foot Emanation centered on you appears. For 5 minutes, Dragons cannot enter the area or affect anything within it. If you move in a way that would place a Dragon inside the Emanation, the effect immediately ends.

A creature within 5 feet of the Emanation may take a Magic action to challenge it, making a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success, that creature is no longer affected by the Emanation.

Name: Scroll of Protection (Elementals)
Text:
Reading this scroll as a Magic action creates a 5‑foot Emanation centered on you. For 5 minutes, Elementals cannot enter the area or influence anything within it. If your movement would place an Elemental inside the Emanation, the effect ends.

A creature within 5 feet of the Emanation may take a Magic action to resist it, making a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success, the creature is no longer affected.

Name: Scroll of Protection (Fey)
Text:
Using a Magic action to read this scroll produces a 5‑foot Emanation centered on you. For 5 minutes, Fey creatures cannot enter the area or affect anything within it. If you move in a way that would place a Fey inside the Emanation, the effect ends.

A creature within 5 feet of the Emanation may take a Magic action to attempt to break through, making a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success, it is no longer affected by the Emanation.

Name: Scroll of Protection (Fiends)
Text:
Reading this scroll as a Magic action creates a 5‑foot Emanation centered on you. For 5 minutes, Fiends cannot enter the area or affect anything within it. If your movement would cause a Fiend to be inside the Emanation, the effect ends.

A creature within 5 feet of the Emanation may take a Magic action to oppose it, making a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success, the creature is no longer affected by the Emanation.

Name: Scroll of Protection (Giants)
Text:
Using a Magic action to read this scroll creates a 5‑foot Emanation centered on you. For 5 minutes, Giants cannot enter the area or affect anything within it. If you move in a way that would place a Giant inside the Emanation, the effect ends.

A creature within 5 feet of the Emanation may take a Magic action to challenge it, making a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success, the creature is no longer affected by the Emanation.

Name: Scroll of Protection (Humanoids)
Text:
Reading this scroll as a Magic action creates a 5‑foot Emanation centered on you. For 5 minutes, Humanoids cannot enter the area or affect anything within it. If you move in a way that would place a Humanoid inside the Emanation, the effect ends.

A creature within 5 feet of the Emanation may take a Magic action to challenge it, making a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success, the creature is no longer affected by the Emanation.

Name: Scroll of Protection (Monstrosities)
Text:
Using a Magic action to read this scroll produces a 5‑foot Emanation centered on you. For 5 minutes, Monstrosities cannot enter the area or influence anything within it. If you move in a way that would place a Monstrosity inside the Emanation, the effect ends.

A creature within 5 feet of the Emanation may take a Magic action to resist it, making a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success, the creature is no longer affected.

Name: Scroll of Protection (Oozes)
Text:
When you read this scroll using a Magic action, a 5‑foot Emanation centered on you appears. For 5 minutes, Oozes cannot enter the area or affect anything inside it. If you move in a way that would place an Ooze within the Emanation, the effect ends.

A creature within 5 feet of the Emanation may take a Magic action to attempt to overcome it, making a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success, the creature is no longer affected.

Name: Scroll of Protection (Plants)
Text:
Reading this scroll as a Magic action creates a 5‑foot Emanation centered on you. For 5 minutes, Plants cannot enter the area or affect anything within it. If you move in a way that would place a Plant inside the Emanation, the effect ends.

A creature within 5 feet of the Emanation may take a Magic action to oppose it, making a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success, the creature is no longer affected.

Name: Scroll of Protection (Undead)
Text:
Using a Magic action to read this scroll produces a 5‑foot Emanation centered on you. For 5 minutes, Undead cannot enter the area or affect anything within it. If you move in a way that would place an Undead inside the Emanation, the effect ends.

A creature within 5 feet of the Emanation may take a Magic action to challenge it, making a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success, the creature is no longer affected by the Emanation.

Name: Scroll of Titan Summoning
Text:
When you use a Magic action to read this scroll, a specific titan named within it manifests in an unoccupied space on land or water that you can see within 1 mile. The DM chooses a titan or determines it randomly using the table below.

The titan is Hostile toward all other creatures and vanishes when reduced to 0 Hit Points. If the space is too small to contain the titan, the summoning fails and the scroll is wasted.

1d100 | Titan
01–15 Animal Lord
16–30 Ooze of Destruction
31–45 Colossus
46–60 Elemental Cataclysm
61–75 Empyrean
76–90 Kraken (requires a sufficiently large body of water or the summoning fails)
91–100 Tarrasque

Name: Scroll of Titan Summoning (Animal Lord)
Text:
When you read this scroll using a Magic action, an Animal Lord appears in an unoccupied space on land or water within 1 mile that you can see.

The Animal Lord is immediately Hostile toward all other creatures and disappears when it reaches 0 Hit Points. If the chosen space cannot contain its size, the summoning fails and the scroll is expended.

Name: Scroll of Titan Summoning (Ooze of Destruction)
Text:
When you take a Magic action to read this scroll, a Ooze of Destruction materializes in an unoccupied land or water space you can see within 1 mile.

The Ooze of Destruction is Hostile toward all other creatures and vanishes upon reaching 0 Hit Points. If summoned into a space too small to contain it, the summoning fails and the scroll is wasted.

Name: Scroll of Titan Summoning (Colossus)
Text:
When you read this scroll using a Magic action, a Colossus manifests in an unoccupied space on land or water that you can see within 1 mile.

The Colossus is immediately Hostile toward all other creatures and vanishes when reduced to 0 Hit Points. If the Colossus is summoned into a space too small to contain it, the summoning fails and the scroll is wasted.

Name: Scroll of Titan Summoning (Elemental Cataclysm)
Text:
When you take a Magic action to read this scroll, an Elemental Cataclysm appears in an unoccupied land or water space you can see within 1 mile.

The Elemental Cataclysm is Hostile toward all other creatures and disappears when it reaches 0 Hit Points. If summoned into a space that cannot contain its size, the summoning fails and the scroll is expended.

Name: Scroll of Titan Summoning (Empyrean)
Text:
When you read this scroll using a Magic action, an Empyrean materializes in an unoccupied space on land or water within 1 mile that you can see.

The Empyrean is Hostile toward all other creatures and vanishes when reduced to 0 Hit Points. If the chosen space is too small to contain it, the summoning fails and the scroll is wasted.

Name: Scroll of Titan Summoning (Kraken)
Text:
When you read this scroll using a Magic action, a Kraken materializes in an unoccupied water space you can see within 1 mile. A Kraken requires a sufficiently large body of water; if the space cannot contain it, the summoning fails and the scroll is wasted.

The Kraken is Hostile toward all other creatures and vanishes when reduced to 0 Hit Points. If summoned into an area too small to hold it, the summoning fails and the scroll is expended.

Name: Scroll of Titan Summoning (Tarrasque)
Text:
When you take a Magic action to read this scroll, a Tarrasque appears in an unoccupied land or water space you can see within 1 mile.

The Tarrasque is immediately Hostile toward all other creatures and disappears when it reaches 0 Hit Points. If the chosen space is not large enough to contain its size, the summoning fails and the scroll is wasted.

Name: Sending Stones
Text:
Sending Stones are crafted in matched pairs, each carved to resemble the other. While touching one stone, you can cast Sending from it, targeting the bearer of the paired stone. If no creature carries the other stone, you immediately know this and the spell is not cast.

Once Sending is cast through either stone, neither can be used again until the next dawn. If one stone is destroyed, the remaining stone becomes nonmagical.

Name: Sentinel Shield
Text: While wielding this shield, you gain Advantage on Initiative rolls and on Wisdom (Perception) checks. The shield bears an emblem shaped like an eye.

Name: Serpent Venom
Text: A creature exposed to this venom must make a DC 11 Constitution saving throw, taking 10 (3d6) Poison damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

Name: Set of Gold Nesting Dolls
Text: These ornate gold dolls are part of a broader category of valuable art objects, which can include gemstone-studded jewelry, royal portraits, and intricately crafted ceremonial items.

Name: Shawm
Text:
Ability: Charisma

Utilize: Perform a known melody (DC 10) or improvise a tune (DC 15).

If you are proficient with this tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to related checks. If you are also proficient in a relevant skill, you gain Advantage on the check.

Name: Sheep
Text: A common trade commodity often exchanged directly between merchants without the use of coin.

Name: Shield
Text: A sturdy defensive tool used to deflect attacks and improve survivability in combat.

Name: Shield of Expression
Text: The front of this shield bears a sculpted face. While wielding it, you may use a Bonus Action to change the expression displayed on the face.

Name: Shield of Missile Attraction
Text:
While holding this shield, you gain Resistance to damage from Ranged weapon attacks.

Curse. This shield carries a curse. Attuning to it curses you until a Remove Curse spell or similar magic is used on you. Removing the shield does not end the curse. Whenever a Ranged weapon attack targets a creature within 10 feet of you, the curse redirects the attack to target you instead.

Name: Shield of the Cavalier
Text:
While wielding this shield, you gain a +2 bonus to Armor Class, in addition to the shield’s normal AC bonus.

The shield grants the following extra abilities while you hold it:

Forceful Bash. When you take the Attack action, you may make one of the attacks using the shield against a creature within 5 feet. Add your Proficiency Bonus and Strength modifier to the attack roll. On a hit, the shield deals Force damage equal to 2d6 + 2 + your Strength modifier. If the target is a creature, you may push it up to 10 feet directly away from you. If the creature is your size or smaller, you may also knock it Prone.

Protective Field. As a Reaction, when you or an ally within 5 feet is targeted by an attack or must make a saving throw against an area effect, you can create a stationary 5‑foot Emanation centered on you. When it appears, any creatures or objects not fully inside it are pushed to the nearest unoccupied space. The triggering attack or effect has no effect on anything inside the Emanation. It lasts while you maintain Concentration, up to 1 minute. Nothing can pass into or out of the Emanation, and creatures or objects inside cannot affect anything outside. Once used, this property can’t be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Shortbow
Text:
Range. A ranged weapon lists two distances: normal range and long range. Attacks beyond normal range are made with Disadvantage, and you cannot attack beyond long range.

Ammunition. You must have the correct ammunition to make an attack with this weapon. Each attack consumes one piece of ammunition. Drawing ammunition is part of the attack. After combat, you may spend 1 minute to recover half the ammunition you used (rounded down).

Two‑Handed. You must use both hands to attack with this weapon.

Mastery: Vex. When you hit a creature and deal damage with this weapon, you gain Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

Name: Shortsword
Text:
Finesse. You may use either Strength or Dexterity for attack and damage rolls, but must use the same ability for both.

Light. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light weapon, you may make an additional attack as a Bonus Action with a different Light weapon. You do not add your ability modifier to the damage of that Bonus Action attack unless it is negative.

Mastery: Vex. When you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage, you gain Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

Name: Shovel
Text: By working for 1 hour, you can use this shovel to dig a 5‑foot‑by‑5‑foot‑by‑5‑foot hole in soil or similar material.

Name: Sickle
Text:
Light. When you take the Attack action and attack with a Light weapon, you may make one extra attack as a Bonus Action with a different Light weapon. You do not add your ability modifier to the damage of that Bonus Action attack unless it is negative.

Mastery: Nick. When you make the extra attack granted by the Light property, you may instead make it as part of the Attack action. You can use this extra attack only once per turn.

Name: Signal Whistle
Text: When used as a Utilize action, this whistle emits a piercing sound that can be heard up to 600 feet away.

Name: Silk
Text: A fine and valuable textile often traded directly between merchants without the need for coin.

Name: Silk Vestments with Gold Embroidery
Text: These ornate silk garments, decorated with gold thread, belong to the broader category of precious art objects such as jeweled ornaments, royal portraits, and ceremonial treasures.

Name: Silver
Text: A commonly traded precious metal often exchanged directly without the use of currency.

Name: Silver Dragon Scale Mail
Text:
This armor is crafted from the scales of a silver dragon. Some dragons gift their shed scales, while others are harvested from fallen wyrms, making such armor highly prized.

While wearing it, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class, have Advantage on saving throws against Dragon breath weapons, and gain Resistance to Cold damage.

You may also take a Magic action to sense the distance and direction to the nearest silver dragon within 30 miles. This ability recharges at dawn.

The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Silver and Gold Brooch
Text: A finely crafted brooch of silver and gold, belonging to the category of valuable art pieces such as gemstone jewelry, royal portraits, and ornate ceremonial items.

Name: Silver chalice set with moonstones
Text: A finely crafted silver chalice adorned with moonstones, belonging to the broader category of valuable art objects such as jeweled ornaments, royal portraits, and ceremonial treasures.

Name: Silver ewer
Text: A decorative silver vessel often used in ceremonial or noble settings, part of the wide variety of precious art objects.

Name: Silver necklace with a gemstone pendant
Text: A delicate silver necklace featuring a gemstone pendant, representative of the many forms of valuable artistic jewelry.

Name: Silvered Weapon
Text: An alchemical process has fused silver to this weapon. When you score a Critical Hit against a shape‑shifted creature, the weapon deals one additional die of damage.

Name: Sled
Text: A heavy sled designed for transporting goods or passengers across snow or ice.

Name: Sling
Text:
Range. A ranged weapon lists two distances: normal range and long range. Attacks beyond normal range are made with Disadvantage, and you cannot attack beyond long range.

Ammunition. You must have the correct ammunition to make an attack with this weapon. Each attack consumes one piece of ammunition. Drawing ammunition is part of the attack. After combat, you may spend 1 minute to recover half the ammunition you used (rounded down).

Mastery: Slow. When you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage, you may reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. Multiple hits with Slow weapons do not stack beyond a 10‑foot reduction.

Name: Sling Bullet
Text:
Sling bullets are ammunition used with weapons that have the Ammunition property. Each ranged attack consumes one bullet. Drawing ammunition is part of the attack, and you need a free hand to load a one‑handed weapon. After combat, you can spend 1 minute searching to recover half of the bullets you used.

Sling bullets are usually carried in a separate pouch.

Name: Sling Bullets (20)
Text:
A bundle of 20 sling bullets used with weapons that have the Ammunition property. Each attack consumes one bullet. Drawing ammunition is part of the attack, and you need a free hand to load a one‑handed weapon. After a battle, you may spend 1 minute to retrieve half of the bullets you fired.

These bullets are typically stored in a pouch sold separately.

Name: Slippers of Spider Climbing
Text: While wearing these lightweight shoes, you can move along vertical surfaces and ceilings as easily as walking on the ground, keeping your hands free. Your Climb Speed becomes equal to your normal Speed. However, the slippers do not function on slick surfaces such as those coated in ice or oil.

Name: Smith's Tools
Text:
Ability: Strength

Utilize: Pry open a door or container (DC 20)

Craft: Any Melee weapon (except Club, Greatclub, Quarterstaff, and Whip), Armor (except Hide), Ball Bearings, Bucket, Caltrops, Chain, Crowbar, Firearm Bullets, Grappling Hook, Iron Pot, Iron Spikes, Sling Bullets

Artisan's Tools are specialized sets used to craft items and practice trades. Each tool type requires its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with a tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to ability checks using it. If you are also proficient in a related skill, you gain Advantage on the check.

Name: Smoke Grenade
Text: As an action, you may throw this grenade to a point within 60 feet, or launch it up to 1000 feet using a Grenade Launcher. Upon detonation, it fills a 20‑foot‑radius Sphere with thick smoke for 1 minute, creating a Heavily Obscured area. A strong wind—such as from the Gust of Wind spell—disperses the smoke.

Name: Smoldering Armor
Text: Thin wisps of harmless, scentless smoke drift from this armor while it is worn, giving it a subtly dramatic appearance.

Name: Sovereign Glue
Text:
This thick, milky-white adhesive permanently bonds any two objects together. It must be kept in a container coated internally with Oil of Slipperiness. A found container holds 1d6 + 1 ounces.

One ounce covers a 1-foot-square area. Applying an ounce requires a Utilize action, and the glue hardens after 1 minute. Once set, the bond can be undone only with Universal Solvent, Oil of Etherealness, or a Wish spell.

Name: Spear
Text:
Thrown. A weapon with the Thrown property can be thrown to make a ranged attack, using the same ability modifier you would use for a melee attack with it.

Versatile. This weapon can be used with one or two hands. The damage in parentheses applies when used with two hands.

Mastery: Sap. When you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before your next turn begins.

Name: Spell Scroll
Text:
This item exists in multiple versions, each containing a single spell:

Spell Scroll (Cantrip)
Spell Scroll (Level 1)
Spell Scroll (Level 2)
Spell Scroll (Level 3)
Spell Scroll (Level 4)
Spell Scroll (Level 5)
Spell Scroll (Level 6)
Spell Scroll (Level 7)
Spell Scroll (Level 8)
Spell Scroll (Level 9)

Name: Spell Scroll (Cantrip)
Text:
A Spell Scroll contains a single spell written in arcane script. If the spell is on your spell list, you can read the scroll to cast it without Material components. Casting requires the spell’s normal casting time. After casting, the scroll turns to dust unless the casting is interrupted.

If the spell is on your list but higher level than you can normally cast, you must succeed on a DC 10 ability check using your spellcasting ability. On a failure, the spell vanishes with no effect.

If the spell requires a saving throw or attack roll, the save DC is 13 and the attack bonus is +5.

Name: Spell Scroll (Level 1)
Text:
