A Spell Scroll contains a single spell written in magical cipher. If the spell is on your spell list, you can cast it by reading the scroll, ignoring Material components. The scroll crumbles after the spell is cast unless the casting is interrupted.

If the spell is on your list but of a higher level than you can normally cast, you must succeed on a DC 11 ability check using your spellcasting ability. On a failure, the spell disappears harmlessly.

If the spell requires a saving throw or attack roll, the save DC is 13 and the attack bonus is +5.

Copying a Scroll into a Spellbook. A Wizard can copy a level 1 spell from a Spell Scroll into a spellbook by succeeding on a DC 11 Intelligence (Arcana) check. Whether the check succeeds or fails, the scroll is destroyed.

Name: Spell Scroll (Level 2)
Text:
A Spell Scroll contains a single spell written in arcane cipher. If the spell appears on your spell list, you can read the scroll to cast it without needing Material components. Otherwise, the writing is incomprehensible. Casting the spell requires its usual casting time. Once cast, the scroll disintegrates into dust unless the casting is interrupted.

If the spell is on your list but of a higher level than you can normally cast, you must succeed on a DC 12 ability check using your spellcasting ability. On a failure, the spell vanishes from the scroll with no further effect.

If the spell calls for a saving throw or attack roll, the save DC is 13 and the attack bonus is +5.

Copying a Scroll into a Spellbook. A Wizard spell on a Spell Scroll can be copied into a spellbook. Copying a level 2 spell requires a successful DC 12 Intelligence (Arcana) check. Whether the check succeeds or fails, the scroll is destroyed.

Name: Spell Scroll (Level 3)
Text:
This scroll bears a single spell inscribed in magical shorthand. If the spell is on your spell list, you may cast it by reading the scroll, ignoring Material components. If not, the writing is unintelligible. Casting the spell requires its normal casting time, and the scroll crumbles afterward unless the casting is interrupted.

If the spell is on your list but of a higher level than you can normally cast, you must succeed on a DC 13 ability check using your spellcasting ability. On a failure, the spell disappears harmlessly.

If the spell requires a saving throw or attack roll, the save DC is 15 and the attack bonus is +7.

Copying a Scroll into a Spellbook. A Wizard can copy a level 3 spell from a Spell Scroll by succeeding on a DC 13 Intelligence (Arcana) check. Regardless of success or failure, the scroll is destroyed.

Name: Spell Scroll (Level 4)
Text:
This scroll contains a single spell written in mystical cipher. If the spell is on your spell list, you may cast it by reading the scroll without needing Material components. Otherwise, the scroll’s writing is indecipherable. Casting the spell uses its normal casting time, and afterward the scroll turns to dust unless the casting is interrupted.

If the spell is on your list but of a higher level than you can normally cast, you must succeed on a DC 14 ability check using your spellcasting ability. On a failure, the spell vanishes with no effect.

If the spell requires a saving throw or attack roll, the save DC is 15 and the attack bonus is +7.

Copying a Scroll into a Spellbook. A Wizard can copy a level 4 spell from a Spell Scroll by succeeding on a DC 14 Intelligence (Arcana) check. Whether the check succeeds or fails, the scroll is destroyed.

Name: Spell Scroll (Level 5)
Text:
This scroll contains a single spell written in arcane shorthand. If the spell is on your spell list, you may cast it by reading the scroll, ignoring Material components. Otherwise, the scroll’s writing cannot be understood. Casting the spell requires its usual casting time, and the scroll disintegrates afterward unless the casting is interrupted.

If the spell is on your list but of a higher level than you can normally cast, you must succeed on a DC 15 ability check using your spellcasting ability. On a failure, the spell disappears from the scroll with no other effect.

If the spell requires a saving throw or attack roll, the save DC is 17 and the attack bonus is +9.

Copying a Scroll into a Spellbook. A Wizard can copy a level 5 spell from a Spell Scroll by succeeding on a DC 15 Intelligence (Arcana) check. Whether the check succeeds or fails, the scroll is destroyed.

Name: Spell Scroll (Level 6)
Text:
A Spell Scroll contains a single spell written in arcane cipher. If the spell is on your spell list, you can cast it by reading the scroll without needing Material components. Otherwise, the writing is incomprehensible. Casting the spell requires its normal casting time, and once cast, the scroll turns to dust unless the casting is interrupted.

If the spell is on your list but of a higher level than you can normally cast, you must succeed on a DC 16 ability check using your spellcasting ability. On a failure, the spell disappears from the scroll with no further effect.

If the spell requires a saving throw or attack roll, the save DC is 17 and the attack bonus is +9.

Copying a Scroll into a Spellbook. A Wizard can copy a level 6 spell from a Spell Scroll by succeeding on a DC 16 Intelligence (Arcana) check. Whether the check succeeds or fails, the scroll is destroyed.

Name: Spell Scroll (Level 7)
Text:
This scroll bears a single spell written in mystical shorthand. If the spell is on your spell list, you may cast it by reading the scroll, ignoring Material components. Otherwise, the scroll is unreadable. Casting the spell requires its usual casting time, and afterward the scroll crumbles unless the casting is interrupted.

If the spell is on your list but of a higher level than you can normally cast, you must succeed on a DC 17 ability check using your spellcasting ability. On a failure, the spell vanishes harmlessly.

If the spell requires a saving throw or attack roll, the save DC is 18 and the attack bonus is +10.

Copying a Scroll into a Spellbook. A Wizard can copy a level 7 spell from a Spell Scroll by succeeding on a DC 17 Intelligence (Arcana) check. Whether the check succeeds or fails, the scroll is destroyed.

Name: Spell Scroll (Level 8)
Text:
This scroll contains a single spell written in arcane cipher. If the spell appears on your spell list, you may cast it by reading the scroll without needing Material components. Otherwise, the writing is indecipherable. Casting the spell requires its normal casting time, and the scroll disintegrates afterward unless the casting is interrupted.

If the spell is on your list but of a higher level than you can normally cast, you must succeed on a DC 18 ability check using your spellcasting ability. On a failure, the spell disappears with no effect.

If the spell requires a saving throw or attack roll, the save DC is 18 and the attack bonus is +10.

Copying a Scroll into a Spellbook. A Wizard can copy a level 8 spell from a Spell Scroll by succeeding on a DC 18 Intelligence (Arcana) check. Whether the check succeeds or fails, the scroll is destroyed.

Name: Spell Scroll (Level 9)
Text:
This scroll contains a single spell written in mystical cipher. If the spell is on your spell list, you may cast it by reading the scroll without requiring Material components. Otherwise, the scroll’s writing is unintelligible. Casting the spell requires its normal casting time, and afterward the scroll crumbles unless the casting is interrupted.

If the spell is on your list but of a higher level than you can normally cast, you must succeed on a DC 19 ability check using your spellcasting ability. On a failure, the spell disappears from the scroll with no further effect.

If the spell requires a saving throw or attack roll, the save DC is 19 and the attack bonus is +11.

Copying a Scroll into a Spellbook. A Wizard can copy a level 9 spell from a Spell Scroll by succeeding on a DC 19 Intelligence (Arcana) check. Whether the check succeeds or fails, the scroll is destroyed.

Name: Spellguard Shield
Text: While wielding this shield, you gain Advantage on saving throws made against spells and other magical effects, and spell attack rolls made against you are made with Disadvantage.

Name: Sphere of Annihilation
Text:
This 2‑foot‑wide black sphere is a void in reality, suspended in place by a stabilizing magical field.

The sphere destroys all matter it touches or that passes through it, except for Artifacts that are immune to its effects. Anything that touches the sphere without being fully consumed takes 8d10 Force damage.

Controlling the Sphere. The sphere remains motionless until a creature attempts to control it. If you are within 60 feet, you may take a Magic action to make a DC 25 Intelligence (Arcana) check. On a success, you control the sphere until the start of your next turn, overriding any previous controller. On a failure, the sphere moves 10 feet directly toward you.

While controlling the sphere, you may use a Bonus Action to move it in a direction of your choice, up to a distance equal to 5 × your Intelligence modifier (minimum 5 feet). Any creature whose space the sphere enters must succeed on a DC 19 Dexterity saving throw or be touched by it, taking 8d10 Force damage. A creature reduced to 0 Hit Points by this damage is completely annihilated, leaving behind only its possessions.

Sphere Interactions. When the sphere contacts a planar portal (such as one created by the Gate spell) or an extradimensional space (such as a Portable Hole), roll on the following table:

1d100 | Result
01–50 | The sphere is destroyed.
51–85 | The sphere passes through the portal or into the extradimensional space.
86–00 | A spatial rupture transports the sphere and all creatures and objects within 180 feet to a random plane of existence.

Name: Spinel
Text:
A gemstone that may appear in vivid red, reddish‑brown, or deep green hues.

Gemstones are compact, durable, and easy to transport compared to coins of equal value.

Name: Spirit Board
Text:
This elaborately crafted wooden board displays the letters of the Common alphabet, along with the words "Yes" and "No" and symbols for "Weal" and "Woe." It includes a heart‑shaped wooden planchette, which must rest on the lettered side for the board’s magic to function.

The board holds 3 charges and regains 1 charge each dawn. While touching the planchette, you can spend 1 minute to cast one of the spells listed below, expending the indicated number of charges. As you cast, you call upon lingering spirits to guide the planchette across the board, spelling out answers.

Spell | Charge Cost
Augury | 1
Commune | 3

Name: Splint Armor
Text:
The wearer suffers Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

If the wearer’s Strength score is below 15, their Speed is reduced by 10 feet.

Name: Sprig of Mistletoe
Text: A Druidic Focus shaped or decorated to channel primal magic. Druids and Rangers can use it as a Spellcasting Focus.

Name: Spyglass
Text: Objects viewed through this device appear magnified to twice their actual size.

Name: Stabling (per day)
Text: Covers the daily cost of housing and caring for a mount or similar creature.

Name: Staff
Text:
Versatile. This weapon can be wielded with one or two hands. The damage in parentheses applies when used with two hands.

An Arcane Focus is crafted or adorned to channel arcane power. Sorcerers, Warlocks, and Wizards may use it as a Spellcasting Focus.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can force it to make a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + the ability modifier used for the attack + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature becomes Prone.

Name: Staff of Adornment
Text:
When you place a Tiny object weighing 1 pound or less (such as a crystal shard, egg, or pebble) above the staff’s tip while holding it, the object floats an inch above the staff. Up to three objects may float at once. While holding the staff, you can cause the objects to slowly rotate or turn.

Versatile. This weapon can be used with one or two hands. The damage in parentheses applies when used with two hands.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + the ability modifier used for the attack + your Proficiency Bonus) or fall Prone.

Name: Staff of Birdcalls
Text:
This wooden staff is carved with bird motifs and holds 10 charges. While holding it, you may take a Magic action to expend 1 charge and produce one of several bird sounds audible up to 120 feet: finch chirp, raven caw, duck quack, chicken cluck, goose honk, loon call, turkey gobble, seagull cry, owl hoot, or eagle shriek.

Regaining Charges. The staff regains 1d6 + 4 charges at dawn. If you expend the final charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the staff bursts into harmless feathers and is destroyed.

Versatile. This weapon can be used with one or two hands. The damage in parentheses applies when used with two hands.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + the ability modifier used for the attack + your Proficiency Bonus) or fall Prone.

Name: Staff of Charming
Attunement: Requires Attunement By a Bard, Cleric, Druid, Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard
Text:
This staff contains 10 charges. While holding it, you may use the following abilities:

Cast Spell. Spend 1 charge to cast Charm Person, Command, or Comprehend Languages using your spell save DC.

Reflect Enchantment. If you succeed on a saving throw against an Enchantment spell that targets only you, you may use a Reaction and 1 charge to turn the spell back on its caster.

Resist Enchantment. If you fail a saving throw against an Enchantment spell that targets only you, you may turn the failure into a success. This property cannot be used again until the next dawn.

Regaining Charges. The staff regains 1d8 + 2 charges at dawn. If you expend the final charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the staff crumbles into dust.

Versatile. This weapon can be used with one or two hands. The damage in parentheses applies when used with two hands.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + the ability modifier used for the attack + your Proficiency Bonus) or fall Prone.

Name: Staff of Fire
Attunement: Requires Attunement By a Druid, Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard
Text:
While holding this staff, you gain Resistance to Fire damage.

Spells. The staff contains 10 charges. While wielding it, you may cast one of the spells listed below using your spell save DC, expending the indicated number of charges.

Spell | Charge Cost
Burning Hands | 1
Fireball | 3
Wall of Fire | 4

Regaining Charges. The staff regains 1d6 + 4 charges at dawn. If you expend the final charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the staff collapses into ashes and is destroyed.

Versatile. This weapon may be used with one or two hands. The damage in parentheses applies when used with two hands.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + the ability modifier used for the attack + your Proficiency Bonus) or fall Prone.

Name: Staff of Flowers
Text:
This wooden staff holds 10 charges. While holding it, you may take a Magic action to expend 1 charge and cause a flower to bloom from soil within 5 feet of you, or from the staff itself. If you do not choose a specific flower, a simple daisy appears. The flower is mundane and behaves as a normal plant.

Regaining Charges. The staff regains 1d6 + 4 charges at dawn. If you expend the final charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the staff dissolves into flower petals and is lost.

Versatile. This weapon may be used with one or two hands. The damage in parentheses applies when used with two hands.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + the ability modifier used for the attack + your Proficiency Bonus) or fall Prone.

Name: Staff of Frost
Attunement: Requires Attunement By a Druid, Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard
Text:
While holding this staff, you gain Resistance to Cold damage.

Spells. The staff contains 10 charges. While holding it, you may cast one of the spells listed below using your spell save DC, expending the indicated number of charges.

Spell | Charge Cost
Cone of Cold | 5
Fog Cloud | 1
Ice Storm | 4
Wall of Ice | 4

Regaining Charges. The staff regains 1d6 + 4 charges at dawn. If you expend the final charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the staff melts into water and is destroyed.

Versatile. This weapon may be used with one or two hands. The damage in parentheses applies when used with two hands.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + the ability modifier used for the attack + your Proficiency Bonus) or fall Prone.

Name: Staff of Healing
Attunement: Requires Attunement By a Bard, Cleric, or Druid
Text:
This staff contains 10 charges. While holding it, you may cast one of the spells listed below using your spellcasting ability modifier, expending the indicated number of charges.

Spell | Charge Cost
Cure Wounds | 1 charge per spell level (maximum 4)
Lesser Restoration | 2
Mass Cure Wounds | 5

Regaining Charges. The staff regains 1d6 + 4 charges at dawn. If you expend the final charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the staff vanishes in a burst of light.

Versatile. This weapon may be used with one or two hands. The damage in parentheses applies when used with two hands.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + the ability modifier used for the attack + your Proficiency Bonus) or fall Prone.

Name: Staff of Power
Attunement: Requires Attunement By a Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard
Text:
This staff holds 20 charges and functions as a magic Quarterstaff that grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls. While you wield it, you also gain a +2 bonus to Armor Class, saving throws, and spell attack rolls.

Spells. While holding the staff, you may cast one of the spells listed below using your spell save DC, expending the indicated number of charges.

Spell | Charge Cost
Cone of Cold | 5
Fireball (5th level) | 5
Globe of Invulnerability | 6
Hold Monster | 5
Levitate | 2
Lightning Bolt (5th level) | 5
Magic Missile | 1
Ray of Enfeeblement | 1
Wall of Force | 5

Regaining Charges. The staff regains 2d8 + 4 charges at dawn. If you expend the final charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the staff keeps only its +2 attack and damage bonus and loses all other properties. On a 20, it regains 1d8 + 2 charges.

Retributive Strike. As a Magic action, you may break the staff, destroying it and releasing its stored power in a 30‑foot Emanation. You have a 50% chance to instantly travel to a random plane, avoiding the blast. Otherwise, you take Force damage equal to 16 × the number of charges in the staff. Each other creature in the area must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw, taking Force damage equal to 4 × the number of charges on a failed save, or half as much on a success.

Versatile. When used with two hands, the staff deals the damage shown in parentheses.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your attack ability modifier + your Proficiency Bonus) or fall Prone.

Name: Staff of Striking
Text:
This staff can be used as a magic Quarterstaff that grants a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

The staff contains 10 charges. When you hit a target with a melee attack using it, you may expend up to 3 charges. For each charge spent, the target takes an additional 1d6 Force damage.

Regaining Charges. The staff regains 1d6 + 4 charges at dawn. If you expend the final charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, the staff becomes a mundane Quarterstaff.

Versatile. When used with two hands, the staff deals the damage shown in parentheses.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your attack ability modifier + your Proficiency Bonus) or fall Prone.

Name: Staff of Swarming Insects
Attunement: Requires Attunement By a Bard, Cleric, Druid, Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard
Text:
This staff contains 10 charges.

Insect Cloud. While holding the staff, you may take a Magic action and expend 1 charge to summon a harmless swarm of flying insects that fills a 30‑foot Emanation centered on you for 10 minutes. The area becomes Heavily Obscured for all creatures except you. A strong wind, such as from Gust of Wind, disperses the swarm.

Spells. While holding the staff, you may cast one of the spells listed below using your spell save DC and spell attack modifier, expending the indicated number of charges.

Spell | Charge Cost
Giant Insect | 4
Insect Plague | 5

Regaining Charges. The staff regains 1d6 + 4 charges at dawn. If you expend the final charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, a swarm of insects devours the staff and then scatters.

Versatile. When used with two hands, the staff deals the damage shown in parentheses.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your attack ability modifier + your Proficiency Bonus) or fall Prone.

Name: Staff of Thunder and Lightning
Text:
This staff functions as a magic Quarterstaff that grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls. It also possesses several special abilities, each usable once per dawn.

Lightning. When you hit a creature with a melee attack using the staff, you may cause it to take an additional 2d6 Lightning damage (no action required).

Thunder. When you hit a creature with a melee attack using the staff, you may cause it to emit a thunderous crack audible out to 300 feet (no action required). The target must succeed on a DC 17 Constitution saving throw or become Stunned until the end of your next turn.

Thunder and Lightning. Immediately after hitting with a melee attack, you may take a Bonus Action to activate both the Lightning and Thunder properties simultaneously. This does not expend their individual daily uses, only this combined one.

Lightning Strike. As a Magic action, you unleash a bolt of lightning in a 5‑foot‑wide, 120‑foot‑long Line. Each creature in the Line must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw, taking 9d6 Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

Thunderclap. As a Magic action, you create a thunderous blast audible out to 600 feet. Each creature within a 60‑foot Emanation centered on you must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes 2d6 Thunder damage and is Deafened for 1 minute; on a success, it takes half damage and is not Deafened.

Versatile. When used with two hands, the staff deals the damage listed in parentheses.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your attack ability modifier + your Proficiency Bonus) or fall Prone.

Name: Staff of Withering
Attunement: Requires Attunement By a Cleric, Druid, or Warlock
Text:
This staff contains 3 charges and regains 1d3 charges at dawn.

It can be used as a magic Quarterstaff. On a hit, it deals normal Quarterstaff damage, and you may expend 1 charge to deal an additional 2d10 Necrotic damage and force the target to make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target has Disadvantage for 1 hour on all Strength and Constitution checks and saving throws.

Versatile. When used with two hands, the staff deals the damage listed in parentheses.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your attack ability modifier + your Proficiency Bonus) or fall Prone.

Name: Staff of the Adder
Text:
As a Bonus Action, you may transform the head of the staff into a living, venomous serpent for 1 minute, or revert it to its inert form.

When you take the Attack action, you may make one attack using the animated snake head, which has a 5‑foot reach. Use your Proficiency Bonus and Wisdom modifier for the attack roll. On a hit, the target takes 1d6 Piercing damage plus 3d6 Poison damage.

The snake head can be targeted while animated. It has AC 15, 20 Hit Points, and Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage. If reduced to 0 HP, the staff is destroyed. If not destroyed, it regains all lost HP when reverting to its inert form.

Versatile. When used with two hands, the staff deals the damage listed in parentheses.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your attack ability modifier + your Proficiency Bonus) or fall Prone.

Name: Staff of the Magi
Attunement: Requires Attunement By a Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard
Text:
This staff contains 50 charges and functions as a magic Quarterstaff that grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls. While holding it, you also gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls.

Spell Absorption. While holding the staff, you have Advantage on saving throws against spells. When a spell targets only you, you may use a Reaction to absorb it, negating its effect and adding charges equal to the spell’s level. If this would raise the staff above 50 charges, it detonates as if you triggered its Retributive Strike.

Spells. While holding the staff, you may cast any of the spells listed below using your spell save DC, expending the indicated number of charges.

Spell | Charge Cost
Arcane Lock | 0
Conjure Elemental | 7
Detect Magic | 0
Dispel Magic | 3
Enlarge/Reduce | 0
Fireball (7th level) | 7
Flaming Sphere | 2
Ice Storm | 4
Invisibility | 2
Knock | 2
Light | 0
Lightning Bolt (7th level) | 7
Mage Hand | 0
Passwall | 5
Plane Shift | 7
Protection from Evil and Good | 0
Telekinesis | 5
Wall of Fire | 4
Web | 2

Regaining Charges. The staff regains 4d6 + 2 charges at dawn. If you expend the final charge, roll 1d20. On a 20, it regains 1d12 + 1 charges.

Retributive Strike. As a Magic action, you may break the staff, destroying it and releasing its stored energy in a 30‑foot Emanation. You have a 50% chance to instantly travel to a random plane, avoiding the blast. Otherwise, you take Force damage equal to 16 × the number of charges in the staff. All other creatures in the area must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw, taking Force damage equal to 6 × the number of charges on a failed save, or half on a success.

Versatile. When used with two hands, the staff deals the damage listed in parentheses.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your attack ability modifier + your Proficiency Bonus) or fall Prone.

Name: Staff of the Python
Text:
As a Magic action, you can throw this staff into an unoccupied space within 10 feet, where it transforms into a Giant Constrictor Snake. The snake obeys your commands and shares your Initiative, acting immediately after your turn.

On your turn, if the snake is within 60 feet and you aren’t Incapacitated, you can mentally direct it (no action required). You choose its movement and actions, or give it a general order such as attacking or guarding. Without commands, it simply defends itself.

As a Bonus Action, you can order the snake to revert to staff form in its current space. After doing so, you can’t use this property again for 1 hour. If the snake drops to 0 Hit Points, it dies, reverts to a staff, and the staff shatters. If it returns to staff form before reaching 0 HP, it regains all lost Hit Points.

Versatile. When used with two hands, the staff deals the damage shown in parentheses.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your attack ability modifier + your Proficiency Bonus) or fall Prone.

Name: Staff of the Woodlands
Text:
This staff holds 6 charges and can be used as a magic Quarterstaff that grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls. While holding it, you also gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls.

Spells. While wielding the staff, you may cast one of the spells listed below using your spell save DC, expending the indicated number of charges.

Spell | Charge Cost
Animal Friendship | 1
Awaken | 5
Barkskin | 2
Locate Animals or Plants | 2
Pass without Trace | 2
Speak with Animals | 1
Speak with Plants | 3
Wall of Thorns | 6

Tree Form. As a Magic action, you may plant the staff’s end into earth in an unoccupied space and expend 1 charge to transform it into a living tree. The tree is 60 feet tall with a 5‑foot‑diameter trunk and a 20‑foot canopy. It appears natural but radiates faint Transmutation magic detectable with Detect Magic. While touching the tree, you may use a Magic action to return it to staff form. Any creature in the tree falls when it reverts.

Regaining Charges. The staff regains 1d6 charges at dawn. If you expend the final charge, roll 1d20. On a 1, it becomes a mundane Quarterstaff.

Versatile. When used with two hands, the staff deals the damage shown in parentheses.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your attack ability modifier + your Proficiency Bonus) or fall Prone.

Name: Star Ruby
Text:
A ruby displaying a bright white star-shaped pattern at its center.

Gemstones are compact, durable, and easier to transport than coins of equal worth.

Name: Star Sapphire
Text:
A deep blue sapphire featuring a white star-shaped pattern at its core.

Gemstones are compact, durable, and easier to transport than coins of equal worth.

Name: Star Rose Quartz
Text:
A pink-hued quartz with a white star-shaped center.

Gemstones are compact, durable, and easier to transport than coins of equal worth.

Name: Stone of Controlling Earth Elementals
Text: When you press this 5‑pound stone against the ground, you may take a Magic action to summon an Earth Elemental. It appears in an unoccupied space of your choice within 30 feet, follows your commands, and acts immediately after you in the Initiative order. The elemental remains for 1 hour, until it dies, or until you dismiss it as a Bonus Action. This property can’t be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Stone of Good Luck
Text: While you carry this polished agate on your person, you gain a +1 bonus to all ability checks and saving throws.

Name: String
Text: This 10‑foot length of string can be knotted as a Utilize action.

Name: Studded Leather Armor
Text: A reinforced leather armor fitted with metal studs for added protection.

Name: Sun Blade
Text:
This item resembles a simple sword hilt.

Blade of Radiance. While holding the hilt, you may take a Bonus Action to manifest or dismiss a blade of radiant energy. While active, this magic weapon functions as a Finesse Longsword. If you are proficient with Longswords or Shortswords, you are proficient with the Sun Blade.

You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls with this weapon, which deals Radiant damage instead of Slashing. When you hit an Undead, it takes an additional 1d8 Radiant damage.

Sunlight. The glowing blade sheds Bright Light in a 15‑foot radius and Dim Light for another 15 feet. This illumination counts as sunlight. While the blade exists, you may take a Magic action to increase or decrease both radii by 5 feet each, to a maximum of 30 feet or a minimum of 10 feet.

Finesse. You may use Strength or Dexterity for attack and damage rolls, but must use the same ability for both.

Versatile. When used with two hands, the weapon deals the damage shown in parentheses.

Mastery: Sap. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before your next turn begins.

Name: Sword of Answering
Text:
You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this blade. Additionally, while holding it, you may use a Reaction to make a melee attack against any creature within your reach that deals damage to you. You have Advantage on this attack roll, and the damage you deal with this special strike ignores any Immunity or Resistance the target has to that damage type.

Versatile. This weapon can be wielded with one or two hands. The damage in parentheses applies when used with two hands.

Mastery: Sap. When you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before your next turn begins.

Name: Sword of Kass
Text:
Kass was once a mighty champion who served Vekna, rewarded with this blade. As Kass’s ambition grew, the sword urged him to overthrow Vekna. Legends claim Kass struck down Vekna, but the lich destroyed Kass in turn, leaving only the sword behind.

Bloodthirst. The sword craves bloodshed. If it does not draw blood within 1 minute of being unsheathed, its wielder must make a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success, the wielder takes 3d6 Psychic damage. On a failure, the wielder becomes dominated by the sword as if affected by *Dominate Monster*, compelled to spill blood. The effect ends once the sword’s demand is met.

Magic Weapon. You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls with this sword. It scores a Critical Hit on a 19 or 20 and deals an extra 2d10 Slashing damage to Undead.

Random Properties. The sword has:
1 minor beneficial property
1 major beneficial property
1 minor detrimental property
1 major detrimental property

Spells. While carrying the sword, you may cast the following spells from it (save DC 18):
*Call Lightning*, *Divine Word*, *Finger of Death*. Each spell may be cast once per dawn.

Spirit of Kass. While the sword is on your person, you gain:

Battle Hunger. Add 1d10 to your Initiative rolls.

Blade of Defense. When you take the Attack action with the sword, you may shift some or all of its attack bonus to your Armor Class until the start of your next turn.

Necrotic Resistance. You gain Resistance to Necrotic damage.

Sentience. The Sword of Kass is a sentient Chaotic Evil weapon with Intelligence 15, Wisdom 13, and Charisma 16. It has hearing and Darkvision out to 120 feet. It communicates telepathically and speaks Common.

Personality. The sword’s purpose is to bring ruin to Vekna. It seeks the destruction of Vekna’s followers, works, and schemes, and also desires the downfall of anyone corrupted by the Eye or Hand of Vekna.

Destroying the Sword. A creature attuned to both the Eye of Vekna and the Hand of Vekna may use the Wish property of those Artifacts to destroy the sword if it is within 30 feet. After casting *Wish*, the creature must make a DC 18 Charisma saving throw. On a failure, nothing happens. On a success, the Sword of Kass is unmade.

Versatile. When used with two hands, the weapon deals the damage listed in parentheses.

Mastery: Sap. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before your next turn.

Name: Sword of Life Stealing
Text: When you hit a creature with this magic weapon and roll a natural 20 on the attack roll, the target takes an additional 15 Necrotic damage if it is neither a Construct nor an Undead. You also gain Temporary Hit Points equal to the Necrotic damage dealt.

Name: Sword of Sharpness
Text:
When you strike an object with this enchanted blade and hit, you maximize the weapon’s damage dice against that object.

When you hit a creature with this weapon and roll a natural 20 on the attack roll, the target suffers an additional 14 Slashing damage and gains 1 level of Exhaustion.

Name: Sword of Vengeance
Text:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magical blade.

Curse. A vengeful spirit haunts this weapon. Attuning to it extends the curse to you. While cursed, you refuse to part with the weapon and keep it on your person at all times. You also have Disadvantage on attack rolls made with any weapon other than this one.

Additionally, whenever you take damage from a creature during combat, you must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, you are compelled to attack the creature that harmed you until either you or the creature drops to 0 Hit Points, or until you are unable to reach it.

The curse can be ended normally. Alternatively, casting *Banishment* on the weapon expels the spirit, turning the blade into a simple +1 weapon with no further properties.

Name: Sword of Wounding
Text: When you hit a creature with this enchanted weapon, it takes an additional 2d6 Necrotic damage and must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be unable to regain Hit Points for 1 hour. The creature may repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

Name: Sylvan Talon
Text:
While carrying this weapon, you can understand the nonverbal communication of all Fey creatures, and they can understand yours.

Secret Message. As a Magic action, you may use the weapon to cast *Message*. Once used, this property cannot be used again until the next dawn.

Name: Talisman of Pure Good
Attunement: Requires Attunement By a Cleric or Paladin
Text:
This talisman radiates powerful divine purity. Any Fiend or Undead that touches it takes 8d6 Radiant damage, and takes the same damage again at the end of each of its turns while holding or carrying it.

Holy Symbol. You may use the talisman as a Holy Symbol, gaining a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls while wearing or holding it.

Pure Rebuke. The talisman contains 7 charges. While wearing or holding it, you may take a Magic action to expend 1 charge and target a creature you can see on the ground within 120 feet. A blazing fissure opens beneath the target, which must make a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw. Fiends and Undead have Disadvantage on this save. On a failed save, the target is swallowed by the fissure and destroyed without leaving remains. On a successful save, the target avoids falling but suffers 4d6 Psychic damage. The fissure then seals itself completely. When the final charge is used, the talisman dissolves into golden motes of light and is destroyed.

Name: Talisman of Ultimate Evil
Text:
This talisman embodies absolute malevolence. Any creature that is not a Fiend or Undead takes 8d6 Necrotic damage upon touching it, and takes the same damage again at the end of each of its turns while holding or carrying it.

Holy Symbol. You may use the talisman as a Holy Symbol, gaining a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls while wearing or holding it.

Ultimate End. The talisman holds 6 charges. While wearing or holding it, you may take a Magic action to expend 1 charge and target a creature you can see on the ground within 120 feet. A fiery rift opens beneath the target, which must make a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw. Celestials have Disadvantage on this save. On a failed save, the target is cast into the fissure and annihilated. On a successful save, it avoids falling but suffers 4d6 Psychic damage. The fissure then vanishes without a trace. When the final charge is used, the talisman melts into foul-smelling sludge and is destroyed.

Name: Talisman of the Sphere
Text:
While wearing or holding this talisman, you gain Advantage on Intelligence (Arcana) checks made to control a Sphere of Annihilation.

Additionally, at the start of your turn while controlling a Sphere of Annihilation, you may take a Magic action to move it 10 feet plus an additional distance equal to 10 × your Intelligence modifier. This movement does not need to follow a straight line.

Name: Talking Doll
Text: While this doll remains within 5 feet of you, you may spend a Short Rest teaching it up to six phrases, each no longer than six words, and assign a condition that triggers each phrase. You may also replace older phrases with new ones. The triggering condition must occur within 5 feet of the doll for it to speak. For example, if someone picks up the doll, it might say, "I want a piece of candy." All stored phrases vanish when your Attunement to the doll ends.

Name: Tankard of Sobriety
Text: This tankard bears a stern sculpted face on one side. Any nonmagical alcoholic drink poured into it can be consumed without causing intoxication. Magical beverages or harmful substances such as poison are unaffected by the tankard's magic.

Name: Tent
Text: A tent provides shelter for up to two Small or Medium creatures.

Name: Tentacle Rod
Text: This rod ends in three flexible tentacles. While holding it, you may take a Magic action to command each tentacle to lash out at a creature you can see within 15 feet. For each tentacle, make a melee attack roll with a +9 bonus. A hit deals 1d6 Psychic damage. If all three tentacles hit the same target, it must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or become Restrained until you become Incapacitated, you use a Bonus Action to release it, or it moves beyond 15 feet of you. While Restrained in this way, the creature takes 3d6 Psychic damage at the start of each of its turns. At the end of each of its turns, it repeats the save, ending the effect on a success.

Name: Thieves' Tools
Text:
Ability: Dexterity

Utilize: Pick a lock (DC 15) or disarm a trap (DC 15).

If you are proficient with these tools, add your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check that uses them. If you are also proficient in a relevant skill used for the check, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Three-Dragon Ante Set
Text:
Ability: Wisdom

Utilize: Determine if another player is cheating (DC 10), or attempt to win the game (DC 20).

If you are proficient with this tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any related ability check. If you are also proficient in a skill tied to that check, you gain Advantage on the roll.

Name: Thunderous Greatclub
Text:
While attuned to this enchanted club, your Strength becomes 20 unless it is already 20 or higher. The weapon deals an extra 1d8 Thunder damage to creatures it hits, and an additional 3d8 Thunder damage to unattended objects.

The weapon also grants the following abilities:

Clap of Thunder. As a Magic action, you may slam the weapon against a solid surface, producing a thunderous boom audible out to 300 feet and creating a 30‑foot Cone of thunderous force. Each creature in the Cone must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or fall Prone. Nonmagical, unattended objects in the Cone take 3d8 Thunder damage.

Earthquake. As a Magic action, you may strike the ground to unleash a violent tremor in a 50‑foot‑radius circle centered on the impact point. Structures touching the ground take 50 Bludgeoning damage. Each creature on the ground must succeed on a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw or fall Prone. A creature Concentrating must also succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw or lose Concentration. You may also create a fissure 30 feet deep and 10 feet wide anywhere in the area. Creatures on that spot must make a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw, falling in on a failure or moving with the edge on a success. Structures on that spot collapse into the fissure. This property can’t be used again until the next dawn.

Two‑Handed. This weapon requires two hands to make attacks.

Mastery: Push. When you hit a Large or smaller creature with this weapon, you may push it up to 10 feet directly away from you.

Name: Tiger Eye
Text:
A brown gemstone with a golden, reflective center.

Gemstones are compact, durable, and easier to transport than coins of equal value.

Name: Tinderbox
Text: A small kit containing flint, steel, and tinder—typically oiled cloth—used to start fires. Lighting a Candle, Lamp, Lantern, Torch, or any exposed‑fuel source takes a Bonus Action. Lighting any other fire requires 1 minute.

Name: Tinker's Tools
Text:
Ability: Dexterity

Utilize: Construct a Tiny object from scrap materials that falls apart after 1 minute (DC 20).

Craft: Musket, Pistol, Bell, Bullseye Lantern, Flask, Hooded Lantern, Hunting Trap, Lock, Manacles, Mirror, Shovel, Signal Whistle, Tinderbox.

Artisan's Tools are specialized sets used for crafting and trade work. Each type requires its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with these tools, add your Proficiency Bonus to any related ability check. If you are also proficient in a relevant skill, you gain Advantage on the check.

Name: Tome of Clear Thought
Text: This volume is filled with mental‑training exercises and logical challenges, its pages infused with arcane power. If you devote 48 hours within a span of 6 days or fewer to studying its teachings and practicing its methods, your Intelligence score increases by 2, up to a maximum of 30. Afterward, the tome’s magic fades, but it regains its power after a century.

Name: Tome of Leadership and Influence
Text: This book contains lessons on persuasion, presence, and social mastery, its words imbued with enchantment. If you spend 48 hours over no more than 6 days studying its content and applying its techniques, your Charisma score increases by 2, to a maximum of 30. Once used, the tome becomes mundane until its magic returns in a hundred years.

Name: Tome of Understanding
Text: This book offers exercises in perception, intuition, and inner clarity, its passages suffused with divine insight. If you dedicate 48 hours within a 6‑day period to studying and practicing its guidance, your Wisdom score increases by 2, up to a maximum of 30. Afterward, the tome’s magic fades, returning only after a century has passed.

Name: Tome of the Stilled Tongue
Text:
This eerie tome bears a dried tongue nailed to its cover. Five such books are known to exist, though which one is the original remains uncertain. The tongue on the first tome belonged to a traitorous servant of the lich Vekna, while the tongues on the four replicas were taken from other spellcasters who defied him. The opening pages contain unreadable scribbles, and the rest are blank.

While attuned to the tome, you may use it as both a Spellbook and an Arcane Focus. Additionally, while holding it, you can take a Bonus Action to cast a spell inscribed in the tome without expending a spell slot or requiring Verbal or Somatic components. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.

Only you can detach the tongue from the cover. Doing so permanently erases all spells written within.

Vekna observes anyone who uses this tome and may inscribe cryptic messages inside it. These writings usually vanish after being read.

An Arcane Focus is crafted or adorned to channel arcane power. Sorcerers, Warlocks, and Wizards can use such an item as a Spellcasting Focus.

Name: Topaz
Text:
A gemstone of rich golden-yellow coloration.

Gemstones are compact, durable, and easier to transport than coins of equal value.

Name: Torch
Text: A Torch burns for 1 hour, shedding Bright Light in a 20‑foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet. When you take the Attack action, you may strike with the Torch as a Simple Melee weapon. On a hit, it deals 1 Fire damage.

Name: Torpor
Text: A creature exposed to Torpor must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or become Poisoned for 4d6 hours. While Poisoned in this way, the creature’s Speed is reduced by half.

Name: Tourmaline
Text:
A gemstone that may appear pale green, blue, brown, or red.

Gemstones are compact, durable, and easier to transport than coins of equal value.

Name: Traveler's Clothes
Text: Durable clothing designed to withstand long journeys and varied climates.

Name: Trident
Text:
Thrown. A weapon with the Thrown property can be hurled to make a ranged attack, and drawing it is part of that attack. If the weapon is normally used for melee, apply the same ability modifier to attack and damage rolls as you would for a melee strike.

Versatile. This weapon can be wielded with one or two hands. The damage shown in parentheses applies when used with two hands.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must make a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + the ability modifier used for the attack + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature becomes Prone.

Name: Trident of Fish Command
Text:
This enchanted trident holds 3 charges and regains 1d3 charges each dawn. While carrying it, you may expend 1 charge to cast *Dominate Beast* (save DC 15), but only on a Beast with a Swim Speed.

Thrown. A weapon with the Thrown property can be hurled to make a ranged attack, and drawing it is part of that attack. If the weapon is normally used for melee, apply the same ability modifier to attack and damage rolls as you would for a melee strike.

Versatile. This weapon can be wielded with one or two hands. The damage shown in parentheses applies when used with two hands.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must make a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + the ability modifier used for the attack + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature becomes Prone.

Name: Trinket
Text:
When creating your character, you may roll once on the Trinkets table to receive a Tiny trinket—an odd, simple object touched by a hint of mystery. The DM may also use this table to populate rooms, pockets, or treasure hoards.

(Full trinket table preserved exactly as provided.)

Trinkets
1d100  Trinket
01  A mummified goblin hand
02  A crystal that faintly glows in moonlight
03  A gold coin minted in an unknown land
04  A diary written in a language you don't know
05  A brass ring that never tarnishes
06  An old chess piece made from glass
07  A pair of knucklebone dice, each with a skull symbol on the side that would normally show six pips
08  A small idol depicting a nightmarish creature that gives you unsettling dreams when you sleep near it
09  A lock of someone's hair
10  The deed for a parcel of land in a realm unknown to you
11  A 1-ounce block made from an unknown material
12  A small cloth doll skewered with needles
13  A tooth from an unknown beast
14  An enormous scale, perhaps from a dragon
15  A bright-green feather
16  An old divination card bearing your likeness
17  A glass orb filled with moving smoke
18  A 1-pound egg with a bright-red shell
19  A pipe that blows bubbles
20  A glass jar containing a bit of flesh floating in pickling fluid
21  A gnome-crafted music box that plays a song you dimly remember from your childhood
22  A wooden statuette of a smug halfling
23  A brass orb etched with strange runes
24  A multicolored stone disk
25  A silver icon of a raven
26  A bag containing forty-seven teeth, one of which is rotten
27  A shard of obsidian that always feels warm to the touch
28  A dragon's talon strung on a leather necklace
29  A pair of old socks
30  A blank book whose pages refuse to hold ink, chalk, graphite, or any other marking
31  A silver badge that is a five-pointed star
32  A knife that belonged to a relative
33  A glass vial filled with nail clippings
34  A rectangular metal device with two tiny metal cups on one end that throws sparks when wet
35  A white, sequined glove sized for a human
36  A vest with one hundred tiny pockets
37  A weightless stone
38  A sketch of a goblin
39  An empty glass vial that smells of perfume
40  A gemstone that looks like a lump of coal when examined by anyone but you
41  A scrap of cloth from an old banner
42  A rank insignia from a lost legionnaire
43  A silver bell without a clapper
44  A mechanical canary inside a lamp
45  A miniature chest carved to look like it has numerous feet on the bottom
46  A dead sprite inside a clear glass bottle
47  A metal can that has no opening but sounds as if it is filled with liquid, sand, spiders, or broken glass (your choice)
48  A glass orb filled with water, in which swims a clockwork goldfish
49  A silver spoon with an M engraved on the handle
50  A whistle made from gold-colored wood
51  A dead scarab beetle the size of your hand
52  Two toy soldiers, one missing a head
53  A small box filled with different-sized buttons
54  A candle that can't be lit
55  A miniature cage with no door
56  An old key
57  An indecipherable treasure map
58  A hilt from a broken sword
59  A rabbit's foot
60  A glass eye
61  A cameo of a hideous person
62  A silver skull the size of a coin
63  An alabaster mask
64  A cone of sticky black incense that stinks
65  A nightcap that gives you pleasant dreams when you wear it
66  A single caltrop made from bone
67  A gold monocle frame without the lens
68  A 1-inch cube, each side a different color
69  A crystal doorknob
70  A packet filled with pink dust
71  A fragment of a beautiful song, written as musical notes on two pieces of parchment
72  A silver teardrop earring containing a real teardrop
73  An eggshell painted with scenes of misery in disturbing detail
74  A fan that, when unfolded, shows a sleepy cat
75  A set of bone pipes
76  A four-leaf clover pressed inside a book discussing manners and etiquette
77  A sheet of parchment upon which is drawn a mechanical contraption
78  An ornate scabbard that fits no blade you have found
79  An invitation to a party where a murder happened
80  A bronze pentacle with an etching of a rat's head in its center
81  A purple handkerchief embroidered with the name of an archmage
82  Half a floor plan for a temple, a castle, or another structure
83  A bit of folded cloth that, when unfolded, turns into a stylish cap
84  A receipt of deposit at a bank in a far-off city
85  A diary with seven missing pages
86  An empty silver snuffbox bearing the inscription "dreams" on its lid
87  An iron holy symbol devoted to an unknown god
88  A book about a legendary hero's rise and fall, with the last chapter missing
89  A vial of dragon blood
90  An ancient arrow of elven design
91  A needle that never bends
92  An ornate brooch of dwarven design
93  An empty wine bottle bearing a pretty label that says, "The Wizard of Wines Winery, Red Dragon Crush, 331422-W"
94  A mosaic tile with a multicolored, glazed surface
95  A petrified mouse
96  A black pirate flag adorned with a dragon's skull and crossbones
97  A tiny mechanical crab or spider that moves about when it's not being observed
98  A glass jar containing lard with a label that reads, "Griffon Grease"
99  A wooden box with a ceramic bottom that holds a living worm with a head on each end of its body
00  A metal urn containing the ashes of a hero

Name: Truth Serum
Text: A creature exposed to Truth Serum must make a DC 11 Constitution saving throw or become Poisoned for 1 hour. While Poisoned in this way, the creature is unable to knowingly speak a falsehood.

Name: Turquoise
Text:
A pale blue‑green gemstone.

Gemstones are compact, durable, and easier to transport than coins of equal value.

Name: Universal Solvent
Text:
This tube contains a cloudy liquid with a sharp alcoholic scent. When discovered, it holds 1d6 + 1 ounces.

You can take a Utilize action to pour 1 or more ounces onto a surface within reach. Each ounce instantly dissolves up to 1 square foot of adhesive it touches, including Sovereign Glue.

Name: Veteran's Cane
Text: As a Bonus Action, you can transform this walking cane into a mundane Longsword, or revert the Longsword back into a cane. You must be holding the item to change its form.

Name: Vial
Text: A Vial can hold up to 4 ounces of liquid.

Name: Vicious Weapon
Text: This enchanted weapon inflicts an additional 2d6 damage on any creature it strikes. The bonus damage matches the weapon’s normal damage type.

Name: Viol
Text:
Ability: Charisma

Utilize: Perform a familiar melody (DC 10), or improvise a tune (DC 15).

If you are proficient with this tool, add your Proficiency Bonus to any related ability check. If you are also proficient in a relevant skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Vorpal Sword
Text:
You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magical blade. It also ignores Resistance to Slashing damage.

When you attack a creature that has at least one head and roll a natural 20 on the attack roll, you sever one of its heads. The creature dies if it cannot survive without that head. A creature is immune to this effect if it has Immunity to Slashing damage, lacks a head, does not require one, or is deemed too large by the DM for decapitation. Such a creature instead takes 30 extra Slashing damage. A creature with Legendary Resistance may expend one use to avoid decapitation, taking the extra damage instead.

Name: Wagon
Text: A sturdy four-wheeled vehicle designed for transporting goods or passengers.

Name: Walloping Ammunition
Text: A creature struck by this ammunition must succeed on a DC 10 Strength saving throw or fall Prone.

Name: Wand
Text: An Arcane Focus is crafted or adorned to channel arcane power. Sorcerers, Warlocks, and Wizards may use such an item as a Spellcasting Focus.

Name: Wand of Binding
Text:
This wand contains 7 charges.

Spells. While holding the wand, you may cast one of the following spells (save DC 17), expending the listed number of charges:

Spell | Charge Cost
Hold Monster | 5
Hold Person | 2

Regaining Charges. The wand regains 1d6 + 1 charges at dawn. If you expend the final charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand disintegrates into ash.

Name: Wand of Conducting
Text:
This wand contains 3 charges. While holding it, you may take a Magic action to spend 1 charge and produce orchestral music simply by waving it. The sound carries up to 120 feet and stops when you cease moving the wand.

Regaining Charges. The wand regains all expended charges at dawn. If you use the final charge, roll a d20. On a 1, a mournful tuba note plays as the wand disintegrates into dust.

Name: Wand of Enemy Detection
Text:
This wand holds 7 charges. While holding it, you may take a Magic action to expend 1 charge. For 1 minute, you sense the direction—though not the distance—of the nearest creature Hostile to you within 60 feet. The wand detects Hostile creatures even if they are Invisible, ethereal, disguised, hidden, or plainly visible. The effect ends if you stop holding the wand.

Regaining Charges. The wand regains 1d6 + 1 charges at dawn. If you expend the final charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand collapses into ash.

Name: Wand of Fear
Text:
This wand contains 7 charges.

Spells. While holding the wand, you may cast one of the following spells (save DC 15), expending the listed number of charges:

Spell | Charge Cost
Command ("flee" or "grovel" only) | 1
Fear (60‑foot Cone) | 3

Regaining Charges. The wand regains 1d6 + 1 charges at dawn. If you expend the final charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand turns to ash and is destroyed.

Name: Wand of Fireballs
Text:
This wand contains 7 charges. While holding it, you may expend up to 3 charges to cast Fireball (save DC 15). Spending 1 charge casts the spell at 3rd level, and each additional charge raises the spell’s level by 1.

Regaining Charges. The wand restores 1d6 + 1 charges at dawn. If you use the final charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand turns to ash and is destroyed.

Name: Wand of Lightning Bolts
Text:
This wand holds 7 charges. While holding it, you may expend up to 3 charges to cast Lightning Bolt (save DC 15). Spending 1 charge casts the spell at 3rd level, and each additional charge increases the spell’s level by 1.

Regaining Charges. The wand regains 1d6 + 1 charges at dawn. If the final charge is used, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand disintegrates into ash.

Name: Wand of Magic Detection
Text: This wand carries 3 charges. While holding it, you may expend 1 charge to cast Detect Magic. The wand regains 1d3 charges each dawn.

Name: Wand of Magic Missiles
Text:
This wand contains 7 charges. While holding it, you may expend up to 3 charges to cast Magic Missile. Spending 1 charge casts the spell at 1st level, and each additional charge increases the spell’s level by 1.

Regaining Charges. The wand regains 1d6 + 1 charges at dawn. If the final charge is used, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand crumbles into ash.

Name: Wand of Orkus
Text:
Forged and once wielded by Orkus himself, this horrific wand occasionally slips from the demon lord’s grasp, reappearing wherever he senses an opportunity to further his dark ambitions.

The wand is crowned with a skull taken from a human champion slain by Orkus. It can magically adjust its size to suit its wielder’s hand. All Holy Water within 10 feet of the wand is instantly destroyed.

Any creature other than Orkus attempting to attune to the wand must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a success, the creature suffers 10d6 Necrotic damage. On a failure, the creature dies and, if it is a Humanoid, rises as a Zombie.

Magic Weapon. You may wield the wand as a magic Mace that grants a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls. It deals an additional 2d12 Necrotic damage on a hit.

Random Properties. The Wand of Orkus possesses the following random properties:

2 minor beneficial properties
1 major beneficial property
2 minor detrimental properties
1 major detrimental property

The detrimental properties are suppressed while Orkus is attuned to the wand.

Protection. While holding the wand, you gain a +3 bonus to Armor Class.

Spells. The wand has 7 charges and regains 1d4 + 3 charges at dawn. While holding it, you may cast one of the following spells (save DC 18), expending the listed number of charges:

Spell | Charge Cost
Animate Dead | 1
Blight | 2
Circle of Death | 3
Finger of Death | 3
Power Word Kill | 4
Speak with Dead | 1

While attuned to the wand, Orkus or a follower blessed by him may cast these spells using 2 fewer charges (minimum 0).

Call Undead. As a Magic action, you may summon 15 Skeletons and 15 Zombies, which rise from the ground or form in unoccupied spaces within 300 feet. They obey your commands until destroyed or until the next dawn, when they collapse into inert remains. This property can’t be used again until the next dawn.

While Orkus holds the wand, he may summon any type of Undead, and they do not crumble at dawn.

Sentience. The Wand of Orkus is a sentient Chaotic Evil item with Intelligence 16, Wisdom 12, and Charisma 16. It has hearing and Darkvision out to 120 feet.

The wand communicates telepathically with its wielder and speaks Abyssal and Common.

Personality. The wand exists to fulfill Orkus’s desire to extinguish all life. It is cruel, nihilistic, and devoid of humor. To manipulate its wielder, it pretends loyalty and makes extravagant promises it never intends to keep, such as aiding in overthrowing Orkus.

Destroying the Wand. To destroy the Wand of Orkus, it must be brought to the Positive Energy Plane by the ancient hero whose skull adorns it. This requires restoring the long‑lost hero to life—difficult, as Orkus has imprisoned the hero’s soul. Bathing the wand in positive energy causes it to shatter, but unless the required conditions are met, it reforms instantly on Orkus’s layer of the Abyss.

Name: Wand of Paralysis
Text:
This wand contains 7 charges. While holding it, you may take a Magic action to expend 1 charge, firing a thin blue ray at a creature you can see within 60 feet. The target must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or become Paralyzed for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, it repeats the save, ending the effect on a success.

Regaining Charges. The wand regains 1d6 + 1 charges at dawn. If the final charge is used, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand turns to ash.

Name: Wand of Polymorph
Text:
This wand holds 7 charges. While holding it, you may expend 1 charge to cast Polymorph (save DC 15).

Regaining Charges. The wand regains 1d6 + 1 charges at dawn. If the final charge is used, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand disintegrates into ash.

Name: Wand of Pyrotechnics
Text:
This wand contains 7 charges. While holding it, you may take a Magic action to expend 1 charge and create a harmless burst of multicolored light at a point you can see within 120 feet. The flash is accompanied by a crackling sound audible up to 300 feet away. The light is as bright as a torch flame but lasts only a moment.

Regaining Charges. The wand regains 1d6 + 1 charges at dawn. If the final charge is used, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand erupts in a harmless display of sparks and is destroyed.

Name: Wand of Secrets
Text: This wand holds 3 charges and regains 1d3 charges at dawn. While holding it, you may take a Magic action to expend 1 charge. If a secret door or trap lies within 60 feet of you, the wand pulses and points toward the nearest one.

Name: Wand of Web
Text:
This wand contains 7 charges. While holding it, you may expend 1 charge to cast Web (save DC 13).

Regaining Charges. The wand regains 1d6 + 1 charges at dawn. If the final charge is used, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand turns to ash.

Name: Wand of Wonder
Text:
This wand contains 7 charges. While holding it, you may take a Magic action to expend 1 charge and choose a point within 120 feet. A spell or magical effect originates from that point, determined by rolling on the Wand of Wonder Effects table. Spells cast from the wand have a save DC of 15. If a spell normally has a shorter maximum range, its range becomes 120 feet when cast from the wand. If multiple targets are possible, the DM determines randomly which are affected.

Regaining Charges. The wand regains 1d6 + 1 charges at dawn. If the final charge is used, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand crumbles into dust.

Wand of Wonder Effects
1d100  Effect
01–20  You cast a spell from the chosen point. Roll 1d10: 1–2 Darkness; 3–4 Faerie Fire; 5–6 Fireball; 7–8 Slow; 9–10 Stinking Cloud.
21–25  Nothing happens at the chosen point. Instead, you are Stunned until the start of your next turn, convinced something spectacular occurred.
26–30  You cast Gust of Wind. The Line extends from you to the chosen point.
31–35  Nothing happens at the chosen point. Instead, you take 1d6 Psychic damage.
36–40  Heavy rain falls for 1 minute in a 120‑foot‑high, 60‑foot‑radius Cylinder centered on the chosen point. The area becomes Lightly Obscured.
41–45  A cloud of 600 oversized butterflies fills a 60‑foot‑high, 30‑foot‑radius Cylinder centered on the chosen point. They remain for 10 minutes, Heavily Obscuring the area.
46–50  You cast Lightning Bolt. The Line extends from you to the chosen point.
51–55  The nearest creature to the chosen point is enlarged as if by Enlarge/Reduce. If it cannot be affected and is not you, you become the target instead.
56–60  A magically created creature appears in an unoccupied space near the chosen point. It is uncontrolled and vanishes after 1 hour or at 0 HP. Roll 1d4: 1 Rhinoceros; 2 Elephant; 3–4 Rat.
61–64  Grass covers a 60‑foot‑radius circle centered as close to the chosen point as possible. Existing grass grows to ten times its size for 1 minute.
65–68  An unattended object of the DM’s choice within 120 feet vanishes into the Ethereal Plane. If no valid object exists, nothing happens.
69–72  Nothing happens at the chosen point. Instead, you shrink as if by Enlarge/Reduce for 1 minute.
73–77  Leaves sprout from the creature nearest the chosen point. They wither and fall after 24 hours unless removed.
78–82  Nothing happens at the chosen point. Instead, a burst of shimmering light radiates from you in a 30‑foot Emanation. Each creature must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution save or be Blinded for 1 minute, repeating the save at the end of each turn.
83–87  Nothing happens at the chosen point. Instead, you cast Invisibility on yourself.
88–92  Nothing happens at the chosen point. Instead, 1d4 × 10 gems worth 1 GP each shoot from the wand in a 30‑foot‑long, 5‑foot‑wide Line. Each gem deals 1 Bludgeoning damage, divided evenly among creatures in the Line.
93–97  You cast Polymorph on the nearest creature. Roll 1d4: 1 Black Bear; 2 Giant Wasp; 3–4 Frog.
98–00  The nearest creature must make a DC 15 Constitution save. On a failure, it becomes Restrained and begins turning to stone. It repeats the save at the end of its next turn: success ends the effect; failure causes full Petrification until cured by Greater Restoration or similar magic.

Name: Wand of the War Mage
Text:
Multiple versions of this wand exist:

+1 Wand of the War Mage
+2 Wand of the War Mage
+3 Wand of the War Mage

Each version enhances spell attacks and allows the wielder to ignore half cover when making spell attacks.

Name: War Pick
Text:
Versatile. This weapon can be wielded with one or two hands. The damage shown in parentheses applies when used with two hands to make a melee attack.

Mastery: Sap. When you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before your next turn begins.

Name: Warhammer
Text:
Versatile. This weapon may be used with one or two hands. The damage listed in parentheses applies when wielded with two hands.

Mastery: Push. When you hit a Large or smaller creature with this weapon, you can push it up to 10 feet directly away from you.

Name: Warhorse
Text: A powerful mount trained for battle, capable of carrying heavy loads and moving swiftly across the battlefield.

Name: Warship
Damage:
Speed: 2½ mph
Carrying Capacity: 200 tons cargo, 60 passengers
Crew 60, AC 15, HP 500, Damage Threshold 20
Text:
Speed. A ship sailing against a strong wind moves at half speed. In a dead calm, sailing ships cannot move and must be rowed. Keelboats and Rowboats are suited for rivers and lakes. When traveling downstream, add the current’s speed (typically 3 mph). These vessels cannot be rowed against a strong current but may be pulled upstream by animals on shore.

Crew. Ships larger than Keelboats or Rowboats require skilled hirelings to operate. The minimum crew size varies by ship type.

Passengers. The listed capacity assumes hammock accommodations for Small or Medium passengers. Private cabins reduce capacity to one‑fifth. A hammock typically costs 5 SP per day, while a private cabin costs around 2 GP per day.

Damage Threshold. See the rules glossary.

Ship Repair. Repairs can be made while docked. Restoring 1 Hit Point requires 1 day and 20 GP in materials and labor. In well‑equipped shipyards, both time and cost are halved.

Name: Waterskin
Text: A Waterskin can hold up to 4 pints of liquid. Adequate hydration is essential to avoid the effects of dehydration.

Name: Wave
Text:
Kept within the depths of White Plume Mountain, Wave is adorned with carvings of tides, shells, and aquatic life.

You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magical weapon. When you roll a natural 20 on an attack roll with it, the target suffers an additional 21 Necrotic damage.

While holding Wave, you gain the following benefits:

Combat Ready. You have Advantage on Initiative rolls.

Underwater Adaptation. When submerged, a sphere of air surrounds your head, allowing you to breathe normally.

Aquatic Command. Wave holds 3 charges and regains 1d3 charges at dawn. While carrying it, you may expend 1 charge to cast Dominate Beast (save DC 20), but only on a Beast with a Swim Speed.

Globe of Invulnerability. While holding Wave, you may cast the 9th‑level version of Globe of Invulnerability. This property cannot be used again until the next dawn.

Sentience. Wave is a sentient Neutral weapon with Intelligence 14, Wisdom 10, and Charisma 18. It has hearing and Darkvision out to 120 feet.

The weapon communicates telepathically with its wielder and speaks Aquan.

Personality. Wave fervently promotes devotion to sea deities and often hums ocean shanties. It becomes displeased if its wielder fails to advance its maritime goals.

Destroying Wave. Wave can be destroyed only on Thunderforge Island, where it was created. It must be melted down by a storm giant or someone empowered with storm giant strength. Doing so enrages a sea god, who dispatches mighty agents to punish those responsible.

Thrown. A weapon with the Thrown property can be hurled to make a ranged attack, and drawing it is part of that attack. If it is a melee weapon, use the same ability modifier for attack and damage rolls as you would for melee attacks.

Versatile. This weapon may be used with one or two hands. The damage in parentheses applies when used with two hands.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must make a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + the ability modifier used for the attack + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature becomes Prone.

Name: Weapon of Warning
Text:
While this weapon is within your reach and you are attuned to it, you and allies within 30 feet gain the following benefits:

Alarm. The weapon magically awakens each affected creature who is sleeping naturally when combat begins. This does not wake creatures under magical sleep.

Supernatural Readiness. Each affected creature has Advantage on Initiative rolls.

Name: Weaver's Tools
Text:
Ability: Dexterity

Utilize: Repair a tear in fabric (DC 10), or stitch a Tiny decorative pattern (DC 10).

Craft: Padded Armor, Basket, Bedroll, Blanket, Fine Clothes, Net, Robe, Rope, Sack, String, Tent, Traveler's Clothes.

Artisan's Tools are specialized sets used for crafting and trade work. Each type requires its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with these tools, add your Proficiency Bonus to any related ability check. If you are also proficient in a relevant skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Well of Many Worlds
Text:
This elegant black cloth, smooth as silk, folds down to the size of a handkerchief but expands into a 6‑foot‑diameter circular sheet.

You may take a Magic action to unfold the Well of Many Worlds and place it on a solid surface, where it becomes a two‑way portal—6 feet across—to another plane or world. Each time it opens, the DM determines the destination. The portal stays open until a creature within 5 feet uses a Magic action to grasp the cloth’s edges and fold it closed.

After creating a portal, the Well cannot open another for 1d8 hours.

Name: Well-made tapestry that is 10 feet by 10 feet
Text: A finely crafted tapestry of impressive size, often depicting historical scenes, noble lineages, or mythic events. Art objects of this kind may include gilded idols, gemstone‑set jewelry, royal portraits, or ornate ceremonial pieces.

Name: Wheat
Text: A staple trade commodity commonly exchanged by merchants without the need for coinage.

Name: Whelm
Attunement: Requires Attunement By a Dwarf or a Creature Attuned to a Belt of Dwarvenkind
Text:
Whelm is a mighty dwarven-forged weapon long lost within the depths of White Plume Mountain.

You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magical weapon.

Hurl. Whelm has the Thrown property with a normal range of 60 feet and a long range of 180 feet. When you hit with a ranged attack using Whelm, the target takes an extra 1d8 Force damage, or 4d8 Force damage if the target is a Construct, Elemental, or Giant. After the attack—hit or miss—the weapon immediately returns to your hand.

Shock Wave. As a Magic action, you can strike the ground with Whelm, releasing a shock wave. Each creature of your choice on the ground within 60 feet must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw or be Stunned for 1 minute. A creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. This property can’t be used again until the next dawn.

Supernatural Awareness. While holding Whelm, you sense the location of any hidden or secret doors within 30 feet. You may also cast Detect Evil and Good or Locate Object from the weapon. Once you cast either spell, you cannot cast it again from the weapon until the next dawn.

Sentience. Whelm is a sentient Lawful Neutral weapon with Intelligence 15, Wisdom 12, and Charisma 15. It has hearing and Darkvision out to 120 feet.

The weapon communicates telepathically with its wielder and speaks Dwarvish, Giant, and Goblin.

Personality. Whelm is tied to the dwarf clan that forged it—known as the Dankil or Mightyhammer clan—and yearns to be returned to them. Its purpose is to defend dwarves, and conflict arises if its wielder does not share this mission.

Destroying Whelm. Whelm can be dissolved in the acidic bile of a freshly slain ancient black dragon. It may also be melted down in the forges of the Mightyhammer clan, but only by that clan’s rightful leader.

Thrown. A weapon with the Thrown property can be hurled to make a ranged attack, and drawing it is part of the attack. If it is a melee weapon, use the same ability modifier for attack and damage rolls as you would for melee attacks.

Versatile. This weapon can be used with one or two hands. The damage in parentheses applies when used with two hands.

Mastery: Push. When you hit a Large or smaller creature with this weapon, you may push it up to 10 feet directly away from you.

Name: Whip
Text:
Finesse. When attacking with a Finesse weapon, you may use either Strength or Dexterity for attack and damage rolls, but must use the same modifier for both.

Reach. This weapon increases your reach by 5 feet for attacks and Opportunity Attacks.

Mastery: Slow. When you hit a creature and deal damage, you may reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. Multiple hits with Slow weapons do not stack beyond a 10‑foot reduction.

Name: White Dragon Scale Mail
Text:
Dragon Scale Mail is crafted from the scales of a specific type of dragon. Some dragons gift their shed scales, while others are harvested from slain dragons. Either way, such armor is prized.

While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC, have Advantage on saving throws against Dragon breath weapons, and gain Resistance to Cold damage.

You may also take a Magic action to sense the distance and direction to the nearest white dragon within 30 miles. This ability cannot be used again until the next dawn.

The wearer has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Name: Wind Fan
Text: While holding this fan, you can cast Gust of Wind (save DC 13) from it. Each additional use before the next dawn increases the chance of failure by 20 percent; if the fan fails, it rips apart into nonmagical scraps.

Name: Winged Boots
Text: These boots contain 4 charges and regain 1d4 charges at dawn. While wearing them, you can take a Magic action to expend 1 charge and gain a Fly Speed of 30 feet for 1 hour. If you are airborne when the effect ends, you descend at 30 feet per round until you reach the ground.

Name: Wings of Flying
Text: While wearing this cloak, you can take a Magic action to transform it into wings sprouting from your back. The wings last for 1 hour or until you dismiss them early with another Magic action. They grant you a Fly Speed of 60 feet. If you are in the air when the wings vanish, you fall. Once the wings disappear, they cannot be used again for 1d12 hours.

Name: Woodcarver's Tools
Text:
Ability: Dexterity

Utilize: Carve a wooden pattern (DC 10)

Craft: Club, Greatclub, Quarterstaff, most Ranged weapons (excluding Pistol, Musket, and Sling), Arcane Focus, Arrows, Bolts, Druidic Focus, Ink Pen, Needles.

Artisan's Tools are specialized sets used for crafting and trade work. Each type requires its own proficiency.

If you are proficient with these tools, add your Proficiency Bonus to any related ability check. If you are also proficient in a relevant skill, you gain Advantage on that check.

Name: Wooden Staff
Text:
Versatile. This weapon may be wielded with one or two hands. The damage shown in parentheses applies when used with two hands for a melee attack.

A Druidic Focus is crafted in a particular shape and decorated—carved, wrapped, or painted—to channel primal magic. Druids and Rangers can use such an item as a Spellcasting Focus.

Mastery: Topple. When you hit a creature with this weapon, it must make a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + the ability modifier used for the attack + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature gains the Prone condition.

Name: Wraps of Unarmed Power
Text:
Multiple versions of this item exist, each enhancing unarmed strikes:

+1 Wraps of Unarmed Power
+2 Wraps of Unarmed Power
+3 Wraps of Unarmed Power

Name: Wyvern Poison
Text: A creature exposed to Wyvern Poison must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw, taking 24 (7d6) Poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Name: Yellow Sapphire
Text:
A vivid yellow or yellow‑green gemstone.

Gemstones are compact, durable, and far easier to transport than coins of equal worth.

Name: Yew Wand
Text: A Druidic Focus is shaped and decorated—carved, wrapped, or painted—to channel primal magic. Druids and Rangers can use such an item as a Spellcasting Focus.

Name: Zircon
Text:
A pale blue‑green gemstone.

Gemstones are small, easy to carry, and simpler to secure than an equivalent value in coinage.
